CHAPTER 1218 .
AN ACT tofurtheramend thecharterof thecityof Covington in re-
specttosupplyingwater forthecitizensof saidcity,and others.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the trusteesof the Covington reservoirbe,
and arehereby,authorizedto issueand sellthebonds
of the cityof Covingtonin a sum notexceedingone
hundred and twenty thousand dollars, in addition to
the bonds authorized by act amending saidcitychar-
ter, approved the firstday of May , one thousand
eighthundred and eighty-six,and amendment there-
to,approvedthefifteenthday of February,one thou-
1- LOCAL LAWS , VOL . 3.

2 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
thousand eighthundred and eighty-eight,the proceeds
thereofto be appliedby them to the completion of the
reservoirs, pumping -house, machinery and pipe con-
nectionsnow under constructionby them for supply-
ing the inhabitantsof saidcitywith water, under the
authorityof said act,approved the firstday of May ,
one thousand eight hundred and eighty-six,and the
said amendment thereto . Said bonds shall be issued
and sold under the same terms and conditions pre-
scribed in said act and amendment for the issuance
and saleof the bonds therein authorized,except that
saidbonds shallnot be sold forlessthan par,with the
accrued unmatured interest .
§ 2. That when said trusteesshall have completed
said reservoirs, pumping -house, machinery and pipe
connections,as in said act of May first, one thousand
eight hundred and eighty-six provided, there re-
mains any of saidbonds unsold,or any money pro-
vided for said construction,derived from any source,
unexpended by them therein, the same shallbe dis-
posed of as directed in the twenty -ninth sectionof
saidact of May first,one thousand eighthundred and
eighty-six.
§3 . That the agreement made by saidtrusteeswith
the United StatesGovernment to supply the military
post, recentlyestablishednear said reservoirs, with
water,shall be reduced to writing, and executed by
saidtrustees, and the same shallbe legaland binding
in likemanner as if the said trusteeshad been legally
empowered in the premises at the time said agree-
ment was made .
§ 4. This actshallbe in forcefrom itspassage.
HARVEY MYERS ,
Speaker of the House of Representatives.
JAMES W. BRYAN ,
Speaker of the Senate .
Approved May 3,1890.

CHAPTER 1219 .
AN ACT to amend an act, entitled"An act to amend and reduce into
one the severalacts relatingto the charter and amendments thereto
of the town of Winchester ," and to authorizethe board of councilmen
of saidtown tocontractfora water supplyforsaidtown.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That subsection fifteenth of section six of said
act be, and it is hereby, so amended as to authorize
the board of councilmen of said town to contract with
any person, company or corporation to supply said
town with water forallpurposes,and to incur a debt
or liabilityto pay for same , as provided in saidsub-
section; and to levy and collecta tax on the citizens
and property of said town sufficientto dischargesaid
indebtednessor liabilityas taxes are now leviedand
collected in said town .
§ 2. That the actionof saidboard in submitting the
question as to whether it shall be authorized to con .
tractfor a water supply forsaidtown to the votersof
said town , at an election to be held on the twenty-
sixth day of April, one thousand eight hundred and
ninety, or at such times as shallhereafterbe fixedby
said board , as provided in said subsection fifteenth,
and the electionheld in pursuance thereof, shall be
valid and binding to the same extent as if ithad been .
ordered after this act takes effectand in pursuance
and by virtue of its authority .
§ 3. This act shall take effect from and after its
passage .
Approved May 3,1890.

CHAPTER 1221 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Workingmen's Buildingand Loan Asso-
ciation .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That W. S. Miller, W. S. Downton , J. Roe
Young , W. W. Wiseman , L. R. Wharton , Charles
G. Payne , W. J. Thornton , J. F. Sluser, Jacob Cor-
bett, W. S. Emmal , B. C. Corbett, W. C. Bascom and
G. W. Easton,theirassociates,successorsand assigns,
are hereby created a corporationunder the name and
styleof the " Workingmen's Building and Loan Asso-
ciation," with power in that name to contractand be
contractedwith,sue and be sued ; may have perpetual
succession; may have a common seal, and alter the
same atpleasure; and may purchase,use,lease,hold
and sellreal and personalestate,loan itsfunds, and
take mortgages and othersecuritiestherefor, and may
borrow money by hypothecation of itssecurities.
§ 2. The principalplace for the transactionof its
businessshallbe in the cityof Middlesborough ,Ken-
tucky, and shall, within one year afterthe approval
of thisact, organizein good faithfor the transaction
of the business of the association .
§ 3. The general nature of itsbusiness shall be to
assistitsmembers in saving and investingmoney ,and
inbuying and improving realestate,and forprocuring
money forother purposes by loaningto such of them
as may desire it; and in default of applicationby
members to other persons, natural or artificial, the
funds accumulated from the weekly contributionof
itsstockholders , and also such other funds as the di-
rectorsmay obtain by hypothecating itssecurities.
§ 4. The amount of capitalstock of the association
may be an amount not exceeding fivehundred thou-
sand dollars, divided into shares of one hundred dol-
larseach,which shallbe paid in as shallbe prescribed
by the by -laws ; and the by -laws may make the shares

i
LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
or interestof the stockholderstransferable,and fixthe
mode of making such transfer.
§ 5. The associationshallhave power to establish
by -laws,and make any rulesand regulationsdesiredor
expedient forthe management of itsaffairs,not incon-
sistent with the Constitution or laws of Kentucky or
the United States. The by -laws shallbe adopted by
the stockholders,and shallnot be amended except at
an annual meeting or a calledmeeting for thepurpose ,
and by a majority vote of the stock represented at
such meeting .
§ 6. The government and affairsof the association
shallbe vested in and conducted by a board of nine
directors, a majority of whom shall constitute a
guorum to transact business, who shall be elected
annually as the by -laws may prescribe. At such elec-
tion each stockholder shall be entitled to have one
vote for each share of stock owned by him . The di-
rectors thus elected shall annually , as soon after their
electionas convenient, elect a president, vice-presi-
dent, secretaryand treasurer,who shall perform the
duties assigned them by the by -laws,and shall hold
officeuntil their successors are elected and qualified.
The firstboard of directors shall consist of the above-
named incorporatorsuntilthe firstannual electionbe
provided for by the by -laws. The board of directors
may appoint such agents and employes as they may
deem fitfor the best interest of the association in ac-
cordance with the provisionsof the by -laws.
§ 7. The highest amount of indebtednessor liabil-
ity to which thisassociationis at any time to subject
itselfisone-half of the sum paid in for stock.
§ 8. The privateproperty of the members of the as-
sociationis exempt from liabilityfor the corporate
debts .
§ 9. Provided , That the foregoingcorporatorsshall
not commence businessunder thischarteruntiltwenty
thousand dollarshave actuallybeen paid in cash, ver-
ifiedby the oath of the treasurer.
§ 10. This act shall take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved May 3, 1890 .

10 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 1222 .
AN ACT to allow the citizensof thisCommonwealth to meet , organize,
hold lodgemeetings or religiousservicesinthedistrictschool-houses
of this State .
Be it enacted by theGeneral Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the citizens of this Commonwealth shall
have the right to occupy and use any districtschool-
house within thisStatefor organizing,using and hold-
ing lodgesorreligiousservices,when itdoes not conflict
with any schoolthat isor may be in progressin said
building: Provided , That allassembliesthat may oc-
cupy said districtschool-house shall be responsible
to the trustees of said school-house for any damage
done to the building, furniture or apparatus while
occupying said building.
§ 2. That this act shall not be construed to mean
that white citizenscan occupy and use a district
school -house that is intended for colored children ; nor
can colored citizensoccupy and use a districtschool-
house that is intended for white children .
§ 3. This actshallonly apply to Marshall and Lyon
counties .
§ 4. This act shall take effectfrom and after its pas-
sage .
Approved May 3,1890.

CHAPTER 1225 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof John G. Reynolds,of Green county,Ken-
tucky .
WHEREAS , John G. Reynolds , of Green county ,
Kentucky , prior to the passage of the law requiring
common school teachers in this Commonwealth to be
examined in civilgovernment ,obtainedfrom the board
of examiners for Green county a certificateof quali-
ficationas a common school teacher for said county
of Green , which , by itsterms, will not expire until
the thirtiethof June , one thousand eight hundred and
ninety ; and whereas,without undergoing an examina-
tion in civilgovernment ,as requiredby law, approved
since the grant of said certificateto said Reynolds ,
saidReynolds ,by oversightin the presentschoolyear,
contracted for teaching the common school district
number thirty-two in Green county,and did,in fact,
teach four months of said common school in said dis-
trict before discovering his mistake ; and whereas ,
said Reynolds did forthwith, upon discoveringhis
mistake , pass the examination in civilgovernment
required by law successfully; now , therefore, in view
of the foregoingpremises,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county superintendent of common

12 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
schools in and for Green county ,Kentucky , be,and
he ishereby, authorized,directedand required to pay
over to said John G. Reynolds that part of the school
money due common schooldistrictnumber thirty-two ,
in Green county,for the year one thousand eighthun-
dred and eighty-nine-ninety, that under his contract
with the trustees of said school district is due said
John G. Reynolds for said four months of school
taught : Provided , The trusteesof said districtshall,
beforesaidmoney ispaid,make a reportto the county
superintendentthat saidschoolwas actuallytaught
for saidtime by saidReynolds , and that he was duly
qualified,exceptas tocivilgovernment.
§ 2. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved May 3,1890.
CHAPTER 1226 .

freetothepublic.

monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the court of claims of Harrison county ,
Courtappointa majority of the justicesof the peace of the countythree commis-
sioners .
being present and concurring therein, may appoint
three intelligentand discreetresidenttax-payers of
saidcounty as commissioners to ascertainand report
to the county court the probable cost of securing of
publicuse the stock held by individualsand corpora-
tions, other than said county, in all the turnpike
roads in said county , and of releasingthe corpora-
tions owning and operating said roads from their
debts and liabilities,and of putting and keeping said
turnpikes in good travelingorder at the expense of
the tax -payers of said county .
§2 . The commissioners so appointed, after being

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 13
duly sworn to faithfullydischargetheirduties assworn.
such ,shallascertainand reportto the county court-
First . The number of miles of turnpike road in
said county owned and operatedby each turnpike
company ,personor associationof persons, with the
number and par value of the sharesof stock or inter-
ests therein held by said county, and by each other
person or corporation .
Second . The average annual grossearnings and cost
of operating and maintaining each of said turnpikes,
includingthe interestupon the debts and liabilitiesof
the corporation owning and operating the same , and
the average annual net income thereof within five
years next before the appointment of said commis-
sioners, and the probable net earnings for the next
five thereafter .
Third . The resources, and all the debts and liabili-
ties of each of said turnpike companies, including
whatever may be due the county for dividends on its
-stock ,with the annual interestthereon ,and the names
of the persons owning and holding each of said debts
and liabilitiesand the sureties thereon , and whether
or not the county has any indemnity against said
-debts and liabilities.
Fourth . The value of each toll-house and toll-house
grounds owned by each of said turnpike companies .
Fifth . The cash value of the shares of stock or
interestof each individualor corporationother than
said county in each of said turnpikes, taking into
-considerationthe decrease in value by reason of the
debts and liabilitiesof the company ; and where any
of them extends into another county under the same
management ,the value of the part lying in Harrison
county as compared with the value of the entireroad.
Sixth . The cash value of each of the debts and lia-
bilitiesof each of said turnpike companies , based
upon the capacity of its road to earn more than
enough to pay the expense of operation and keeping
itin proper repair, and the proportionof such excess

thatmay be used in payment of saiddebts and liabil-
itiesunder the conditionsof said county's subscrip-
tion to the capital stock of the company .
Seventh. The lowest price at which said court of
claims forsaidcounty may be able to obtain, by dona-
tion or purchase forpublic use,any of said stock or
interestsheld by persons other than said county, and
the lowest price at which any of the holders of said
debts and liabilities, and the suretiesthereon, will

CHAPTER 1227 .
AN ACT concerningthe Bourbon County AgriculturalAssociation.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the Bourbon County AgriculturalAssoci-
ation is hereby granted the privilegeof selling, or
having sold, pools on horse-races during its annual
meeting, on the grounds of the association, and at
one place in the city of Paris not on said grounds .
Said pool-sellingshall be under the exclusive con-
trolof the Bourbon County AgriculturalAssociation.
§ 2. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved May 3, 1890.
CHAPTER 1228 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Kentucky CapitalizationCompany .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That Thomas A. Harris, William G. Welch and
David W. Sanders, their associates, successors and

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 27
assigns,be,and are hereby,createda body -corporate,
under the corporatename of the " Kentucky Capitali-
zation Company ;" and under that name may sue and
be sued, plead and be impleaded ,and have and adopt
a corporate seal, and generallyto do and perform in
their corporate name such acts and deeds as may be
necessary and proper in the conducting and perform-
ance of their business as hereinafter set forth .
§ 2. The objectsof the foregoing company is the
purchase , promoting and utilizingof patent rightsto
valuable inventionsby establishingmanufactories, in-
troducing the products thereof; disposing of terri-
torialrightsand privileges, and generally to act as
principal or agents in the transactionof business of
that nature .
§ 3. Books of subscriptionfor stock of said com-
pany may be opened and closed at the discretionof
said corporators; but all stock subscribed shall be
paid for at fullpar value at the time of subscription.
§ 4. The capitalstock of saidcompany shallnot ex-
ceed fivemillion dollars, and shall be divided into
shares of one hundred dollarseach. Privateproperty
shallnot be liableforcorporatedebts.
§ 5. Said company shallhave power , if deemed ex-
pedient, to organize its business into distinctivede-
partments and bureaus, keeping separate books of
account for each,and ifdeemed expedient,declaring
and payingdistinctivedividendstosuch departments
according to theirrespectivenet earnings; but the ag-
gregate capital stock of all departments shall not
exceed the capitalstock of the parent company .
§ 6. Said company shallhave power to purchase for
cash, or forcash and stock,or for stock alone, patent
rights and realestatenecessaryfor the conducting of
itsbusiness,and tosellthewhole,or any partthereof,
as it may deem expedient; but all stock issued in
payment for patent rightsor realestateshallbe
deemed and held as fullpaid and unassessable.
§7. Said company shallhave power to make all

28 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
needful rules and regulationsfor the conducting of
itsbusinessand the management of itsaffairs,not in-
consistent with the laws of this Commonwealth .
§ 8. The principalplace of business of said com-
pany shallbe in the cityof Louisville,Kentucky ; but
said company may establish agencies beyond and
without this Commonwealth .
§ 9. This act shall be in force and take effect from
and afteritspassage.
Approved May 8,1890.
CHAPTER 1229 .

WHEREAS , Robert Caldwell, by deed , dated June
fifteenth, one thousand eighthundred and sixteen,re-
corded in the Madison County Court,conveyed a piece
of land of about one and one -half acres to trustees of
the Madison Academy and theirsuccessors forever,
for educationalpurposes ; and whereas, the grantees
in saiddeed and their successorsdid erect buildings
thereon and keep up an academy for the teaching of
children, and the same lotof ground and buildingsso
erectedthereon have been for many years used as a
districtschool by trusteeselected under the general
school laws of Kentucky ; and whereas , under the
Caldwell deed , authoritywas granted the citizensof
Richmond to erect,if they chose so to do , a house for
religiousworship on one part of saidland, and au-
thority was granted to Richmond Lodge , number
twenty -five,of Free Masons ,to erect thereon a lodge
building , but neither said authorized church or lodge
was ever, in fact, so erectedon said land ; and where-
as,itisproposed to have erectedon saidlotof ground
a new and costlybuilding for common school pur-
poses,and for the perfectionof the titleto same in

the trustees of the district schools and their successors
forever .

property was given to the citizensunder the Caldwell
deed for church uses to the district school for the
purposes aforesaid.

behalf of saidlodge of Masons ,whatever rightor priv-
ilege in said property was given to said lodge forMa-
sonicpurposesby the Caldwelldeed to the trustees

CHAPTER 1231 .
AN ACT to authorizethe countycourtof Fayettecountyto obtain,
purchase or leaseturnpikeroadsin saidcounty,and maintain them
freeof tollfrom thetravelingpublic.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. For the purpose of enabling the county of
Fayette to obtain turnpikes situatedin said county ,
3- LOCAL LAWS , VOL . 3 .

34 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the saidcounty,through and by itscounty court and
court of claims and levy,may , and it is hereby au-
thorized to, accept constructed turnpikes from the
stockholdersor owners thereof,and keep said roads
in repairby working the county convictsupon them ,
and by freelabor,to be paid forby an annual tax, as
part of the county levy; and such roads,when thus
obtained, shall be free of toll from the traveling
public.
§2 . The said Fayette County Court, for the same
purpose, may acquire such roads by purchase from
the stockholdersor owners thereof; and when thus
obtained, they shallbe kept up in the same way , and
to pay forthem the county court may make a county
levy tax.
§ 3. The said county court is hereby authorized to
leaseany turnpike road, or part thereof, situated in
said county,on such terms and conditions, and for
such length of time, as may be agreed on ; and for
this purpose itshallbe lawful for the owners of a ma-
jorityof the stock in any such turnpike to make a
validleaseof the same . In making any such contract,
an agentappointedby theFayetteCounty Courtshall
representand vote the stockof Fayette county insuch
turnpike. Said turnpikeshallbe kept up in the same
way ;and to pay forthem the county court may make
a county levy tax : Provided , That the Fayette
County Court, composed of the county judge and
justices, for the purpose of carrying into effectthe
provisionsof this act, and to defray the expenses
of the purchase or leaseof said turnpike roads, and
to maintain the same ,are hereby authorizedand em-
powered to levy and collecta tax on the taxable prop-
ertyin said county not exceeding twenty centson the
one hundred dollarsof taxable property in any one
year. Said tax shallbe collectedin the same manner ,
and under the same conditions, as the tax in aid of
county levy; and the said county court, composed
as aforesaid,ishereby authorizedand empowered ,for

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 35
the payment of the price of any turnpike road pur-
chased , to issue and sell a sufficientnumber of the
bonds of said county,in denominations of one thou-
sand dollarseach, payable at such times, and with
interestat such rates, not exceeding six per cent. per
annum ,asmay be determined upon by saidcourt;and
the said courtshallprovide forthe payment of the in-
terestand principalof saidbonds by a tax upon the
taxable property of saidcounty ,to be collectedas the
tax hereinbeforeprovided for.
§ 4. The titleto such turnpike roads may be trans-
ferredby a surrenderof allthe stock in such turnpike
road company to the county , or by deed of convey-
ance made to said county .
§ 5. The courtof claims of Fayette county shall, in
sixty days afterthe ratificationof thisact,as herein-
afterprovided,and annuallythereafter,electa super-
visorof such free roads. Said supervisor isempow-
ered to appoint such agents and assistantsas said
county court may think necessaryand proper. Said
supervisor shall,under the directionof said county
court, have saidturnpikes worked by the county con-
victs, free labor employed by him , or by contract.
The duties and pay of such supervisor,and hisagents,
or assistants,shallbe fixedby regulationof saidcoun-
ty court .
§ 6. This act shall be submitted to the approval of
the votersof Fayettecountyat the August election,
one thousandeighthundred and ninety. The judges
of said electionshall propound to each voter the
question," Are you for freeturnpikes?" or " Are you
against free turnpikes?" The vote thus taken shall
be certifiedby the officersof the electionto the county
judge of Fayette county, and if a majority of the
votes cast at said election be in favor of free turn-
pikes,then the county court shall at once be author-
ized and empowered to carry intoeffectthe provisions
of this act .
§ 7. This actshalltake effectfrom and afteritspas.
sage . Approved May 3,1890 .

sand eight hundred and ninety, be, and is hereby ,

tween the word " payable," in the second lineof said
section,and the word " and," in the thirdlineof said
section, and inserting in lieu thereof the word " semi-
annually ."
§ 2. That sectioneightof saidact be,and ishereby ,

" The taxes levied under the provisions of this act
shallbe due and payable tendays aftersuch levy,and

unpaid ."
§ 3. That sectionseven of saidactishereby amended
by strikingout the words , " his commission for col-
lectingsaid taxes shallbe fixed and allowed by the
saidcourtofclaims,but shallnotexceedfivepercent.
on the amount collected," and insertinginlieuthereof
the following words , to wit : " His commissions for

have power , and itshallbe theirduty , to fillthe va-
cancy ; and the person so appointed shallhave allthe
power of a member -elect,and shallhold untilthe next
regularelection, and untilhis successoriselectedand
qualified.
ARTICLE III .
§1 . There shallbe elected,by the qualifiedvotersAssessorelected.
of said town , at the same time that councilmen are
elected, a town assessor,who shallhold his officefor
one year, and untilhis successoriselectedand quali-
fied. No person shall be eligibleto said officewho
has not arrived at the age of twenty -four years, and
who has not resided in said town for six months before
his election. Before proceedingtoact,saidofficershall
take an oath , before some officerauthorized to admin-
isterit, that he willfaithfullydischarge allthe duties
of the officeof assessoraccording to law, which shall
be in addition to the general oath required by the
Constitution . He shall execute bond to the town of
Madisonville, with good sureties, to be approved by
the board of councilmen ,conditionedfor the faithful
performance of his officialduties, upon which bond
suitmay be institutedby any person aggrieved in any
court of competent jurisdiction, and recoveryhad to
the extent of the injury sustained. If the assessor
shall failto give said bond within ten days afterhis
election, the board shalldeclarethe officevacant, and
fillthe vacancy by appointment of another qualified
person .

76 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 2. It shallbe the duty of the assessor, on the tax-
book prepared for that purpose, to call on the tax-
payers,and, in alphabeticalform,take the names of
allmale citizens,allqualifiedvoters,pollsand persons
residingin the town limits,also each separate article
of property,real, personal or mixed , subjectto taxа-
tion under this act, with the names of the owners of
saidproperty. Said listor assessment shallbe made
as iftaken the tenth of January in each year,except
merchants, grocers, druggists and person dealing in
goods ,wares and merchandise,who shallbe assessed
as of date April firstin each year. The assessorshall
make out and submit his tax-book and a reportof his
action at regular or called meetings of the board in the
month of May in each year .
§ 3. The assessor shall have power to administer an
oath to each tax-payer at the time of taking his or
her list, and itshallbe the duty of the tax-payers to
liston oath allpropertyand estatesubjectto taxation
under this act. If any person shall failor refuseto
listhis or her property,the assessorshalllistthe same
on the bestevidencehe can procureas to the character
and value of the property,or he may report the same
to the tax supervisors, who shallhave power to make
such listsat the time of theirmeeting. The board of
councilmen may , by ordinance, provide penalties
againstallpersons who failor refuse to listwith the
assessoras requiredby thisact.
§ 4. The clerk of said town shall, at the proper
Dutiesofclerk.time,during each year, make up and deliver to the
assessor.aform of tax-book , which may be corrected
or reformed by the board of councilmen . Said book
shallcontain the appropriate headings and columns
for listingall persons and property subject to taxa-
tion .
§ 5. Any vacancy in the officeof assessormay be
filledby the board of councilmen ; and the person ap-
pointed by said board shallhold and discharge the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 77
duties of said officeuntil the next succeeding election,
and untilhis successoriselectedand qualified.
§ 6. There shallbe elected,by the qualifiedvotersAttorney.
of said town , at the same time that councilmen are
elected,a town attorney, who shallhold his officefor
one year,and untilhis successoriselectedand quali-
fied. No person shallbe eligibleas town attorneywho
has not resided in said town for at least six months
before his election; who isnot twenty -one years of
age, and who has not been a regularpracticingattor-
ney for two years next beforehis election.
§ 7. The town attorneyshallattend and prosecute
all cases of a criminal or penal nature when parties
are triedfor an infractionof the ordinances and by-
laws of the town . He shallperform such other legal
servicesas the board of councilmen may request, or
as may be prescribedby ordinance.
§ 8. The town attorney shall receive, as compensa-
tion for his services, thirtyper cent.on all finesim-
posed and collectedin the police court for an infraction
of the ordinances and by -laws of the town at which
said attorney has attended and prosecuted. He shall
receivesuch other compensation, for specialservices,
as the board of councilmen may provide .
§9 . Any vacancy in the officeof town attorneythat
may be causedby death,resignationor otherwisemay
be filledby the board of councilmen ,and the person
so appointed shall hold and discharge the duties of
said officeuntil the next regular election, and until
his successoriselectedand qualified.
ARTICLE IV .
§ 1. There shallbe electedon the firstMonday inPolicejudge.
August , one thousand eighthundred and ninety, and
every four years thereafter, by the qualifiedvotersof
saidtown,a policejudge,who shallhold hisofficefor
four years, and untilhis successor is qualified. The
policejudge shallhave thequalificationsof a county
judge. He shalltake in substancethesame oathsof

78 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
officeasa countyjudge,and executebond as provided
by the generallaws fora justiceof the peace.
§ 2. The policejudge shall be a conservatorof the
peace,and his court shall be styled the Madisonville
PoliceCourt; saidcourt shall be a court of record ;
the judge thereof may act as clerk, and certified
copiesof the recordsof saidcourt,over the signature
of the judge, shall be evidence in any court of this
Commonwealth .
§ 3. The policecourt shallbe held at such place,
within the limits of said town , as the board of coun-
cilmen may , by ordinance, designate; and should
said board failto provide and designatea place for
holding saidcourt, then the policejudge may selecta
place. The policejudge shallfixstatedterms, not
exceeding four terms per year, for the trialof civil
actions and proceedings, at which process shall be
made returnable . Until the terms of said court are
fixedby saidjudge by order of record, the same shall
be as now establishedby law . Said court may open at
any time for the trialof criminaland penal cases, or
where penalties are fixed.
§ 4. The saidpolicecourt shallhave exclusiveorigi-
nal jurisdictionof allprosecutionsand actions for a
violationof the ordinancesand by -laws of the town ;
and allfinesimposed and collectedfor a violationof
said ordinances and by -laws shall be paid to the
town treasurer .
§ 5. The said policejudge shallhave the same juris-
dictionin feloniesand misdemeanors that justicesof
the peace of Hopkins county now have by law, and
alsosuch other jurisdictionas are conferredupon
policejudgesby theCriminalCode of Practice.
§ 6. The said policecourt shall have originaland
concurrentjurisdictionwith the justicesof the peace
of Hopkins county in allcivilactionsand proceedings
within the town limits .
§7. The mode of procedurein saidpolicecourt,in
criminaland penal actionsand prosecutions,shallbe

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 79-
as provided in the Criminal Code of Practice,and in
civilactionsand proceedingsas providedintheCivil
Code of Practice. The policejudge shallhave power
to issueallprocess,and enforcethe attendanceof wit-
nesses, finefor contempt , and enforcethe judgments
and ordersof saidcourt,as given by law to justicesof
the peace .
§ 8. Any person who has been sentenced to impris-
onment under the judgment of the policecourt shall
be committed to the jailof Hopkins county to remain
during such sentence. Any person againstwhom a
finehas been imposed by the judgment of saidcourt,
and who shallfailto pay or replevythe fineand costs,
shall be committed to the jailof Hopkins county, to
remain in confinement until the fine and all costs shall
be discharged; and the imprisonment shall be at the
rateof two dollarsper day untilthe fineand costs be
satisfied. The policecourt may in itsordersprovide
forcommitting persons,againstwhom judgments have
been rendered,to the work house,to be worked under
the keeper of said work -house during the term of his
or her sentence, or until the fineand costs shall be
paid ; the imprisonment in the work -house shallbe at
the rateof one dollarper day untilthe fineand costs
be discharged .
9. That whenever the policejudge shallbe satis-
fiedthat a publicoffensehas been committed ,he shall
have power to summon beforehim any person he may
think proper for examination on oath concerningsuch
offense, to enable him to ascertainthe offender,and to
issuea warrant for his arrest. He may issue process
and compel the attendanceof witnesses,and may fine
for contempt .
§ 10. That itshallbe lawfulfor the policecourt, in
any judgment for fineor sentenceof imprisonment
rendered in saidcourt,to provide in the judgment for
working such offenderupon the streets,alleysor pub-
licworks of the town during the period of sentenceor
term of imprisoment, in allcaseswhere said offender-

Vacancy in
officeof police
judge.
Fees .
failsto pay or replevy the fineand costs assessed
against him . The board of councilmen may adopt
rulesfor working all prisonerswho have been com-
mitted to the county jailunder a judgment of said
court or a capias pro fine.
§ 11. That the proceedings and process in the said
policecourt,inallactionsand prosecutionsforan in-
fractionof the ordinances and by -laws of the town ,
shall be in the name of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky, for the benefitof the town of Madisonville.
Process, originalor final,may be directedto the town
marshal or to the sheriff, any constable of Hopkins
county,or any policeman. The defendant shallhave
the rightto replevy, for three months , any judgment
in the police court by giving bond , with approved
security, bearing interestfrom date untilpaid.
§ 12. That if the officeof policejudge be vacant or
the judge be absent, or a party to a suit or prosecu-
tionshall make affidavitthat the said judge willnot
give him or her a fairtrial, or for any other reason
the policejudge can not preside,then it shallbe the
duty of the chairman of the board of councilmen to
preside in saidcourt,and he shallhave allthe juris-
dictionand powers conferred by law upon the police
judge. If, forany cause, the chairman of the board
of councilmen can not preside in said court,it shall
be lawful for the board of councilmen to convene ,
and , by order of record, designate a justiceof the
peace of Hopkins county to preside, and he shall
have all the jurisdictionand powers of the police
judge .
§ 13. The fees of the policejudge shallbe same as
are now given or may hereafterbe given by law to
officersperforming like services, and the fees and
costs shall be taxed in said court as in other courts of
similarjurisdiction. The policejudge shallhave the
same power to collecthis fee-billsby distressand by
actionas justicesof the peace and county judges have
by law.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 81
§ 14. Any vacancy in the officeof policejudge,
whether caused by death, resignationor removal,
shall be filledby the board of councilmen ; and the
person appointed by said board shall hold untilthe
next August election,and untilhissuccessorisqual-
ified. The policejudge,whether electedor appointed,
as provided in this act, shall receivehis commission
from the Governor .
ARTICLE V.
§1 . There shallbe electedby the qualifiedvotersofTownmarshal.
said town , on the firstMonday in August ,one thou-
sand eight hundred and ninety, and every two years
thereafter, a town marshal ,who shall hold his office
for the term of two years, and untilhis successoris
qualified. The town marshal shallbe a citizenof said
town and have the qualificationsof a sheriff.
§ 2. That the town marshal,before he proceeds to
act,shalltake the oath of officeand execute an official
bond in substance as prescribedby law for sheriffs.
Said bond shallbe approved by the Hopkins County
Court , and put to record in the clerk'sofficeat the
Hopkins County Court. Any person injured by the
acts or omissions of said marshal may have remedy
and rightof actionon saidbond in any court of com-
petent jurisdiction. The marshal may be subject to
removal from officein like manner as sheriffs.
§ 3. The town marshal shall have and possess all
the powers and jurisdictionof constablesof Hopkins
county ,and shallbe liableforhis actsand officialcon-
duct in likemanner . He shallattend the policecourt
and assistin procuring evidence; he shallexecute all
processto him directedby saidcourt, or any other
courtof competentjurisdiction,and he shallperform
such other ministerialduties as the board of council-
men by ordinancemay direct.
§ 4. The town marshal shall be entitled to the same
fees for services in civil or criminal cases that are al-
6- LOCAL LAWS , VOL . 3 .

82 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
lowed by law to sheriffsand constablesfor similar
services,which shallbe collectablein the same man-
ner. He shallbe liableand may be prosecuted forhis
acts, conduct and omissions of duty as sheriffsand
constables. He may receivesuch other compensation
as the board of councilmen may provide.
§ 5. Any vacancy in the officeof town marshal may
be filledby the board of councilmen,and the person
appointed, on taking the oaths and giving the bond
required by law, shallhold until the next election,
and untilhissuccessorisqualified.
ARTICLE VI .
§ 1. The clerk of the town of Madisonville, within
thirtydays afterthe assessor'stax-book has been re-
visedby the tax supervisors,shallmake out therefrom
a tax-book containing, in alphabeticalform , a true
listof allpersons chargeablewith tax,the number of
levies,and the aggregatevalue of each person's taxa-
ble property, and the amount of tax due on same .
The clerk shall deliver said tax list to the town mar-
shal or collector, taking a receipttherefor, and said'
taxes shall then be due and collectable .
§ 2. The town marshal shall,by virtueof his office,
be collectorof the taxes. He shall,at some meeting
of the board of councilmen , and before the tax -book
is placed in his hands , execute bond to the town
of Madisonville with good surety, to be approved
by the board of councilmen, conditioned that he
shall,during the next twelve months ,collect,account
for and pay to the town treasurer, and other per-
sons entitledthereto, according to law and the ordi-
nances of the town ,all taxes and public dues, also,
allfines,amercements and penaltiesdirectedor author-
ized by law to be collectedor receivedby him . The
suretiesinsaidbond shalljointlybe worth,afterthe
payment of debts and exemptions ,a sum equal to the
aggregate amount of revenue to be collected for the
year .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 83.
§ 3. The town of Madisonvilleshallhave a lien,from
the date of the marshal'sbond ,upon allproperty sub-
ject to execution belonging to the marshal, or after-
wards acquired by him ,which shallremain in force
untilhe obtainsa quietusfor the revenue.
§4. The town marshal shallaccount for and pay to
the treasurerall taxes and public moneys for which
he isbound ,at such times and under such regulations
as the board of councilmen may by ordersprescribe.
§ 5. The town marshal shall,from and after the day
he receivesthe tax-book ,in each year, proceed to col-
lectthe taxesdue the town . On failureof the persons
bound fortaxes to pay the same ,the marshal may dis-
train the personalproperty owned by , or in the right-
ful possession of, the person from whom the tax is
due , notwithstanding the existenceof any lienthere-
on ; and he may sellthe rightof such person in the
property seizedas will be sufficientto pay the tax,
costsand expenses of sale. The salemust be forcash,
and the time, place and terms of sale shallbe adver-
tised by writtenor printednotices,posted at not less
than threepublic placesin the town ,and fornot less
than ten days next before the sale. If the marshal
shall make an illegalseizureor distressfor taxes,he
shallbe liablein damages to the party aggrieved.
§ 6. The town of Madisonville shall have a lien for
all taxes levied upon all real and personalestatein
the town limitsowned by the persons or corporations
from whom taxes are due : Provided , That purchasers
of property from personsbound for taxes shallbe lia-
ble only for the taxes upon the property purchased.
On alltaxes not paid by the firstof December ,in each
year,thereshallbe added a penaltyof fiveper cent.
on the amount ,to be accounted for as other taxes .
§ 7. If there be no personal estate which the mar-
shal or collectorcan distrainfor taxesdue ,or if the
personalestatebe insufficientto pay the whole of
such tax,and the owner of the land or town lotshall
failto pay the same by the firstof December in the

84 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
year such tax is due , then the marshal or collector
shall,on the firstday of some county or circuitcourt,
at the court-house door, in the town of Madisonville,
proceed to sellso much of the land and town lotsas
may be necessaryto pay the tax and penalty. Said
salesshallbe for cash. The time, place and terms of
sale, together with a statement of the names of the
owners or occupants, the number or a briefdescrip-
tion of the lot or land,and the amount of tax and
penaltydue thereon,shallbe publishedin a weekly
newspaper publishedin Madisonville,or by not less
than three printedor written noticesto be posted at
publicplaces in the town , for at leastten days next
before the sale .
§ 8. The marshal or collector,after making a sale
of property fortaxes, shallmake a fullreportof his
proceedings to the board of councilmen ; said report
shall show the names of the owners of property, the
names of the purchasers,the amounts for which the
property was sold, and a descriptionof the property.
The marshal or collector shall make oath to said re-
port, and also that he has complied with the laws
relatingto the saleof property for taxes.
§ 9. The board of councilmen may designate an
officerof the town to attend allsalesof property sold
for taxes due the town ,who shall, if no other person
will bid an amount sufficientto pay the taxes, fees
and costs,bid in the said propeaty for the town .
§ 10. All lands and town lots sold for taxes due the
town may be redeemed by the owner or hisrepresent-
ative, at any time within two years from the day of
sale,by paying to the purchaser or his representative
the amount of purchase money and interestthereon,
at ten per cent.per annum from the day of saleuntil
redeemed . The person redeeming shall take the re-
ceiptof the purchaser for the redemption money , and
lodge the same with the town clerk,to be filedwith
the reportof sale. If the purchaser or his agent or
representativedo not residein the county of Hopkins ,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 85
or can not be found in said county,the owner of the
property or her representativemay make affidavitof
the fact,and pay the purchase money and interestto
the town marshal or collector, taking a receiptfor the
same . The affidavitand receiptshallbe lodged with
the town clerk with the marshal's report of sale:
Provided , That infants, insane persons, and those
under the disabilityof coverture,shallhave two years
afterthe removal of saiddisabilityor arrivingat full
age to redeem theirrealestate,but on the same terms
and in the same manner as adultsfreefrom disability.
§ 11. The marshal or his successor must convey the
titleof alllands sold for taxes to the purchaser of the
same , if not redeemed as provided in thisact. If the
purchaser be dead ,the conveyance may be made to
his heirs or devisees .
§ 12. If any person bound for tax shallbe insolvent,
or shallhave removed out of Hopkins county with
his estateafterhe was assessedfor tax, and before a
sufficienttime to collectithas elapsed, the marshal or
collectorshall, at a meeting of the board of council-
men , in the month of January in each year (or later
ifthe board so direct), return a listof such persons,
the amount of tax assessed against them , whether
they have removed ,or be insolvent, and make oath
to the same . The board of councilmen may strike
from said listthose whose taxes could have been col-
lected by reasonable diligence, and for the balance on
said list the marshal or collector shall have credit in
settlement with the town .
§ 13. If the marshal shallfailto give bond as re-
quired by thisact,or ifthe officebe vacant, or for
any other cause the board of councilmen shalldeem
itbest,the said board,by order of record,shallhave
power,and it shallbe theirduty,to appointa col-
lectorto collectthe town taxes leviedby the board ;
and the collectorshallhave allthe power and author-
ityfor the collectionof taxes by distressand saleas
the marshal of said town ,and he shallbe liablein like

86 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
manner for any illegalseizureand sale. Before pro-
ceeding to act, the collectorshalltake an oath to well
and trulydischargehisdutiesas requiredby law,and
shallexecute bond with sureties,to be approved by
saidboard, conditionedin allrespectsas the bond of
the town marshal .
§ 14. If the marshal or collectorhas in his hands
forcollectionany taxesor publicdues coming to the
town of Madisonville, which he believescan not be
collectedby distressor saleof property,he may col-
lect from any person indebted to the tax-payer, by
attachment, in the same mode , and by the same
procedure, as are given to sheriffsin collectingthe
State revenue .
ARTICLE II .
§ 1. That appealsmay be taken from the judgments
of the policecourt to the Hopkins Quarterly and Cir-
cuitCourt,in likemanner thatappeals are taken from
the courtsof justicesof the peace.
§ 2. That upon judgments for finesrendered in the
policecourt,it shallbe lawful for the court to issue
capiaspro finefor the imprisonment of the defendant
in the jailof Hopkins county or in the work -house of
saidtown until the fineand costsare paid, or execu-
tionmay issueagainstthe estateof the defendant for
the fineand costs, as in civilcases.
§ 3. The ordinancesof said town now in force, not
in conflictwith this act, shall continue in forceuntil
repealedor modifiedby the board of councilmen.
The records of the trustees and board of council-
men of said town , and allbonds and contractshere-
tofore made by the town or for its benefit, shallre-
main in forceand are made publicrecords.
§ 4. That every male citizenof said town ,who is
qualifiedto vote forRepresentative,shall be entitled
to vote for officers to be elected under this act : Pro-
vided, That before any person shall be qualifiedto
vote he must have paid his taxes for the years preced-
ing the election,and allfinesdue the town .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 87
§ 5. The electionsunder this act shallbe conducted
by the clerk of the town of Madisonville , whose duty
shall be to give not less than fivedays' written or
printed notice of each election and the officersto be
elected; and said noticesshallbe posted at not less
than threepublicplacesin the town . The clerkshall
give to each officera certificateof election.
§ 6. The electionsshallbe viva voce,except dumb
persons, who may vote by ballot; and the election
shall be held between the hours of six o'clock, ante
meridian, and seven o'clock, post meridian.
§7. All laws in forcein thisCommonwealth punish-
ing illegalvoting at Stateelectionsshallapply to per-
sons voting illegallyfor officersof said town ,subject
only to such qualificationsas are made in thisact.
§ 8. The board of councilmen may by ordinance pro-
vide the way and manner for deciding any contested
election between claimants to offices who are selected
under this act .
§ 9. The officersnow holding officein the town of
Madisonville shall hold and discharge the duties of
their several positionsuntil the end of their terms,
and until the next election,as provided in this act ;
but in the dischargeof theirdutiesshallbe governed
by the provisionsof thisact,so soon as the same goes
into effect.
§ 10. Any vacancy in the officesof said town shall
be filledby the board of councilmen unless otherwise
provided in this act. The person receivingan ap-
pointment may hold untilthe succeedingelection.
§ 11. The board of councilmen shall have full
power to fix and provide penaltiesfor a violationof
any of the by -laws or ordinancesof the town in such
sums , and for such terms of imprisonment , as they
may deem best,not, however , in any case to exceed
one hundred dollarsfineor thirtydays imprisonment ,
eitheror both, for each offense.
§12. The board of councilmen shall cause a true
and accurate survey to be made of said town ,show-
1

88 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
ing the locationof the streets, alleys and town lots
therein,with the dimensions of same , from which a
correctedplator map shallbe made . When said sur-
vey and plat shallreceivethe approval of saidboard ,
the same shallbe so certifiedby the clerk, and re-
corded in the clerk'sofficeof the Hopkins County
Court .
§ 13. This act shall take effectand be in force from
itspassage.
Approved May 3,1890.
CHAPTER 1238 .
AN ACT to amend the charterof the Frankfort Coal and Iron Mining ,
Oil,Salt and Timber Company .

monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That an act to charter the " Frankfort Coal and

proved May fifth, one thousand eight hundred and
eighty,be, and the same ishereby, amended by strik-
ing out of the third sectionthe words " two hundred

thereof the words " five hundred thousand dollars ."
§2 . This act to be in force from and after its pas-
sage .

stock shallbe paid for as may be called for by said

scribed for. At the firstmeeting of the stockholders
said president, vice-president and directorsshall be

lar meetings of stockholders shall be held on the
firstMonday in April of each year afterthe firstyear

CHAPTER 1243 .
AN ACT relatingtotheworking of thepublicroadsinDaveisscounty.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . Provided , That hereafter,when the clerkof the
Daveiss County Court shalldelivercopiesof the order
appointingsurveyorsofthepublicroadstothesheriff,
asprovidedinsectiontwenty-six,chapterninety-four,
of the General Statutes, and take his receiptfor the
same , it shall be that he make his returns to the
county court clerk, instead of to the clerk'sofficeof
the circuitcourt,as now provided by law .
§ 2. The surveyor shallservefor two years, as now
provided by law, and shallbe exempt from thepay-
ment of the county levy for the time, not exceeding,
however , two years .
§3 . The surveyor shallbe requiredto work his road
not lessthan six days in each year.
§ 4. If the surveyor failsto work his road, as pro-
vided herein, six days in each year, he shallbe liable
in a civilactionfor alldamages to any person or per-
sons by reason of his road being out of order.
§ 5. When any surveyor failsto keep his road in
good travelingorder, any person may go before the

94 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
county court at any time and entera ruleagainsthim ,
in the name of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , to
show cause why he failsto keep his road in good
order, and may have as many witnesses summoned as
the county judge may deem necessary.
§ 6. And it shall be the duty of the county judge
to order the clerk of the county court to issue a sum-
mons and direct it to any peace officerto summons
saidsurveyor to appear beforethe next regularterm
of the Daveiss County Court, and show cause why he
failsto work or repair his road .
§ 7. Surveyor shallhave at leastten days' notice.
§ 8. If the surveyor responds or answers the rule,
and proves that his road isin good order, or that he
has worked and repairedand put his road in good
order, the rule shall be dismissed upon his paying
cost of said rule: Provided , That if the surveyor of
saidroad proves thatthe road was in good order,then
the costshallbe paid by the party complaining. Said
response or answer shall be in writing, and verified
by the surveyor.
§ 9. If the surveyor failsto respond or answer the
ruleas herein provided, he shallbe fined the sum of
ten dollarsand cost,and a capias may be issued, as
now provided by law,in casesof finesin favorof the
Commonwealth of Kentucky .
§ 10. And it shall not bar a rule being entered
againsthim at the next term of the county court, un-
lesshe shallrepair, or cause to be repaired,his road.
§ 11. Itshallbe the duty of the county attorney of
Daveiss county to attend and represent the Common-
wealth in such action, and shall receive the same fee
as is provided by law in cases of breaches of the
peace .
§ 12. It shall be the duty of the county judge ,
under this act, to enter up an order requiring the
clerk of the circuit court to rterieve all road orders
that may be in his officeto the officeof the county
clerk .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 95 .
§ 13. After said order is made and entered on the
order -book of the county court, then this act shall
apply to allsurveyors heretoforeappointed, and have
not served out their time .
§ 14. Surveyors are required to keep the drift
clearedaway from allthe bridgesand culvertson the
roads of which they respectivelyare surveyors.
§ 15. This act shall be in force from and after its
passage, and shallapply only to Daveiss county .
Approved May 3,1890.

CHAPTER 1245 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe People'sSupply Gas Company , in Boyd
county,
Be it enacted by theGeneral Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That George N. Brown ,C. Cecil,Junior,Thomas
R. Brown ,Z. F. Smith ,C. Cecil,Senior, W. Houshall
and W. Hume Logan ,and their successorsand as-
signs,be,and they are hereby,created a body -corpo-
rate,under the name and styleof the People's Sup-
ply Gas Company ; and by that name shall have
perpetual succession, and the power to contract and
be contractedwith,to sue and be sued, implead and
be impleaded ; to purchase, receiveby gift,or own
otherwise,any property necessary to the purposes of
thisact, real,personal or mixed ; may have a com-
mon seal,and alterthe same at pleasure; and may
establishby -laws,and make rules and regulationsfor
the management of the affairsof the company , not
in conflict with the laws of this State or the United
States .
§ 2. The affairsof said company shallbe managed
by a board of directorsof not more than seven nor
less than three directors, who shall be elected for one
year,or untiltheirsuccessorsareelectedand quali-
fied. A majority of directorsat any meeting may
constitutea quorum for business. The stockholders
shallelectthe directorsat their annual meeting, but
any vacancy in the board may be filledat any time
by the directorsthemselves. The persons named in
the firstsectionhereof as corporatorsshallcompose
the firstboard of directors, with fullpowers conferred
on subsequent boards. The directorsshall electa
president, secretary and treasurer, and such other
officersand agents as they may deem proper. In all
stockholders' meetings one vote shallbe castfor each

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 97
share of stock held and represented,to be voted in
person or by writtenproxy .
§ 3. The capitalstock of saidcompany shallbe such
amount as the directorsmay fix, not less than five
thousand nor more than one milliondollars,divided
into shares of one hundred dollars each , which shall
be subscribed and paid for,by any person or corpora
tion, in such amounts , and upon terms prescribed
by the board of directors, and transferableon the
books of the same .
§ 4. Said company is hereby empowered to own ,by
purchase or otherwise, coal and coal mines and other
mineralsand materials, for use in the manufacture
of gas for fuelor lighting; to develop,utilize, own ,
convey or otherwisedisposeof naturalgas,petroleum,
saltwater, building stone, cement , clay or other
mineral or material of marketable value that may
be found on the lands which it may acquire by
purchase, lease or otherwise; but the main pur-
pose of the company shall be to manufacture
gas for fuel or light, and to produce the same
from coal or shale, or other materials, or by bor-
ing for natural gas ; and to these ends said com-
pany is authorized to purchase and to constructany
and all machinery and equipments necessary to
mine coal or other material, and to move the same
to their machinery and works ; to manufacture or
otherwise to produce gas,to lay pipes and operatethe
same for the deliveryof gas, and may employ other
and suitable means for the conveyance, utilization,
distribution, storage and sale of the products of the
company in Boyd county, in thisState, or to any
pointsin other States, and to build across any stream
in so doing,to and from any of itslands or property.
§5 . With the authoritiesof any cityor town said
company shall have power to contract to lay pipes
and constructworks for the deliveryof gas forfuelor
lightwithin the corporatelimitsof same .

§ 6. This company shallhave power to unite or con-
solidatewith any other company operating,on terms
mutually agreed on ; and in order to carry out the
purposes herein set forth, said company shall have
the power to acquireby purchase,giftor condemna-
tion, under the laws of the State, the right of way
and use of any land along the route selectedby the
board of directors, for the purposes of conveyance of
the products of itsmining or manufacturing, in order
to the economic use and disposalof the same ; and all
such land as may be needed for such conveyance and
rightof way may be condemned and paid for in the
manner prescribedin the act, entitled" An act to pre-
scribethe mode of condemning lands for railroadand
turnpike companies," approved April eleventh, one
thousand eight hundred and eighty-two ,the provis-
ions and powers of which act shall apply to and be

§ 7. The principal place of business of this com-
pany shallbe in Catlettsburg, or such cityor town in
this State as the board of directorsmay name . The
private property of the stockholdersof the company
shallbe exempt from itscorporatedebts and liabili-
ties.
§ 8. The said company shallhave the power to bor-
row any sum of money , not exceeding one hundred
thousand dollars, for the purposes set forth in this
act; and may issue bonds for the payment thereof,
bearing interestnot over six per cent. per annum ,
payable semi-annually, with coupons attached, the
principal to mature at such time as the directors may
fix, not exceeding twenty-five years from the date
thereof; and to securethe payment of same the com -
pany may execute a mortgage upon allits property,
realand personal. The saidcompany shallbegin work
in good faithwithin three years from the date of this
act .
§ 9. This act shall take effect from the date of its
passage . Approved May 3,1890.

sage .

CHAPTER 1259 .
AN ACT toestablishan additionalvotingplaceinDaveisscounty.

monwealth of Kentucky :

ing place in the county of Daveiss, the place of voting

lished shall be as follows: Beginning at the bridge

road ; thence with said road to the intersection of the

Birkhead's farm ; thence with Birkhead's eastlineto

across North Panther creek, on the said road ; and
thence with the said creek to the intersection of
Burnett's Fork creek ; thence with the said Bur-
nett'sFork creek to the beginning.

CHAPTER 1260 .
AN ACT toincorporateGray Lodge number twenty-seven,Knights of
Pythias,ofVersailles,Kentucky .

monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the officersand members of Gray Lodge
number twenty-seven, Knights of Pythias, of Ver-
sailles, Kentucky , and their successors,be, and are
hereby,created a body -politicand corporate, in per-
petual succession, by the name and styleof Gray
Lodge number twenty-seven,Knights of Pythias, of

ble of contractingand being contractedwith,of suing
and being sued, of pleading and being impleaded, of
purchasing and holding real and personal estate; to
receiveall necessary conveyances ; to sell, convey ,
mortgage and disposeof all such estate,realor per-
sonal, as they now have or hereafteracquire.
§ 2. That the supervisionand care of the funds, in-
vestments and other lodge property shall be, and is
hereby,intrustedand confided to threeof the mem-
bers of saidlodge,and their successorsin office,who
are to be known as trusteesof said lodge, who shall
have fullpower to make allcontractsfor said lodge,
such as purchasing,building, renting, insuringor for
any other purpose as said lodge may direct,pursuant
to the rules,by -laws and instructionsof said lodge.

ing ten thousanddollars,which shallbe thelimitof
itsdebt, and issueitsbond or notes therefor,bearing
a rate of interestnot exceeding eight per cent. per
annum , and to secure the payment of the same may

erty .

ernment as itmay ,from time to time,deem necessary

same at pleasure.

sage .

CHAPTER 1261 .
AN ACT to empower the county commissionersof Kenton county to
make subscriptionto the capitalstock of turnpikeroads in said
county .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1. That the county of Kenton , the county commis-
sionersallbeing present and concurring,be,and they
are hereby , authorized and empowered to subscribe
stock forand on behalfofsaidcounty to any or allturn-
pike road companies which have been or may hereafter
be incorporatedin Kenton county under chapter fifty-
sixof the General Statutes,or by the Legislatureof the
State. Said subscriptionmay be any sum not exceed-
ing one thousand dollarsforeach mile of road within
said county,or in that proportionfor fractionalparts
of a mile .
§ 2. Said county commissioners, all being present
and all concurring, shallhave power to levy a tax
upon all the property in said county of Kenton sub-
ject to taxation for revenue purposes, outside the
citiesand towns of Covington, Central Covington and
West Covington and Ludlow , sufficientfor the pur-
pose of paying said subscriptionof stock aforesaid,
which levy shallin no year exceed the sum of twenty-
five cents on each one hundred dollars of taxable

122 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
property, to be collectedin the same manner and by
the same officer,under the same liabilitiesand penal-
ties that the State revenue tax is now collected .
§ 3. That the county commissioners shallnot levy
the tax deed directitscollectionuntilthe company or
companies desiringto availthemselves of the benefit
of the county subscriptionshall present to the said
commissioners a recordshowing the estimatedcost of
each mile or part of a mile,and that they have a bona
fide subscriptionsufficient, when added to the county
subscription,to constructeach mile or fractionthereof
of such road or roads to which the county subscription
is asked .
§ 4. The amount of subscription per mile made
under the provisionsof this act to the capitalstock
of any company shallbe paid to the treasurerof such
company on the completion of each mile of road or
fraction thereof, as aforesaid.
§ 5. The dividends for stock subscribed under au-
thorityof thisact shallbe paid intothe county treas-
urer, and be applied in aid of the county expenses
incurred outside of said towns and cities.
§ 6. Before said county commissioners shall sub-
scribestock to,or exerciseany of the powers herein
provided for, the question shall be submitted to the
qualifiedvotersof said portionof saidcounty as afore-
said, whether or not they are in favor of empowering
said commissioners to subscribe to the capital stock of
turnpike roads constructed within said portion of
Kenton county , at an electionto be held at each
votingplace in saidportionof Kenton county , at the
regular August electionin the year one thousand
eight hundred and ninety; and if the affirmative
of said proposition shall receivea majority of the
votes cast, the said commissioners shall be author-
ized to exercise the powers conferred by this act,
and said commissioners may submit the question to
the qualifiedvoters of said part of the county every
two years under and by this act, if they deem it

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 123
proper , and so enter it upon theirrecords; further-
more , they shall be the solejudges as to whether or
not they shallsubscribestock to any road,and are not
bound to subscribeto any .
§ 7. The said commissioners may subscribeto the
stock of any company that may undertake the recon-
struction of the Covington and Lexington Turnpike
Road , lying in Kenton county , south of Walton ,
which has been abandoned by said Covington and
Lexington Company , in like manner as any other
road in the county of Kenton . The commissioners
are authorized to take stock in any company under-
taking to reconstructany other abandoned turnpike
road in said county .
§ 8. It shallbe the duty of the sheriffof Kenton
county to open a poll at each of the voting places in
said county as provided for in the sixth section of
this act ; and said election shall be conducted ,and re-
turns thereof made and canvassed , and the result de-
clared, in the same manner and by the same officers
who conducted the electionfor county officers. The
sheriffshallgive thirtydays' noticeof the time and
places of holding said electionby printed notices,
posted in at leastthree public places in the neigh-
borhood of each voting place, and by at least two
insertions in some newspaper published in Kenton
county next before the election.
§ 9. The county court shallcause the resultof said
election held under this act recorded in its order-
book , and be certifiedto said county commissioners.
The expenses incurred in holding the electionor elec-
tions in accordance thereof under this act shall be
paid out of the county levy.
§ 10. This actshallbe in forcefrom itspassage.
. Approved May 3,1890.

CHAPTER 1273 .
AN ACT to legalizean appropriationof fifteenhundred dollars,and
the levyof an ad valorem tax of eightcentson each one hundred
dollars' worth of taxablepropertyin Crittendencounty,Kentucky ,
made by thecountycourtof saidcountyforthe benefitof thosewho
sufferedfrom the cyclone.
WHEREAS , On the twenty -seventh day of March ,
one thousand eight hundred and ninety, a terrific
cyclone passed through Crittendencounty,Kentucky ,
destroying the livesof many , and the property of all
in the track of itsdeadly march , leavingthe survivors
wholly destituteof the necessitiesof life; and where-
as, the judge of the county court of said county, for
the purpose of giving quick and immediate reliefto
the sufferersby reason of said disaster, convened the
justicesof the peace of said county ; and whereas,
said court, being constitutedby a majorityof said
magistrates, made an appropriationof fifteenhundred
dollars for the purpose of said relief, and levied an
ad valorem tax of eight cents on each one hundred
dollars' worth of taxable property in said county
with which to meet and pay the amount of said sum
so appropriated; and whereas,doubt existsas to the
authority of said court to make said appropriation
and levy for said purpose ; now , for remedy whereof,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That said order of appropriation,for the pur-
pose of relievingthe sufferersin the track of said
cyclone in saidcounty, and the levy of an ad valorem
tax of eight cents on each one hunred dollars' worth
of the taxable property of said county, so appropri-
ated and leviedby saidcourton thefirstday ofApril,
one thousand eight hundred and ninety,be, and the
same ishereby,legalizedand made valid,binding.
§ 2. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage . Approved May 5, 1890 .

166 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .

as policejudge,town marshal or trusteeforsaid town

published twice a year, in June and December , and

the officialsof said town .
§ 4. This act shall take effectand be in force from
and after itspassage .

CHAPTER 1279 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Midway Cemetery Company .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That John A. Steele, Richard S. Starks,John
B. Swope , H. C. Poynter and Richard Godson , and
theirassociates, be, and they are hereby declared to
be,a body -politicand corporate,under the name and
Corporatename.styleof the Midway Cemetery Company ; and by that
name said company shallhave perpetual succession;
shall be able and capable in law to have and use a
common seal,sue and be sued,plead and be implead-
ed, in all the courts of this Commonwealth , and to
have and exerciseallthe rightsincidentto corpora-
tions. They shallhave power to purchase and hold
any quantityof landin Woodford county,Kentucky ,
not exceeding fiftyacres(includingany land that may
now be held by them , or any of them ,jointlyas indi-
viduals,thatmay hereafterbe devotedto the objects
of saidcemetery company ) ; saidland tobe held solely
and exclusivelyfora cemetery and ornamental grounds
connected therewith; and shall never be alienated,
soldor used forany otherpurpose whatever ; but said
company may erectsuch necessarybuildingsand im-
provements thereon, for the use and occupancy of
theirsuperintendentand hisfamily,as may be deemed
expedient ; and may permit their superintendent, or
other officers, to use any portion of saidgrounds not
sold for burial lots for horticulturalpurposes ; but
the same shallnot be used in any manner inconsistent
with the reverenceand respectdue to the cemetery of
the dead .
§2 . That no part of said land, or any lot therein,
shallbe subjectto sale,by virtueof any mortgage,
judgment ,executionor decree,forany cause whatever ;
nor shallit be subject to condemnation for any rail-
road, turnpike road, or other purpose whatever ; nor

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 173
shallany such road or passway be opened through said
grounds ,or any part of them , without the consent of
said company .
§ 3. That within one year afterthe passage of this
act,said incorporators,or any threeof them ,may or-
ganize under this charter by the electionof a presi-
dent , secretaryand treasurer(who shallbe ex officio
directorsof the affairsof said company ), and such
other directors, superintendent and other officersas
may be deemed by them necessaryor expedient.
§ 4. That saidcompany shallhave the power to sell
buriallotsat eitherpublic or privatesale; and when
sold,itshallbe the duty of the presidentto execute
to the purchaser, giving the number of the lot, to-
gether with such brief descriptionas to location,
and so forth, when necessary, a certificatethereof,
which shall vest the purchaser with the titleto such
lot .
§ 5. They shallhave power to make such by -laws
for the regulationand controlof lots, and for the con-
trol,care and management of the graves and grounds ,
and the mode of ornamentingthe same, as may be
necessaryforpreservingthe beauty, symmetry and
decency of the grounds and graves.
§ 6. They shallprepare and keep an accuratemap
and surveyof saidgroundsand lotson saidcemetery,
and shallkeep a record of allsalesand transfersof
lots .
§ 7. That if any person or personsshallforcibly,Penalties.
and without lawful authority, violateany of the
graves of the dead, or willfullydefaceany of the
tombstones, monuments , shrubbery, fixtures, or any
thing on saidgrounds, such person or persons so
offending, or any one aidingor abettingthem ,shall
be deemed guiltyof a misdemeanor , and , on convic-
tionthereof by the policecourt of the town of Mid-
way ,shallbe finedin thesum ofnotlessthan fivenor
more than fiftydollars, or imprisonedin the county

174 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
jailof Woodford county not lessthan ten nor more
than thirty days .
§ 8. The town of Midway may purchase any num-
ber of the lotsof said cemetery company as may be
designatedby ordinance or resolutionof the chairman
and board of trusteesthereof, as now provided by
itscharter,and not inconsistenttherewith; and lots
so purchased by said town of Midway shall be used
for burying therein paupers,strangersor sojourners
who may die in said town ,having no estatesufficient
fordefrayingburialexpenses or purchasing a lot,and
such lots shallbe free,but subject to the controlof
saidboard of trusteesand cemetery company as here-
in provided ; and the county of Woodford , Independ-
ent Order of Odd Fellows, the Buford Lodge , number
fourhundred and ninety-four, of Free and Accepted
Masons ,at Midway ,Kentucky ,shallalsobe privileged
and are empowered to purchase and hold such num-
ber of said lotsof said cemetery company as either
of said bodies or lodges, or the Woodford County
Court, may designate.
§ 9. This act to take effect from and after itspas-
sage .
Approved May 3,1890 .
CHAPTER 1280 .
AN ACT tomake theofficeof countycourtclerkand county surveyor
of Henry county compatible.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of theCom-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court clerk of Henry county
be,and he is hereby , authorized, if he desiresto do
so,to appointthecountysurveyor,of Henry county,
a deputy county court clerk, forthe purpose of taking
acknowledgmentsof deedsand otherpapers,and the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 175
officesare made compatible to that extent, and ac-
knowledgments taken by the county surveyor after
his appointment and qualificationare entitled to
same creditas iftaken by the county court clerk; and
alllaws in conflictare repealed.
§ 2. That thisactshalltake effectfrom and afterits
passage .
[Became a law May 3, 1890, withoutthe approvalof the Governor.]

CHAPTER 1303 .
AN ACT providingpunishment forvagrantsand tramps in Marion
county .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That if any male person over sixteen years of
age,who is able to work ,be found loiteringor ram-
bling about,not possessing the means to maintain
himself, or who isidle or dissolutein habits,without
lawful means of subsistence,or who is going about
begging,without betaking himself to labor or some
honest callingto obtain a livelihood,he shallbe ad-
judged to be a vagrant,and , upon convictionin any
courtof competent jurisdiction, he shall be punished
by a whipping of not less than fivenor more than
fiftylashes, with a leathern whip , well laid on his
bare back .
§ 2. Said whipping shall be administered by the
sheriffof the county in which such convictionis had ,
in public,and near the court-house door of such coun-
ty; but this act shall apply only to the county of
Marion .
§ 3. This act shall take effectfrom and after itspas-
sage; and alllaws or partsof laws in conflictwith the
provisionsof thisact are hereby repealed.
Approved May 6,1890.

CHAPTER 1308 .
AN ACT for the benefit of Peter C. Sower .
WHEREAS , Peter C. Sower obtained a licensefrom
the county clerkof Woodford county to retailspirit-
ous,vinous and malt liquorsin the town of Versailles,
Kentucky , for and during the year one thousand
eight hundred and ninety, for which he paid one
hundred and fiftydollars; and afterdoing business
under said licensefor about three weeks his family
became sick, and he was forced thereby to abandon
said liquorbusinessat Versailles, and to leasethe
small saloon there to one William Bodkins ; there-
fore,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That said Sower is hereby authorized to trans-
fer said licenseto said Bodkins for this year, one
thousand eight hundred and ninety.
§ 2. This act to take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved May 6 , 1890 .

CHAPTER 1316 .
AN ACT toestablisha citygovernment forthe town of Catlettsburg,
in Boyd county.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
ARTICLE I.
§1 . That bereafterthe town of Catlettsburg shall
be known and styled City of Catlettsburg, and that
the boundary thereofbe extended as follows,and that
so much of the county of Boyd as is embraced within
the followingboundary ,to wit : Beginning at a large
beech standing on the east sideof Key's creek about
three feet from a stone, the west corner in division be-
tween lands of L. T. Moore and N. S. Williams ;
thence in a straightlineto cornerof Killen and Mar-
cum with the M. Runyon lands on south sideof the
Catlettsburgand Cannonsburg Turnpike County Road ;
and thence to center of Chadwick's creek , where the
militarylinecrossesthe same ; thence with the thread
of said creek to its mouth on the Big Sandy river;
thence to and with the boundary lineof State of Ken-
tucky and Boyd county, on the Big Sandy and Ohio
rivers,to a point opposite the mouth of Key's creek ;
thence to the mouth of Key's creek ; thence up and
with the thread of same to a point opposite the large
beech, the beginning corner ; thence to the place of
beginning. And the persons residing within said
boundary are hereby createda body -politicand corpo-
rate, by the name and style of City of Catlettsburg,
and by that name may have perpetual succession, sue
and be sued , and may contract and be contracted
with,and purchaseand receivedonationsof allprop-
erty necessaryor suitablefor use or ornamentation of
said city; and may selland convey the same ; and
may or not use a common seal; and shall be vested
with allpowers conferred,or that may be conferred,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 287
by the general laws of State to bodies of likecorpo-
rate character .
§ 2. So much of the county roads as liewithin the
limitsof the city of Catlettsburg, as herein defined,
shallremain county roads,and be under the same con-
troland supervisionof the Boyd County Court and
commissioners as heretofore, under the road law for
Boyd county ,except for jurisdictionof the mayor's
court of saidcity; and infractionof the provisionof
this charterand of ordinancesof saidcityand police
regulationsthereof shallbe treated as within and a
part of saidcity. When residentsof the city, who
are requiredby law to work saidroads,shall perform
labor upon same , such persons shallbe exempt from
the payment of poll-tax.
ARTICLE II .
Wards and Representation .
§ 1. Said cityisdivided intofour wards , as follows:
The firstward shallinclude all that part of the city
which liessouth of the presentsouth corporatelineof
the present town of Catlettsburg,and a projectionof
that lineat itswestward termination, directlywest-
ward tillitstrikethe western or back boundary line
of the cityof Catlettsburg, as given in sectionone of
article one .
§ 2. The second ward shallincludeallthat part of
the citythat liesbetween the firstward on the south
and the center of Catlett'screek on the north .
§ 3. The third ward shallinclude all that part of
the citythat liesbetween thesecondward and pres-
ent north corporatelineof the present town of Cat-
lettsburg,and the extensionof that north corporate
line directlywest untilitstrikesthe back or western
boundary lineof thecityof Catlettsburg,asgiven in
section one, articleone .
§ 4. The fourthward shall include all that part of
the citythat liesnorth of the third ward .

§ 5. The board of councilhereafterelectedmay , by
ordinance, change said wards , or createnew wards , at

mands ,endeavoring to equalizethe populationas near
as possible: Provided , Such alterationshall not be
made except on a generalcensus of allthe inhabitants
of saidcitybeing taken previousthereto.
§ 6. The wards of the cityshallbe entitledto elect
and be representedby two common councilmen each.
ARTICLE III .
Board of Councilmen , Duties , Etc.
§1 . That the fiscal, municipal and prudentialcon-
cernsof said city, togetherwith the government and
controlthereof,and the exerciseof the legislativeand
contractingpowers herein granted, shall be vested in
a board of councilmen,to be electedon the firstMon-
day in January , one thousand eight hundred and

hereinafterprovided,by the qualifiedvoters of said
city,and to be styled Board of Councilmen of City
of Catlettsburg. A majority of saidcouncilmen shall
constitutea quorum for the transactionof business.
In meetings of said board each councilman present
shallbe entitledto one vote. No one shallbe eligible
for election to office of councilman who has been a
member of the council by electionduring the two
terms next preceding his election, nor who has not
been a bona fide housekeeper of, and residedwithin,
the limitsof saidcitytwo years next precedinghiselec
tion,and who shallnot be at the time of his election
a qualifiedvoter of said city, and an owner of real
estate therein at the time of his election. Each coun-
cilman,beforeauthorizedto enterupon dischargeof
hisduty,shalltakean oath to faithfullyperform the
same .
§ 2. The term of officeof councilmen shall be two
years from date of theirelection, except as otherwise
