provided herein, and they shallhold theirofficeuntil
theirsuccessorsshallhave been duly electedand qual-
ified .
§ 3. The mayor of the city, who shall,by virtue of
his office,be presidentof the board of council,shall
have power at all sessionsof the council to enforce
good order and decorum ,and to punish for contempt
in same manner when holdinghiscourt; and incase
of tievote at any meeting of said board ,cast the de-
ciding vote. In case of mayor's absence,the board
of councilmay appoint a presidentpro tempore, or in
case of a vacancy in that office, may electanother in

every month ,at such times and placesasitmay desig-
nate, and oftenerif emergency requires it; and may
prescribemode of callingspecialmeetings ; and mem-
bers of the councilfailingto attend a meeting shallbe
liable to a fine not exceeding two dollars for each ab-
sence, to be assessedby the board of council: Pro-
vided , That no member shall be fined if his absence
was unavoidable, or he has a good and sufficientex-
cuse therefor .
§ 5. All vacanciesthat may occur afteran election,
eitherin the officeof councilman ,mayor or citymar-
shal,may be filledby the board of council; and the
persons thus appointed shallremain in officeuntilthe
succeeding annual election,when the unexpired term,
if any , shall be filled; if none, successorsin office
shall be elected for the term .
§ 6. After the firstgeneralelectionfor councilmen,
the councilmen from each ward , respectively,elected
at said election,shalldetermine by lot which ones of
the two councilmen from thewards ,respectively,shall
have their terms of officeterminated one year from

one year,and allthe others electedat said election,
two years from date of theirelection; and on the first

to the same ,and fix fees and compensation for his
services .
Sixteenth.To provide for the preventionof training
or breaking horses, or exhibitingstallionsin the pub-
licstreetsand placesof the city.
Seventeenth. To provide for the removal from the
limitsof the cityor killingof mischievous or vicious
animals, and for the punishment , by suitable fines
and penalties,of the owner or keeper of such animals
forallowingthem to go at large.
Eighteenth.The board ofcouncilshallhave power to
purchase lands for,and locatecemeteries,eitherwithin
the citylimitsor elsewherein Boyd county,and exer-
cisefulland complete controlover the same ,and en-
force the proper regulationand management thereof
by adequate finesand penalties.
Nineteenth. The board of councilshallhave power to
appoint or employ an attorneyto attend the business
of the city, and to prosecuteforand on behalf of the
board of councilallprosecutionsin which the board
of councilmay be interested,and to allow him a rea-
sonable compensation therefor. That whatever per-
centagemay be allowed the cityattorney on finesim

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 295
posed by the mayor , or may be allowed him by the
board of councilas compensation forhisservices,shall
be his fees for such services within the meaning of the
tenth section of the third article of the Constitution of
Kentucky , and not subjectto be remitted.
12. The board of council shall, by ordinance,
whenever the public necessityshallrequire it, cause
any new streetor alleyto be opened , or an old street
or alleyextended or widened , or establishany market
place, and tobuild,extend, improve,or gradeor pave
the wharf or wharves in front of said city on the
Ohio or Big Sandy rivers, and to procure the con-
demnation of any realestateforsuch purposes : Pro-
vided, That in no case shallprivateproperty be taken
for any such purpose without the written consent of
theclaimant, or a justand fullcompensation therefor
be firstpaid in money tosaidclaimant. Ifthe amount
of such compensation can not be fixed by agreement ,
the board of councilshallcause a petitionto be filed
in the Boyd County Court,statingthe streetor alley
they wish opened , widened or extended,or the lands
they desirecondemned to build, extend or improve
the wharf or wharves , and the names of the owners ,
if known ,of the lotsand lands through or over which
they desire to have said street or alley opened ,
widened or extended , and the width thereof, or the
land or lands they desirefor the purpose of building,
extending or improving the wharf or wharves , and
thereupon said court shallorder a summons to issue
forsuch owner or owners to appear on some day of
that or some subsequent term of said court, to show
cause why such streetor alleyshould not be opened ,
widened or extended ,or such wharf or wharves should
not be made , extended or improved , which sum-
mons shall be executed on such owners , if in the
county of Boyd ,if not, on his,her or their agent, if
one isknown ; and on the return of the summons exe-
cuted,or thereturnof no inhabitantor known agent,
and no one appearing after warning order,the said
.

296 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
2.
court shallorder the said street or alley,wharf or
wharves to be opened , widened , extended, made or
improved ;and ifany one or more of such owners of
lotsor lands appear and demand it, the court shall
award a writ of ad quod damnum ,to be directed to
the proper county officer,to be executed and returned
as provided by law for writsof that nature for open-
ing public roads; and on the return of the writ duly
executed, the court shall order the board of council
of the cityof Catlettsburgto pay thedamages assessed
to the party or partiesentitledthereto,and shallorder
the street,alleyor ground for wharf or wharves to be
opened , widened , extended, made or improved upon
the payment of saiddamages ,
§ 13. That if for any reason the board of council do
not think proper to intrustthe citymarshal with the
collectionof the taxes or otherrevenues of the city,or
the marshal of the cityshall be unable or refuse to
perform thatduty , they shallhave power to appoint a
collector, who , aftertaking an oath and executing a
bond ,as requiredof the marshal,shallhave fullpower
and authorityto perform allthe dutiesrequiredof the
marshal in the collection of the taxes and other reve-
nues of the city; and hisactsso performed shallhave
the same validityand effectas if performed by the
marshal ; and he shallreceive such compensation for
his servicesas the board of councilmay deem reason-
able .
§ 14. The board of council may appoint a street
commissioner to superintend the streetsand alleys
of the city, who may be removed by said board of
councilat any time,and another may be appointed by
the board in his stead ; and in the event the board of
councildo not appoint a streetcommissioner,the city
marshal shallbe,by virtueof his office,also commis-
sionerof the streetsand alleysof the city until an
appointment of the board of council.
§ 15. All ordinances heretoforepassed by the board
of trusteesof town Catlettsburg, and now in effect,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 297
shall be in forceand apply to cityof Catlettsburgas
they did to said town ; and allactionson same ,and
on all contracts heretofore made with said town , shall
be maintained in name of and be for the benefit of
cityof Catlettsburg,as heretoforeforsaid town .
§ 16. The board of councilshallhave power to pass
ordinancesto permit personsagainstwhom judgments
are rendered for finesand cost,for violationof any
ordinance of the city, to work the same out on public
streetsor alleysof saidcity.
§ 17. The board of councilmay cause a survey to be
made of said city, and have plotthereof recorded in
the Boyd county clerk'soffice; and may designate
the boundary of saidcityby proper monuments ; and
may likewisesurvey the wards , and establishmonu-
ments thereof .
§ 18. All ordinancesor by -laws passed by the board
of council shallgo into effectin ten days after their
passage, unless declared by an order of the board to
take effect at a different date .
ARTICLE IV .
Mayor ,His Duties, Etc.
§1 . At the same time,and in the same manner as
herein provided for the electionof councilmen, a
mayor of said cityshallbe elected by the qualified
voters of said city. The mayor shall be known by
the styleof Mayor of the City of Catlettsburg. The
term of the officeof mayor shall be two years from
date of election; and he shallhold his officeuntil his
successor be electedand qualified. No one shall be
eligibleto the officeof mayor unless he shall have
resided in saidcitytwo years next preceding his elec-
tion,and be a qualifiedvoterof saidcity. The mayor ,
before enteringupon the dutiesof his office,shalltake
an oath to faithfullyperform the same . He shall,by
virtue of his office,be president of board of council,and
cast the deciding vote in allcasesof tieon any ques

298 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
tionvoted upon by saidboard . He shallbe a judicial
officer,and presideat the sittingsof the mayor's court,
and have jurisdictionwithin limitsof said city,and
for two miles in every directionfrom corporatelimits
of same ,where not granted exclusivelyto any other
municipality,in allcases,civiland criminal,which ex-
istinglaws,or laws that may be passed by the General
Assembly of Kentucky , are conferredupon justicesof
thepeace,and concurrentjurisdictionwith the Boyd
Quarterly Court in civilcases,where the amount in
controversy,exclusiveof interestand costs, does not
exceed two hundred dollars ; and in addition thereto,
shallhave alljurisdictionin feloniesand misdemean-
ors which ,by the Code of Practice in criminal cases,
is conferred upon mayors of citiesas such. The
mayor's court of said city shall be styled Mayor's
Court of City of Catlettsburg,and shall be a court of
record,and copiesof itsproceedings,certifiedby the
mayor , shall be competent evidence in any court of
thisCommonwealth . Said mayor shallhave power to
issue summons for witnesses to give evidence in cases,
proceedingspending beforehiscourt, and upon their
failureto attend,issuecompulsory process to compel
theirattendance, as in cases of attachment for con-
tempt ; he shallhave power to fineand imprison for
contempt:Provided, Said fineshallin no case ex-
ceed ten dollars, nor imprisonment more than twelve
hours ; he shallhave power to requirethe marshal, or
other officerauthorizedtoexecute processin hiscourt,
to summon jurors in any case cognizablebefore him ,
when a jury can be required before a justiceof the
peace or Boyd QuarterlyCourt, and to compel their
attendance as in cases of contempt ; he shall be a
conservatorof the peace, and shall,ex officio,proceed
againstviolatorsof the by-laws and ordinancespassed
by the board of councilwithout the need of inform-
ers .
§ 2. All male persons convicted of any violationof
the by -laws or ordinancesof said city, and failingto

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 299
pay the finesthereforassessedagainst them and the
costs of prosecution, may be compelled to work upon
the streetsand alleys,and other property of said city,
and be confined in the cityjailor lock-up at night
until the fines and costs are discharge , at the rate
fixedby the board of council. It shallnot be lawful
for said work to be done by a substitute.
§ 3. The mayor shallhave power to commit all of-
fenders liableto commitment to the county jail of
Boyd county ; and the jailerthereofshallreceiveand
keep the same as other prisonersare receivedand kept
by him . The mayor shallalsohave power ,on alljudg-
ment in hiscourt in penal prosecutionor misdemean-
ors to issuea capias pro fine, or cause the same to be
repleviedforthwith,or commit to jailof said cityor
of Boyd county,untilthe finesand costs thereofare
discharged .
§4. The mayor shallholdhiscourtsforthetrialof
civilcases on the firstSaturday in every month , and
may continuehis courtsuntilthe businessbefore him
iscompleted. His court shallbe open at all times for
the trialof any breach of the peace and other viola-
tion of the penal and criminal laws,or of the ordi-
nances of saidcity, and shallhold said trialswithout
regard to the time of serviceof process,but shallcon-
tinue from time to time for good cause shown .
§ 5. That upon alljudgments rendered in the may-
or's court,either party shallhave the right to appeal
from said judgments in the same manner and upon
the same terms appeals are or shallbe requiredby the
generallaws to be taken from judgments of mayors
in similar cases .
§ 6. The mayor shall execute bond , with good
surety to the city,for the payment of all finesand
moneys that come to his hands , and for the faithful
discharge of his duties.
§ 7. The mayor shallbe entitledto same fees, to be
taxed as those allowed clerks of quarterly courts

300 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
for similar services, and to be collected in the same
way .
§ 8. All finesinflictedbefore mayor's court for vio-
lationof provisionsof this charter, or of ordinances
of said city, shall be for the benefitof the city of
Catlettsburg; and all warrants issued by the mayor
for said violation shall run in the name of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky for benefitof city of Cat-
lettsburg, and otherwarrants issued by him shallrun
in the name of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , and
the fines assessed under same shall be for the benefit
of Commonwealth , and shallbe paid over,as required
by law, to trusteeof jury fund forBoyd county .
ARTICLE V.
Marshal - His Duties , Powers , Etc.
§ 1. At same time and in same manner as provided
for electionof councilmen and mayor for said city,
a citymarshal shallbe electedby the qualifiedvoters
of said city,who shallbe styledMarshal of the City of
Catlettsburg. No one shall be eligibleto the officeof
marshal unless he shallhave residedin said citytwo
years next precedingthe election,and be a qualified
voter of said city; and no one shall be eligiblefor
more than one term of said office until a term has in-
tervened . The term of officeof city marshal shall be
two years from the date of election ; and he shall hold
his officeuntil his successor iselectedand qualified.
It shallbe the duty of the marshal to attend the sit-
tingsof the courtsto be held by the mayor , preserve
order and execute orders of said court ; to serve all
processes and precepts,and to collectall executions
directed to him from the mayor's court,or any court
thatisauthorizedto directthe same to him ,and make
due returnthereof, in doing which he may go to any
part of the county . He shall collect all taxes due
saidcity,and allotherdemands which may be put into
his hands to collect,and account for and pay over the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 301
same to whomsoever may be entitledthereto, under
the same rules and regulationsrequired by law of
sheriffs; and forfailureto perform any of the duties
required of him ,he shallbe subjectto the same pro-
ceedings had against sheriffsin similar cases. The
said marshal shall be entitled to the same fees for
collectingthe city tax that sheriffsare entitledto
for collecting the county levy ; and in all other
cases, the same allowed sheriffsfor similar ser-
vices: Provided ,however, That saidmayor shallhave
power and authority, whenever it is made to ap-
pear by affidavitthat it is impracticable, or for some
good causethatthe marshal can not executeprocess,
to directthe same to the sheriffor any constable of
Boyd county : Provided further, That the saidmar-
shal shallbe investedwith allthe power and authority
which is given to constablesin all cases cognizable
before said mayor ; and before the said citymarshal
shall proceed to the execution of the duties of his
office,he shalltake an oath beforethe board of coun-
cilthathe will faithfullyand impartiallyexecute the
duties of his office without favor or affection, and
shall also give bond with good security, to be ap-
proved by the board , with such penalty as the board
of council may fix,conditioned for the faithfuldis-
charge of thedutiesof hisoffice,upon which he may
be liableto motion or suitbeforethe mayor's court or
in courts having jurisdictionin similarcases,on bond
of sheriffsand constablesfor failureto dischargeany
duty , or to pay over any taxes or money which ought
to have been collectedby him .
§ 2. The marshal shall have the power and author-
ity to summons any person or persons to assisthim in
the execution of any writ that may come into his
hands , or in the arrestof any person or persons for
the violationof thisact or of any of the by -lawsor
ordinances of said city,or forsuppressionof breaches
of the peace,affray, riot, rout or dispersionof any
unlawful assembly; and any personor personsfailing

302 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
to obey saidsummons and render the necessaryassist-
ance shallbe reported by the marshal to the mayor ,
who shallthereupon enter a judgment against each
person disobeying the summons , for the sum of not
lessthan fivenor more than ten dollars, upon which
judgment a capias pro fineshallissue,which shallbe
collectedaccording to law : Provided , That no such
judgment shallbe rendered untilfivedays' notice, by
warrant issuedby the mayor ,has been given the per-
son or persons so reported .
ARTICLE VI .
Elections - Qualified Voters.
§ 1. All electionsfor said city shall be held be-
tween seven o'clock ante meridian and six o'clock
post meridian, at placesin each ward provided by the
board of council,by judges, sheriffsand clerks,to be
appointed by said board ; and shallbe conducted in
allrespectsas electionsunder the State laws ; and the
penaltiesimposed by the electionlaws of the State
shall apply to offensescommitted at said elections.
The pollsof electionsof councilmen,mayor and mar-
shal shallbe returned to the county court of Boyd
county at the firstterm aftersaid election; and the
said county court shallcertifythe resultof the elec-
tion,so far as mayor is concerned,to the Governor
of the State, whose duty itshallbe to issuea commis-
sionforthe person electedto saidoffice.
§ 2. All male persons over the age of twenty -one
years, and who are inhabitantsof said city, and who
have in good faithresidedin said cityone year next
preceding the election,and in the ward in which they
shallvote sixtydays next preceding the saidelection,
and shallhave paid theirtaxes forthe next preceding
year,shallbe thequalifiedvotersof saidcity.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 303
۱
ARTICLE VII .
Assessor- Appointment of, Duties, Etc.
§ 1. It shallbe the duty of the board of council,at
its firstregular meeting afterthe annual electionof
each year for said city,or as soon thereafteras possi-
ble,to appoint an assessor,who ,afterbeing firstsworn
faithfullyto discharge the duties of his office,shall
callupon the owners of taxableproperty in saidcity,
or their agents,and make out a true listof their es-
tate, realpersonalor mixed,in said citysubjectto
taxationunder generallaws of the State, and owned
by them on the tenth day of January of the year in
which the assessmentismade ,and itsvalue then ; and
this shallbe made upon the oath of the tax-payer or
agent,as now requiredto be administered by county
assessorsin taking listsforState revenue and county
levy.
§ 2. Said assessor,in taking the listof taxableprop-
erty in saidcity,shallhave the same power , and pro
ceed in likemanner , and be governed by likerules,as
prescribedby law for county assessorsin taking list
of taxable property in the county,both in case of res-
identsand non-residentsof said city,exceptifthere
isproperty owned in said cityby non -residentsthere.
of, the said assessorshall, unless listedby owners
thereof, cause the same to be valued by two respect-
able citizensof said citycompetent to affixitsvalue,
and return the valuation thereof to the board of coun-
cil. If the assessor should not be satisfiedwith the
valne fixedby the tax-payer, he may callupon two or
more persons togive theirestimateof the value there-
of, who shallbe sworn by said assessor; and from all
the evidence, and his own knowledge , said assessor
shall affixthe true value . Said listshall embrace all
thepropertyinsaidcitynow subjecttotaxationunder
generallaws of the State,except farmingand unim-
proved lands,and landsused for farming purposes,

304 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and lands used for interment of the dead , and lands
not benefitedby being within the city, shall not be
subject to taxation for city revenue. If any person
failor refuse to give a listof his taxable property
when calledupon or notifiedfor thatpurpose by the
assessor, or give a falseor fraudulentlist, as required
by law ,he shallbe so reported by the assessorto the
mayor ,who shallissuea warrant therefor, and , upon
convictionthereof,the said delinquent shall be fined
not exceedingtwenty dollars,and be subjectto the pay-
ment of twice the amount of the tax upon his estate
failedor refusedto be listedor falselyor fraudulently
listed .
§ 3. The assessorat the time,or before he returns
histax-book , shallalsoreturn the names of alldelin-
quents describedin the precedingsectionto the mayor ,
and shall,as to fraudulent delinquents,state in what
the fraud or falsehood consists .
§ 4. The mayor shall issue a summons , in which
shall be stated the offense,in general terms, against
each of the delinquents returned by the assessor, re-
turnableto the next term of the mayor's court,which
shall hear and determine the case . The defendant
shall have the right to have a jury composed of six
housekeepers to try the facts,ifdemanded ,beforethe
trialis begun ; said jury shallbe summoned by the
marshal. If the defendant be found guilty, the court
shall enter judgment for the fine,and double tax as
aforesaid and costs . The court shall fix the value of
the taxableproperty upon which to impose the double
tax upon the statement of the defendant made upon
oath,or upon suchotherevidenceasitmay be enabled
toobtain,and executionshallissueforthefine,double
tax and costs. The fineand tax shall be certifiedby
the mayor to the council, and accounted for by the
collectingofficeras other publicmoneys .
§5 . The mayor's court, before a judgment is ren-
dered againsta delinquent, may , if itis satisfiedthat
the delinquentwas not willfullyin default, take the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 305
listof taxable property of such delinquent in the
manner prescribedby law . The listaforesaidshall
forthwith be certifiedto the council, charged to the
collector and collected as other taxes . In such cases
the mayor's court may excuse the delinquent from
the payment of the fineand double tax upon payment
of the costsof the prosecution.
§ 6. It shall be the duty of the city attorney to
prosecute under the preceding section, and if he does
so,shallbe allowed thirtyper cent.of the fineforhis
services .
§ 7. When it shall be known to the marshal that
any person has failedto give in a listof his taxable
property in any year when it shallbe liableto taxa-
tion,he shallreportsuch person to the mayor to be
dealt with, fined and taxed as delinquents reported
by the assessor. No marshal or assessor shall be
liableto cost in proceedings against delinquentsre-
ported by them .
§ 8. Any person who has failedto give in his listof
taxable property because he was not called upon by
the assessormay , after the assessorhas returned his
tax-book , listthe same with the city clerk at any
time, who ,on taking the same , shallbe governed by
the law regulatingthe duty of the assessor.
§ 9. The assessor shall make out his tax-book in fair
legiblehandwriting, in alphabeticalorder, add the
amount of value of each column , the aggregate
thereof, and prove itsaccuracy before he returnsthe
same .
§ 10. The assessorshall,for his services, receivea
compensation,tobe fixedand regulatedby the coun-
cil .
ARTICLE VIII .
Levy and Collectionof City Taxes .
§ 1. The board of councilshallhave power to levya
poll-tax of not exceeding two dollarsupon each male
20- LOCAL LAWS , VOL . 3 .

306 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
adult inhabitantof the city,and an ad valorem tax of
not more than one dollar upon each one hundred dol-
lars' worth of taxableestatein saidcity. Beyond the
amount of one dollar on each one hundred dollars of
taxable estate in said city, no other tax shall be levied
until an election, to be held for that purpose ,under
the order of the board of council, and after three
weeks ' noticeposted in three or more public placesin
saidcity, or published forthat length of time in one
of the newspapers of saidcity, specifying the object
of the tax, and a majority of the qualifiedvoters of
saidcityvoting,shallvote in favor of the levy.
§ 2. All taxes hereafterleviedin said cityshall be
due and owing from the tax payers in threeequal in-
stallments, as follows: The firston the firstMonday
in May of each year,the second on the firstMonday
inJuly,the thirdon the firstMonday in September
in each year. Persons paying the whole amount of
their city taxes at or before the maturity the first
installmentshall receivea creditof fiveper centum
thereof. On all installments unpaid at maturity
thereof six per centum thereof shall be added as a
penalty, and shall be collectedfrom the tax payers
failingto pay installmentsas they mature in same
manner as the taxes. The penaltiesfor failingto pay
installments of taxes shall be computed on the
amount of taxes due and penaltiesincurred hereto-
fore, if any ,and said penaltiesas they accrue shall
be charged by the treasurer on each tax-payer's
statement of tax. All the taxes and penaltiesdue
on or beforethe firstMonday in September of each
year shallbe paid to the treasurerof the city,who
shallgive not less than ten days' notice,by written
or printed posters, or by publicationin a newspaper
publishedinsaid city,of the time and placein said
cityhe shallattendto receiveand receiptfor said
payments. Alltaxesand penaltiesremainingunpaid
afterfirstMonday in September of each year shall
be placed in the hands of the citymarshal for col

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 307
lection, who shall receipttherefor to the board of
council,and the name ,and amount allowed him for
collectingsame , shall be collectedas the taxes are
collected; and said taxes and penalties shall be due
from him in installmentsof third thereof each thirty
days thereafter,and at the end of each thirtydays he
shall settlefor the amounts collectedby him before
the maturity of each installment,and account there-
for to, and pay over as and at such time as the board
of council may designate. For failureof the mar-
shal to collect,settlefor or pay over the taxes and
penaltiesas due from him ,he and his suretiesshall
be responsiblethereforon his officialbond .
§ 3. The board of council shall have a lien on all
the realand personalestatein said cityliablefor
taxes until all the taxes due by the owners thereof
shall be paid ; the taxes shall be collectedby the
marshal afterfirstMonday of September in each
year ; the marshal shallhave the same power to levy
and distrain and sellfor taxes, and the penalties
thereon,and amounts allowed him forcollectingsame ,
and costs of sale, the personal and real property of
any person,whose taxes remain unpaid,which sheriffs
of thisCommonwealth now have . Itshallbe the duty
of the marshal to expose the same to saleon the day
appointed, or so much of the same as may be neces-
sary to pay the tax or taxes,and penaltiesand costs
due on them respectively, to the highest bidder for
cash ; the saleshall take place at such times in the
day , and at such placesas in the case of salesof like
property by the sherifffor taxes; and ifrealestate
be sold, he shall convey it by deed duly acknowl-
edged to the purchaser,which shallpass the titleto
the purchaser as in likecasesof deeds by a sheriff:
Provided ,however, That all real estatethus sold for
taxes owing said citymay be redeemed at any time
within fiveyears by the originalowner or owners, or
their assignees,by paying the purchaser or his heirs
or assigneeshis purchase money , with interestat the

308 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
rateof thirtyper cent.per annum on the sum paid,
and all costsof said sale: And provided also, That
infants, femes covert, and persons of unsound mind ,
shall have two years after their several disabilitiesare
removed to redeem their property. The board of
council may cause property sold for taxes to be bid
in for the city,which shall have the same right as
individualsin becoming purchaser at saidsales.
§ 4. The taxes collectedupon the taxable property
in each ward of the city,and licensecollectedin wards
one and four,shallbe expended by the board of coun-
cilon the public improvements of and forthe benefit
of the ward from which collected, except such ex-
penditures as may be necessaryfor the protectionof
the riverbanks ,shallbe paid for out of the general
fund raisedfrom the taxes laidupon allthe taxable
property of the city. The levy now made and uncol-
lected shall be collected and expended as heretofore.
The money collectedinwards number two and three for
licenseand as taxes,shallbe expended in said wards
numbers two and three,except as provided herein for
expendituresupon the riverbank .
ARTICLE IX .
Treasurer .
§1 . The board of council shall have power to ap-
point a treasurerat same time assessoris appointed,
or as soon thereafteras possible, who shallbe a resi-
dent of the city,and a qualifiedvoter thereof, and
who , before entering upon the duties of his office,
shalltake an oath to faithfullyperform the same ,and
shall give bond for such an amount and with such se-
curitiesas the board of councilshall approve ,condi-
tioned to pay over and account forall moneys which
shall come into his hands while in office, and for the
prompt and faithfuldischarge of allduties imposed
upon him as treasurerof said city. It shall be his
duty to receiveall moneys of the cityderived from

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 309
taxes, license, wharfage , finesor other sources; but
shallnot pay out or expend the same in anywise ex-
cept upon the order of the board of council,signedby
itspresident,or upon a certifiedcopy of the order
allowing the same , attestedby the clerk, which order
shallspecifyfor what purpose the same isto be paid.
He shall keep a carefuland correctaccount thereof,
and make an itemized statement of all receipts and
expenditures for the board of council,which account
shall always be opened for their inspection, or of any
tax-payer of said city; and he shall furnish said
board an attestedcopy thereof,which they shallpub-
lish,by writtenor printedposters, or in a newspaper
published in saidcity,forten days beforethe fifteenth
of November of each year.
§2 . The treasurer shall receive for his services a
reasonablecompensation,to be allowed by the board
of council, and may be removed at any time upon
proper noticeforgood cause shown . If forany cause
the officeof treasurershallbecome vacant by removal,
resignationor otherwise, the board of council may
appoint another .
ARTICLE X.
City Clerk.
§1 . The board of council,at time of appointment
of treasurer, or as soon thereafteras possible, shall
have power to appoint a cityclerk, who shallkeep , in
a fairand legiblehand ,a true record of allthe pro-
ceedings of the board,in a book kept forthatpurpose,
which shallbe signed by the presidentof the board ;
and saidclerk shallalso register,in a separate book ,
and in a fairhand , all the ordinances and by -laws
which may be adopted by the board of council; and
shall also make out tax-books as and when required
by the board of council. Said clerk may , for good
cause, be removed at the pleasure of the board. The
board of councilshallallowhim a reasonablecompen-

CHAPTER 1321 .
AN ACT toprevent the destructionof fishin South Elkhorn,lying
within the borders of Woodford county .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That hereafterit shall not be lawful for any
personorpersonstocatchor destroyany fishin that
portion of South Elkhorn which lieswithin the bor-
ders of Woodford county, Kentucky ,by means of a
seine,set-net, dip-net, trap, or liquids, berries, dyna-
mite, medicine ; and any one thus offending shall be
finedin a sum not lessthan twenty dollarsand not
exceeding one hundred dollarsfor each offense, to be
recovered by a warrant in the name of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky beforethe county judge or a jus-
ticeof the peace of said county, or by indictment of
a grand jury of Woodford county ; and in case of

failuretopay such fineand the costsof prosecution,
he shallbe confinedat hard laborin the jailof Wood-
ford county ; but the period of such confinement, for
the non -payment of the fine,shallnot exceed one day
for each one dollar of the fine.
§ 2. That this act shall take effect from and after
its approval .
Approved May 9, 1890.

not exceedingsixper cent.interestfrom date, payable

able as said company may direct, not exceeding forty
years from date, and secure the payment of principal
and interestthereofby mortgage or deed of trust,
conveying said railway and other property and fran-
chisesof saidcorporationto a trusteeor trustees, and
from time to time fillvacancies that may occur in the
officeof trustees,with such covenantsand stipulations
as may be necessaryto effectthe purpose and object
of its execution .
§ 9. The directorsof said company may make con-
tractswith any corporation, company , individual or
individualsfor constructingand equipping said rail-
road and branches,or any part thereof, and pay for
the same wholly or partiallyin the stock, bonds or
other property of said company , in such manner and
upon such conditions as may deemed for the inter-
ests of said company .
§ 10. Said company shallhave power and authority
to erect bridgesover any stream acrosswhich the line
of itsroad may run,but not so as to obstructnaviga-
tion of such stream ; said company shall have the
power and authorityto cross public roads, turnpike
roads and railroads, and shall also have power and
authorityto take and receivesubscriptionto itscap-
ital stock in lands lying in the counties through
which saidrailroadmay be located; and may receive
conveyance to itselfof such lands,and hold,use and
dispose of the same at pleasure; and may alsoreceive
subscriptionsto its capitalstock in timber or other

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 327
material to be used in the buildingor equipping said
railroad: Provided , That alllands acquired not nec-
essary forthe constructionand operationof the road,
shallbe disposed of within fiveyearsaftertheiracqui-
sition .
§ 11. The board of directorsshallprovide in the by-
laws for the annual election of the board of directors,
consistingof not lessthan seven nor more than thir-
teen in number ,who may be stockholdersof saidcom-
pany ,and fixthe time and determine the manner of
the firstand succeeding electionsof directors. The
directorsshallseverallyhold theirofficesforone year,
and until their successorsare elected and qualified.
The board of directorsmay fillany vacancy occurring
in their number ,and shall choose from their number a
president. It shall requirea majority,including the
president, to constitutea quorum of the board forthe
transaction of business . At the election of directors,
and all other meetings of the stockholders in said com-
pany , stockholders shall be entitledto one vote for
each share of stock so held, which may be castin per-
son or by writtenproxy . All stockholders'meetings
and meetings of the board of directors, and allelec-
tions of officersby the board of directors, shall be
held at Hardinsburg , Kentucky .
§12. It shallbe lawful for the presidentand direct-
ors of said company to open stock-books for the sub-
scriptionto the capitalstock,at such time and place,
and continue them open such length of time as may
be deemed necessary. The capitalstock of saidcom-
pany ishereby declared to be personal property, and
may be issued,certified,transferredand registeredin
such manner and at such place as may be ordered and
provided by the board of directorsof saidcompany .
§ 13. The board of directorsof said company shall
have power to establishsuch ratesof tollfor the con-
veyance of freightor persons on their said railroad
and branches, not exceeding the rates that may be
establishedby generallaw regulatingthe chargesfor
freight and passage on railroadsin thisState.

328 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 14. The constructionof said railroad may be
begun at CeciliaStationor at Hardinsburg , eitheror
both,at the option and in the discretionof the corpo-
rators .
§ 15. The individualproperty of the stockholders
in saidrailwayshallnot be liableforthe debts or lia-
bilitiesof the saidcorporation.
§ 16. That said company shallbegin work within
two years from date hereof,and complete same within
fiveyears from the passage of this act,or otherwise
this charter shall stand as forfeited .
§17. This act shall take effectand be in force from
and afteritspassage.
Approved May 9, 1890 .

CHAPTER 1340 .
AN ACT toprovidefor a registrationof votersin thecityof Owens-
boro .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of theCom-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the common councilof the cityof Owens-
boro shall, at their firstregular meeting in June of
each year ,electfour sober and discreet citizensin each
electionprecinctof the cityof Owensboro , two to act
as judgesof registration,one asclerkof registration,
and one assheriffof registration,intheprecinctsfor

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 337
which they are appointed. No persons shall be eli-
gible as judge,clerkor sheriffwho is not a voter in
the precinctforwhich he isappointed,and a house-
keeper ; and when thereare two distinctpoliticalpar-
ties,said officersof registrationshallbe so selectedas
that one of the judges at each precinctshallbe of one
politicalparty and the other of the opposing political
party . They shallservefor one year from the time of
theirappointment ,and each officerof registration,be-
fore enteringupon the discharge of his duties, shall
take the oath prescribedby sectionone,articleeight,
of the Constitution, and shall, during the period of
his term of service,be excused from jury duty .
§2 . The council clerk shall,at leastfivedays be-
forethe generalregistrationin each year, as provided
for in thisact, give each officerof registrationwritten
noticeof his appointment . Should the councilfailto
elect,or should allor any of the officerselected fail
to attend at the place of registrationfor one hour
afterthe time forcommencing the registration,or re-
fuse to act, then the mayor of the cityof Owensboro
shallappoint officersto act in theirstead forthat reg-
istration, and may administer the necessaryoaths of
office .
§ 3. Officers of registration shall have the same
power topreserveorder atthe placesof registrationas
isexercisedby officersof electionsat the polls. If the
officersof registrationentertainany doubt as to
whether or not any person offeringfor registrationis
entitled to such registration,or if any one'sright to
registeris challenged, citizensmay be calledin,not
exceeding three in number , who shall be examined
touching the qualificationsof such person or persons
who offerto register.
Forms .
§ 4. It shall be the duty of the clerk of the council
to prepare the proper forms, and cause to be printed
22 - LOCAL LAWS , VOL . 3 .

338 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
two registration-books foreach precinctinthecityof
Owensboro , one of which shallbe kept in hisoffice,to
be used as provided in sectionseven of this act,and
the other furnishedto the clerks of registrationand
electionbeforeeach registrationand electionday , as
hereinafterprovided. Said books shall be so ar-
ranged as that the names of voters may be registered
alphabetically,and shallbe ruled and headed as fol-
lows :
NAME .
Residence
SpecialElections
DecemberElection.
AugustElection
Color
Remarks .
General Registration .
§ 5. The officers of registration shall attend the
voting places of their respective precincts on the first
Monday in July in each year,and on the two next
succeeding days ,from the hours of six in the morning
until seven in the evening,and shall record in the
registrationbook , which shall be furnished by the
clerk of the county court to each registrationclerk, a
listof such qualifiedvoters of the precinct as may
apply for registration. If eitherof saiddays shall
fallupon the fourth day of the month , then said
fourthday shall be omitted as a day of registration,
and the registrationshall proceed on the next suc-
ceeding day ,as ifsaidfourth day had not intervened.
Said listof votersshallbe in alphabeticalorder,and
shallshow the name of the person registered, the
name of the streetor alley, and the number , if any ,
of the house, lodging or tenement in which he lived,
and whether he be white or colored ; and if said
house, lodging or tenement be not numbered , the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 339
locationthereof shallbe described in the registration
book as accuratelyas is practicable,giving the street
and between what streets. No person shall be reg-
isteredwho does not personally appear before the
officersof registration; and if he be not personally
known to one of the officers, or if any by -stander
shalldemand it, he shall be sworn by one of the offi-
cers,and interrogatedby him or by such by -stander,
touching his qualificationsas provided in this act.
Opposite the name of each person so sworn the clerk
shallwrite the word " Sworn ," which entry shallbe
prima facie evidence of such swearing in any prose-
cutionunder thisact. Said registrationin July shall
be known as the general registration, and any person
then registeredmay vote at allelectionsuntilthe next
general registration, unless he becomes disqualified
afterregistration.
Qualifications.
§ 6. Every person shallbe entitledto be registered
who would be entitled to vote at the next succeeding
August election- that is to say, every male citizen
who , on that day , shall have attained the age of
twenty-one years, and shallhave resided in the State
two years, or county one year,or in the cityone year,
and in the precinctin which he offersto registersixty
days. No person shall be registeredwho does not
personallyappear before the officersof registration,
and if he be not personallyknown to one of the offi-
cers,or if any by -standershalldemand it,he shallbe
sworn by one of the officersand interrogatedby him ,
or by such by -stander, touching his qualifications, as
provided in this section. Opposite the name of each
person so sworn the clerk will write the word
" Sworn ," which entry shallbe prima facie evidence
of such swearing in any prosecutionunder thisact.
Aliens .
§ 7. Any alienpossessing allthe qualificationsof a
voter named in sectionsix of thisact, except citizen

340 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
ship,shall be entitledto be registered; but the clerk
shallwrite oppositehis name ,in the column headed
" Remarks ," the words " Not naturalized," and such
alienwill not be entitledto vote at any electionheld
under that registration, unlesshe shallproduce to the
officersof electionhis naturalizationpapers in due
form of law .
Mode of Registering.
§ 8. In making the registrationthe clerk shall act
as the recording officer, and the judges shall decide
all questions relatingto the qualificationsof persons
offeringto be registered,except that, in case of a dif-
ferenceof opinion between the judges, the clerkshall
have the casting vote. It shall be the duty of the
clerk to number , consecutively, the names recorded
under each letterof the alphabet as they are taken
down , and ,at the close of the registration, he shall
sign his name as clerk afterthe lastname recorded
under each letter, as aforesaid,in such manner as that
no more names can be recorded above his,and shall
footup , and certify, in the back of the registration
book , the whole number of names recorded at that
registration,and this certificateshallbe signed by all
the officersof registrationbeforeleavingthe place of
registration,and in the presenceof by -standers,any
two of whom may ,if they desire,sign theirnames as
witnesses thereto. In addition to the requirements
above, the clerk shallsignhis name as such clerk at
the footof each page .
Return and Custody of Books .
§ 9. On the day followingthe close of the registra-
tion,the clerkshalldeliverthe registrationbook into
the hands of the clerk of the council, and shall take
his receipttherefor. Itshallbe theduty of saidclerk
to keep saidbooks safelyinhis office, and not to suf-
ferthe same to be taken therefrom,except as pro-
vided in this act . He shall also cause to be made one

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 341
copy of each registrationbook , in the blank -book re-
tainedby him ,as provided in sectionfour of thisact,
which shallbe kept in his office, and not be taken
therefrom for any purpose. In case of loss of any
registrationbook , a copy shallbe made by the clerk
of the council from the copy retained in his office,
which copy shallbe used in registrationsand elections
with the same effectas the original. The clerk shall
permit any citizen,at any time,to copy any registra-
tionbook , without fee or charge ; and he shall also
furnishcopiesatthe same rateas provided in section
twenty of this act for copying the book kept in his
office. Copies furnishedto citizensunder thissection
shallbe paid for in cash by the person orderingthem .
Removals .
§ 10. Any person removing from one precinct to
another, afterhaving been registeredfor that year,
may apply to the clerkof registrationof the precinct
from which he removes, on a registrationday , and
have his name canceledoffby writing opposite it the
word " Removed ," and thereupon said clerk shall
givehim a certificatein the followingform :
-
This is to certify that
precinct,
has been canceled .
[Signed]
has removed from the
ward ,and that hisregistration
-, Clerk .
Upon presenting said certificateto the officersof
registrationof the precinctto which he has removed ,
he shall be entitledto be there registered,if he pos-
sesses the other qualificationsnamed in section six of
thisact ; and no person so removing, after being reg-
istered,shallbe registeredin another precinctwithout
the production of such a certificate.
Striking off Names .
§ 11. Any voter may ,by giving fivedays ' noticein
writingto any person whose name has been registered,
move the citycouncilat itsnext regular meeting, not

342 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
lessthan fivedays after serviceof said notice, to
strikehis name from the register; and both parties
may introduce witnesses, not exceeding two in num-
ber, on each side. Said noticemust be served per-
sonally. If, at the hearing, the council shall be of
the opinion that the person registeredis not lawfully
entitledto register, it shalldirectthe clerk to strike
his name from the register, by insertingopposite it
the words " Strickenoffby order of the citycouncil."
If the person upon whom notice is attempted to be
served can not be found,the clerk shallwriteoppo-
sitesuch name , on the registrationbook , the word
" Doubtful," and when , at an election, such person,
whose name is marked " Doubtful ," shall offer to
vote,he shallbe sworn , and his right to vote investi-
gated fully.
§ 12. On the Saturday previous to the firstMonday
in August , one thousand eight hundred and ninety ,
and on the juridicalday previous to every electionto
be held , or vote of the people to be taken thereafter
in the cityof Owensboro ,the clerk of the citycoun-
cil shall deliver to the clerks of election the registra-
tion books for their several precincts, together with
the poll-books ,which said registrationand poll-books
shallbe produced by said clerksat theirseveralpre-
cinctswhen the polls are opened on the day of elec-
tion . At said election no vote shall be received unless
the name of the person offering to vote is on the reg-
istryprovided in thisact.
False Registration .
§ 13. Any person who shall cause himself to be reg-
isteredin more than one electionprecinct, otherwise
than is provided in sectionten of this act, or more
than once in the same precinct, or who shall cause
himself to be registered, knowing that he isnot law-
fullyentitledto registration,and any person who shall
aid or abet in the commission of any of said acts,shall

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 343
be deemed guiltyof a misdemeanor ,and shallbe im-
prisoned in the county jailnot lessthan one nor more
than twelve months .
False Registration Books .
§ 14.Any officerof registration,or other person,who
shallunlawfully alterany registrationbook , or add
any name thereto,or who shallwillfullysecrete,sup-
press or destroy any such book ; or who shallmake ,
or aid in making , any falseor fraudulent registration
book , shallbe deemed guiltyof felony, and shallbe
confinedin the penitentiarynot lessthan one nor more
than fiveyears, and shallforfeitany officehe then
holds,and shall foreverbe disqualifiedfrom holding
office .
Perjury .
§ 15. Any person who shall willfullymake any
falsestatement,under an oath, lawfullyadministered
under thisact,shallbe deemed guiltyof perjury,and
confined in the penitentiarynot less than one nor
more than fiveyears .
Obstructing Registration .
§ 16. Any person who ,by himself or in aid of oth-
ers,shallforciblybreak up or attempt to break up a
registrationheld under thisact,or shallforciblypre-
vent or attempt to prevent any person from ap-
proaching or entering a place of registrationfor the
purpose of registering,shallbe finednot lessthan one
hundred nor more than five hundred dollars, or im-
prisoned not less than six nor more than twelve
months in the county jail, or both.
§ 17. No witness shall be excused from testifying
before any grand jury, or in any prosecution or in-
dictment under thisact,on the ground that his testi-
mony may criminatehimself; but no such testimony
shallbe used against him in any prosecution, except
forperjury ingivingthe same .

issueover one hundred and fiftythousand dollarsin
bonds under this act,and same may be issued from

no other purpose than as provided for in this act.
Said county court isalsoempowered to extend any of
the turnpikeswhich itmay obtaincontrolof by virtue
of thisact, and to build such new turnpikesas itmay

turnpikesas the county court may have the controll

ing interest, itmay electa board of directorsfrom the

roads .§7. In casesaidturnpikesarepurchasedor securedSupervisorof

if the vote is in favor of purchasing said turnpikes

jectof itsincorporation. Said stock shallbe divided
intoshares of fiftydollarseach,and shallbe deemed

pany shall be E. H. Baker , W. H. Riddell, J. R.

and G. G. Hughes ; and they shall hold their office

taryand treasurer,and requirebond and securityfrom

electionsfordirectorsby the stockholders,which shall

Provided , That each stockholder shall have one vote

foreach share of his paid-up stock. Each board ,after
the one herein appointed, shall consistof fivemem-
bers and organize as provided in case of the first
board, and with same power as to requiringbond and
surety. If thereat any time occurs a vacancy in any
board of directorsby death,resignationor otherwise,
the remaining members of the board shall fillthe
vacancy from the stockholders .
§ 4. For the purpose raising the capital stock,Subscription
the members of the firstboard of directors, whose
names are given in this act,or such of them as may
choose to act, are authorized to open books or papers
for subscriptionto said stock. The subscriptionmay
be as follows,to wit : " We ,whose names are hereto
subscribed, promise to pay the sum set opposite our
names tothe Burlington Hotel and Livery StableCom-
pany , and to that extent take stock in said company ,
to be paid when and as calledby the board of direct-
ors of the company .
Names . Number of shares . Amount-- ."
§ 5. That any subscriber to said stock who shall failPenalty.
to pay same and as called, may be sued by the com-
pany therefor, and recoveryhad by the company for
the sum calledand unpaid ,with interestfrom thedate
of the call,and costsin any court of the county hav-
ing jurisdictionof the amount claimed, as in other
civil actions .
§ 6. The saidcompany isauthorizedand empowered Powers.
to carry on in the town of Burlington,Kentucky , or
adjacentthereto,the hoteland liverystablebusiness;
and for the purpose of itssaid business itmay take
and hold,by conveyance infee:simple,or by leasefrom
time to time, such real and personal estate in or
adjacent to said town as may be necessaryfor that
purpose , and employ any and all hands or persons
required: Provided , That itshallnot acquireexceed-
ing ten acresof realestate. The said company may
erect allnecessarybuildings,structuresand fixtures,
on any such ground bought or leased by it,as is re-

364 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
quired to enable itto carry on itscorporate business,
and furnishand keep saidproperty in good repair.
§7. The entiremanagement of said company's af-
Management. fairs and business shall be vested in its board of di-
rectors,which shallmeet at leastfour times a year,at
times and places to be fixed by the by -laws, and at
such other specialtimes as the president or two mem-
bers may fix, of which they shall notifythe balance
of the board . The board shall have power to make
all necessaryrulesand by -laws forthe welfareof the

keep a recordof itsproceedings. The presidentshall

dent of the board by order of the board ,and the com-
pany shall be bound thereby, or such contract or
agreement may be signed by the presidentand direct-
ors. A majority of the board shallconstitutea quo-
rum to do business .

Approved May 9, 1890.

CHAPTER 1355 .
AN ACT to define the county line of Estillcounty .
WHEREAS , The county lineof Estillisnot well de-
fined,and persons livingnear the supposed boundary
line do not know in which county they reside nor
which to listtheirproperty fortaxation:
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the boundary line of Estillcounty shall
be as follows, viz: Beginning at the mouth of Red
river; thence up the same with itsmeanders to the
mouth of Plum creek ; thence up the same with its
meanders to itsextreme head ; thence a straightline
to the firstfalls in the Ballard branch , above John
Carmichael's house, where the water fallsover the
cliff; thence with said cliffand east course to the
Powell county line. The territoryon the north side
of the above describedline,from the mouth of Plum
creek to the Powell county line,shallbe included in
the county of Powell ; thence with the Powell county
line to Warren's Cabin , corner of Lee , Wolfe and
Powell counties;thence with the Lee county lineto
the Jackson county line; thence with the Jackson
county lineto the Madison county line; thence with
the Madison county line to the mouth of Drowning
creek ; thence down the Kentucky riverwith itsme-
anders to the beginning.
§2 . This act shall take effect from and after its
passage .
Approved May 9,1890..

CHAPTER 1363 .
AN ACT to prohibitthe saleand use of spirituous, vinous and malt
liquorsin Knott county .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That itshallbe unlawful for any person or per-
sons to sellany spirituous,vinous or malt liquorsin
the county of Knott , in any quantity whatever ; any
person violatingthe provisions of this act shallbe
deemed guiltyof a misdemeanor , and , upon an indict-
ment of a grand jury, or by warrant beforethe county
judge or any justiceof the peace, upon conviction
thereof shall be fined one hundred dollars .
§ 2. That any county or policejudge or magistrate
of Knott county who , from his own personalknowl-
edge,or from information, has cause to believethat
the provisionsof this act have been violated, shall
forthwith issue a warrant for the arrest and trial of
such offender, and forthwith place the same in the
hands of some ministerial officer to be executed .
§ 3. That it shall be the duty of any ministerial
officerreceiving such warrant to forthwith arrest such

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 377
offender, and ,if need be,he may summon the power
of the county to assisthim in making such arrest.
§ 4. That any officernamed inthisact,whoshall fail
or refuseto discharge his duty as herein prescribed,
shallbe deemed guiltyof a misdemeanor,and,upon
indictment and conviction, be fined one hundred dol-
lars for each offense .
§ 5. Itshallbe the duty of the circuitjudge of the
county to give thisact in charge to the grand jury at

CHAPTER 1386 .
AN ACT to change theboundary lineof thetown ofBeattyvilleand
increasethe licenseof venders of spirituous,vinousor malt liquors,
and so forth .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the boundary line of the town of Beatty-
villeshallbe as follows,viz: Beginning at the mouth
of Miery branch,thence up said branch to the resi-
dence of Joseph Strong ; thence in a straightlineto
the Price house, where J. B. Cole now resides, near
SilverCreek ;thence in a straightlineto big rock that
stands on the east bank of Crystal creek ; thence in
a straightlineto the " North Fork " of the Kentucky
river, at the east end of the railroadgrade ; thence
down the riverto the beginning.
§ 2. That the tax on eachvender of spirituous,vin-
ous or malt liquorsshallbe two hundred dollarsper
annum . That all acts and parts of acts in conflict
with thisactare hereby repealed.
§ 3. This act shall take effectand be in force from
and afteritspassage.
Approved May 10,1890.
CHAPTER 1388 .
AN ACT declaring a certain order of the Larue County Court valid
and binding .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the orderof the Larue County Court,made
in January ,one thousand eight hundred and ninety,
confirming the settlement of the executors of John
Petty,deceased, shallnot be held invalidforor be-
cause of any irregularityin the same ; but the same

396 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
shallbe as valid,binding and conclusive upon allper-
sons,and upon allmattersinvolvedin saidsettlement,
as would be the judgment of any court of general
equity jurisdiction, and shall only be liableto im-
peachment or vacation,in the same manner and for
same causes as judgments of courtsgenerallyare.
§ 2. This actshalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved May 10, 1890.

CHAPTER 1389 .

WHEREAS , The necessity exists for a railroad
through tunnels in the several mountain ridges on the
border line of Virginia and Kentucky , whereby the
distance between the seaboard and the interior can be
materiallyshortenedforthe various railroadsnow in
operation,or in process of construction, on the east
and west sidesof said ranges; and whereas, the great
cost of constructinga railroad through the tunnels
aforesaidpreventsitsbeing done by or forthe soleuse
of any one of said railways; and whereas ,the object
of this charter is to secure the construction of said
tunnel railroad, so that allrailway companies may use
the same on like terms ; therefore,
Be it enactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :

Boyle, J. W. Gaulbert, A. P. Humphrey ,John E.
Green ,George M. Davie, J. Stoddard Johnston and
Arthur Cary,be,and they are hereby,appointed com-
missioners, under the directionof any two of whom
subscriptionsmay be received to the capitalstock of
the InterstateTunnel Railway Company hereby in-
corporated; and they may cause books to be opened
at such times and places as they may directfor the

CHAPTER 1392 .
AN ACT defining the rights and powers of the county of Kenton in
turnpike road companies in Kenton county , to the constructionof
which roads said county has contributed her money .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Kenton county shall be entitledto one
share of stock in each turnpike road company in said
county for every fiftydollarswhich said county has
heretofore subscribed to the capital stock thereof, in-
cluding the amount subscribedby saidcounty for the
building of bridges on said turnpike road, or for
bridges used on said road .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 411
§ 2. That at the annual elections of the officersof
such turnpike road company , as mentioned in the
firstsectionof thisact,the saidcounty shallhave the
rightto castone vote for such officerfor each share of
stock to which she is entitled, as provided in the first
section of this act .
§ 3. That it is hereby made the duty of the treas-
urer of the board of commissioners of Kenton county
to cast the vote of said county at said annual elections.
§ 4. That this act shall take effect from and after its
passage .
Approved May 10,1890.
CHAPTER 1393 .
AN ACT to cede toUnited Statesjurisdictionover a site for a public
buildingin the cityofRichmond ,Kentucky .
WHEREAS , There is now pending in the United
States Congress "A billfor the erectionof a public
buildingat Richmond ,Kentucky ," which willproba-
bly become a law ; and whereas, no part of the sum
provided for by said bill for the erectionof said
buildingcan be expended untilthe Stateof Kentucky
shallhave ceded to the United Statesexclusivejuris-
diction over the same during the time the United
States shall be or remain the owner thereof, for all
purposes except the administrationof the criminal
laws of this State, and the serviceof civilprocess
thereon ; therefore,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the State of Kentucky does hereby cede
to the United States exclusivejurisdictionover any
site for a public building in Richmond , Kentucky ,
not exceeding two acres, the title to which may
hereafter become vested in the United States, dur

412 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
ing the time the United States shall be or remain
the owner thereof, forallpurposes except the admin-
istration of the criminal laws of the State and the ser-
viceof civilprocessthereon.
§ 2. This act shall take effect and be in full force
from and afterits passage .
Approved May 10, 1890.

CHAPTER 1405 .
AN ACT toincorporatetheAmerican-German Bank ,at Paducah.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of theCom-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That there ishereby created and established,in
the cityof Paducah , a body -corporateand politic,by
the name and styleof "American -German Bank ," the
stockholders of which , their successorsand assigns,
shall have perpetual succession; and by that name
may sue and be sued ; make contracts,with or without
seal; may have and use a common seal, and alterthe
same at pleasure. It may purchase, hold, use, sell
and convey such real and personal property as may
be necessaryto carry out the legitimatepurposes of
itscreation,and that may ,in the usual course of busi-
ness, be taken or purchased in satisfactionof any
debt due to said bank ; but real estatetaken in pay-
ment of debts must be disposed of within fiveyears.
§ 2. The affairsof saidbank shallbe controlledby
a board of not less than five nor more than nine di-
rectors, to be electedas hereinafterprovided. Said
board of directorsshall annually electone of their
number president; and they may alsoelector employ
a vice-president, cashier, teller, and such other offi-
cers,agents and servantsastheirbusinessmay require,
and prescribetheirdutiesand fixtheircompensation .
They may adopt by -laws,rulesand regulationsfor the
government of said bank and itsbusiness,and have
such other powers and perform such other duties as
are common to boards of directorsof likeinstitutions,
not inconsistent with this charter or the laws of the
country. No person shall be qualifiedto act as di-
rector unless he be a resident of this Commonwealth
and the bona fide owner in his own right of at least
five shares of the capitalstock of said bank . The
saidboard of directorsmay requirebond and security

430 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
of itspresidentand other officers; and the said presi-
dent,directorsand other officersshall,beforeentering
upon the discharge of their duties, take an oath to
faithfullyand honestly discharge the same . The
board of directorsshallkeep a record of all their
meetings , proceedings and orders . Vacancies in the
board shall be filledby a vote of those remaining in
office. The stockholders,at any regularmeeting,may
change the time of electing directors; but said elec-
tions shall not be held at longer intervalsthan one
Jear .
§ 3. The capitalstock of saidbank shall not be less
than one hundred thousand ($100,000)dollarsnor more
than fivehundred thousand ($500,000) dollars,to be
divided into shares of the par value of one hundred
($100) dollarseach, and may be transferredin such
manner as may be provided by the board of directors.
§ 4. The followingpersons,namely ,T. H. Puryear ,
T. J.Atkins,G. M. Oehlschlaeger,George Rock ,E. W.
Vaughan , Henry Burnett and George C. Thompson ,
orany threeor more of them ,arehereby authorizedand
empowered ,at such timesand placesas they may agree
upon , to open books for and receivesubscriptionsto
the capitalstockof saidbank ;and when one thousand
(1000) shares or more shall be subscribed, and fifty
(50) percent.ofsame shallbe paidin,thesubscribers
may meet and electa board of directors, who shall
serveuntilthe firstTuesday in January thereafter,
and until their successorsare qualified; and on the
firstTuesday in every January thereafterthe stock-
holdersof said bank,eitherin personor by proxy,
shall elect directors, who shall continue in officefor
one year,and until their successorsare elected and
qualified. Specialmeetings of the stockholdersmay
be calledby thedirectorswhen they deem itadvisable,
or by any number of stockholders representingone-
third of the stock. In all meetings each stockholder
shall have one vote for each share of stock he or she
may own ; but in no caseshallany stockholderhave a

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 431
vote who has made defaultin the payment of any call
that has been made upon his subscriptionof stock.
The firstmeeting of stockholdersherein provided for
may designatethe time and manner inwhich subscrip.
tionsof stock are to be paid.
§ 5. The business of said American -German Bank
shallbe, and itshallhave the right, to receivemoney
and currency on deposit,upon such terms as may be
agreed upon ; to loan money upon such securityas
may be taken ; to discount,buy and sellgold and sil-
ver, stocks, bonds ,billsof exchange ,and other evi-
dences of debt ; to make advances on approved se-
curities,and upon the agriculturaland other products
of the country ; to receivebonds , stocks, warehouse
receipts, and written evidence of the ownership of
produce and merchandise in pledge for the payment
of money , advances,or debts owing to it, and to do a
general banking business. Infants and married wo-
men may depositinsaidbank in theirown name , and
theirchecks or receiptsshallbe valid.
§ 6. All billsof exchange , and promissory notes
made negotiable and payable to said bank , and all
billsof exchange and promissory notes made negotia-
ble and payable at any bank chartered by this Com .
monwealth , or that may be organized under the laws
of the United States, and discounted by or sold to
said American -German Bank ,shall be placed on the
footingof foreignbillsof exchange ,and remedy had ,
jointlyand severally, againstthe principals, securi-
ties,drawers, acceptorsand indorsers,and any one or
more of them , except that in no caseshalldamages be
had as on a foreignbillof exchange .
§ 7. In any case where said bank may receivecol-
lateralsfor the securityof any debt owing to it, the
person pledging such collateralsand the bank may
enter into a written agreement , to the effectthat if
such debt isnot paid at maturity,or by a specified
time thereafter,the said bank shall have the right to
selland pass the titleto any collateralso pledged at

432 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
such time,place and manner ,and upon such terms as
may be specifiedin said written agreement , and to
apply the proceedsto the payment of the debt for
which the pledge was made . But in no case shall
such sale be made until the owner of such collateral
shallhave had at leasttwenty days' notice mailed to
him ,nor untilsuch noticeshallhave been published by
insertionsin some paper published inthecityofPadu-
cah . And in no case of such sale shall said bank be
held responsible, except forhonest and fairconduct,
and a proper appropriationof the proceeds of such
sale .
§8. The saidbank may ,from time to time, declare
dividends of itsprofitsin such amounts as the board
of directors may determine, which shallbe paid or
passed to the creditof the share-holdersin proportion
to the stock held by them respectively. But the board
of directorsshall, before each dividend is declared,
setapart not lessthan fiveper cent. of the net profits
as a surplus fund, untilsuch surplus fund shall
amount to at leastten per cent.of the whole capital
stock .
§ 9. The saidbank may purchase,leaseor provide a
buildingor premises containinga strong and commo-
dious safety vault, or constructthereinsuch vault,
which shallhave a number of smallcompartments or
boxes designedor adapted forrentingtopersonsor
corporationswho desireto have a placeofsafedeposits
forvaluables; and the bank ishereby empowered to
rent out the boxes or compartments in such safety
vaultto such persons and at such price and forsuch
time as may be agreed between the bank and such
persons as may jointlyor severallyrent one or more
of such boxes or compartments .
§ 10. This act shall be in force from and after its
passage, and continue inforceforthirtyyears.
Approved May 10, 1890.

CHAPTER 1419 .
AN ACT for the appointment of officialreporters for the Fourth Judi-
cialDistrict,composed of the counties of Daveiss,McLean ,Ohio and
Hancock ,and for the preservationof evidence in certaincases.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the judge of the fourth judicialdistrict,
composed of the counties of Daveiss, McLean , Ohio
and Hancock , is hereby, authorized to appoint as
many stenographic reportersfor said districtas he
may deem necessary,which reportersshallbe skilled
in theirprofession, and shallhold their officesat the
pleasureof thesaidjudge of thedistrictforwhich they
are appointed,and beforeentering upon the duties of
their office,shall take an oath for the faithfulper-
formance thereof .
§ 2. When saidappointment shallhave been made ,
the judge aforesaidshall,by an order entered upon
the order-book of his court, fix the fees of said official
reportersfor attendance in court and taking short-
hand notes and making transcriptsof the same ,and
for taking depositions in short-hand , and for such
other servicesas they may be requiredto render ;and
he shallhave power ,from time to time, in his discre-
tion, to make such changes in the schedule of fees as
he may deem proper.
§ 3. Upon the trialof any civilactionin any of the
courts of saiddistrict,if eitherparty to the suitshall
requesttheservicesof saidofficialreporters,the pre-
siding judge may , in his discretion, order a fullre-
port of the testimony; in which case it shall be the
duty of said officialreporter to cause full sten-
ographic notesof the evidenceto be taken,and , upon
requestof eitherparty to the suit, to cause a fulland
accurate transcriptof the same to be made , which
transcriptshallbe filedamong the papers of the case,
and constitute a part of the record . The fees of said
reporterforsaidservicesshallbe paid for as soon as

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 465
rendered by the party or partiesatwhose instancethe
same were ordered, and such fees shall be taxed as
part of the costsof the party paying the same .
§ 4. Upon the trialof any criminal cases in the
fourth judicialdistrict,except in McLean county , if
the Commonwealth attorney or the accused shallre-
quest the servicesof saidofficialreporters, the presid-
ing judge may ,in his discretion, order a fullreportof
the evidence, and transcriptthereof, in which case it
shallbe the duty of the officialreportersto cause full
short -hand notes of the evidence to be taken , and to
causea fulland accuratetranscriptof the same to be
made , and filedamong the papers as part of the
recordof the case. The feesof saidreportersfor tak-
ing and transcribingthe testimony in such casesshall
be paid forby the county in which they are rendered.
§ 5. The testimony of any witnessestaken and filed,
as hereinbeforeprovided, may be used in any subse-
quent trialof the same action in allcases where such
testimonywould be admissibleifembodied in a billof
exceptions. A report of the testimony in any action
taken and filed, as hereinbeforeprovided,may ,when
attestedas correctby the judge beforewhom the trial
was had ,be used for purposes of appeal,and have the
same effectas a billof exceptions, in which cases the
originalreport may be transmitted to the appellate
court,from which itshallbe returnedwhen the appeal
shall have been determined .
§ 6. The officialreportersappointed under the pro-
visionsof thisact shallbe ex officioexaminers ,and
shall have fullpower to take and certifydepositions
in any of the courts of this Commonwealth . When
such depositions shall be taken by them , in short-
hand , and transcribed, the signature of the witness to
the short-hand notes, and the factof such signature
certifiedto by the reporters,shallbe a sufficientsub-
scribingto the depositionby the witness.
§ 7. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
30 - LOCAL LAWS , VOL . 3 .
Approved May 10,1890.

CHAPTER 1429 .
AN ACT toprevent the saleof spirituous,vinousor malt liquors,or
any mixture thereof,incommon schooldistrictnumber twenty-six,in
Calloway county .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be unlawful for any person or
persons to sellany spirituous,vinous or malt liquors,
or any mixture thereof, within common school dis-
trictnumber twenty -six, in Calloway county .
§ 2. That ifany person or persons shall violatethe
provisionsof this act, such person or persons shall,
upon indictment and convictionthereofin the Callo-
way Circuit Court, for each offense be fined not
lessthan fiftynor more than one hundred dollars, in

484 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the discretionof the court orjury tryingthe case;but
ifthe defendant refusesor failsto pay the fineor re-
plevy same , he or she shall be committed to the
county jail,and there to remain until, at two dollars
per day ,saidfineisfullydischarged,or worked on the
publichighway as isnow provided forby law .
§ 3. The provisionsof this act shall not operate
against any person now sellingliquorsin said district
under a licensethey now have, untilthe expirationof
such license .
§ 4. This act shall not take effect or be in force
untilitbe submitted to,and ratifiedby , the votersof
the saiddistrictat a specialelection, to be held at the
school-house on the third Saturday in June , one
thousand eight hundred and ninety, between the
hours of six o'clock in the forenoon and seven o'clock
in the afternoon . The officersfor holding said elec-
tion shall be chosen in the same manner as officers are
now selectedfor general elections, at which time it
shallbe submitted to the legalvotersin said district;
and it shallbe theduty of the officersholding said
electionto open a poll for the purpose of taking the
sense of the legal voters in said districtupon the
propositionwhether or not any spirituous, vinous or
malt liquors,or any mixture thereof therein; and it
shallbe the duty of the sheriffor other officerhold-
ing said electionto propound to each voter who may
vote this question: "Are you in favor of the saleof
spirituous, vinous or malt liquors, or any mixture
thereof, in common school districtnumber twenty-
six?" and his vote shallbe recorded for or againstit
as he directs; the pollshallbe returned as other polls
in regular elections, and shall be examined and com-
pared by the same officers.
§ 5. If itshallbe found that a majority of the legal
votes castat the said electionwere given againstthe
saleof spirituous,vinous or malt liquorsin said dis-
trict, itshall be the duty of the examining board to
certifythat fact, which certificateshall be delivered

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 485
to the clerkof the county court and by him duly re-
corded, and a certifiedcopy thereof shall be prima
facie evidence in all proceedingsunder thisact; and
then this act shall be in full force and effect.
§ 6. It shall be the duty of the sheriffor officerhold-
ing said electionto firstadvertiseand give noticeof
the time and place, alsoobject of said election,for at
leasttwenty days before the day of the election,by
postingprintednoticesat leastfiveconspicuous places
in said district .
§ 7. All acts or parts of acts in conflictwith this act
are hereby repealed.
Approved May 12,1890.

ury of the town .

CHAPTER 1441 .
AN ACT authorizingthe county attorneyof Owen county to receive
certainpublicbooks.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That for the betterpreservationof the reports

pany tocollecttollforthe Sugar Creek and Glencoe Turnpike Road
Company ,in Gallatincounty.

§ 1. That it shall be lawful for the Jonesville and
Glencoe Turnpike Road Company ,in Owen county, to
collecttollforthe Sugar Creek and Glencoe Turnpike
Road Company , in Gallatin county, for that part of
the Sugar Creek and Glencoe pike between Eagle creek
and Glencoe .

CHAPTER 1443 .
AN ACT to prevent stock from running at large on turnpike roads in
Gallatin county .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That itshallbe unlawful forany cattleor other
stock to run at large on any of the turnpike roads in
Gallatin county, where the county has subscribed
stock or aided in the construction of said roads .
§2 . Any person or personsbeing the owner or own-
ers,having the controlof any stock of any kind, who
shallsufferor permit them ,knowingly ,to run at large
on any of the above-named roads in Gallatincounty ,
shallbe subjectto a finefor every such offenseof not
lessthan one nor more than ten dollars,to be recov-
ered by warrant in the name of the Commonwealth
before the county judge or any justiceof the peace in
said county .

510 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 3. Unless said fineis paid or replevied, the stock
so running at large shallbe subject to the fineand
costs,and execution shall be levied upon it,and the
officershallproceed to collectsame as upon other ex-
ecutions, to satisfythe fineand costs.
§ 4. Itshallbe the duty of the sheriffand his dep-
uties, and the several constables of the county , to see
thatthe provisionsof thislaw iscarriedintoeffect.
§ 5. This law shall take effectfrom the firstday of
June , one thousand eight hundred and ninety,and
noticeof the same shallbe posted at each of the toll-
gates on said road at leasttwo weeks beforethislaw
takes effect.
Approved April12,1890.
CHAPTER 1445 .
AN ACT to amend an act,entitled"An act to amend an act to provide
forthe working of personscommitted to thecustodyof the jailerof
Pike, Letcher, Martin , Knott , Floyd , Johnson , Elliott and Carter
counties," approved March twenty-fourth, one thousand eighthun-
dred and eighty-eight, so far as the same applies to the counties of
Floyd and Johnson.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the above entitledact be , and the same is
hereby, amended by providingthatwhenever thework-
ing penalty is adjudged against a road surveyor in
Floyd or Johnson county, and he desiresto give bond
forworking out hisfineupon the publicroads,he shall,
upon the executionof his bond ,report to one of the
justicesof the peace in his magisterialprecinct, in
which case said justiceshallperform allof the duties
required of a surveyor by sectionsthree and four of
said act to which this act is an amendment .
§ 2. Said actisfurther amended by providing that
whenever thereisno work needed in the road precinct
in which the person executing the bond lives,then the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 511
county clerkshalldesignate some adjoining road pre-

§ 3. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
