CHAPTER 1446 .
AN ACT defining the Knott county line.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county line now existing between the
countiesof Knott and Perry be definedas follows, to
wit :Beginning atthe mouth of Yellow creek of Carr's
Fork of the North Fork of the Kentucky river; thence
up the ridge, on the west sideof Yellow creek, to the
main ridge between Carr's Fork and Lot's creek ;
thence down saiddividing ridge to Elk Gap ; thence
down the dividing ridge, between Elk and Second
creek, to Lot's creek ; thence up Lot's creek to the
mouth of Clear Fork ; thence up the point on the west .
side of Clear Fork , to ridgebetween Clear Fork and
Trace Fork to Cockerel Gap of Trace Fork of Clear
creek; thence down the top of the ridge to opposite
the mouth of Trace branch of Troublesome ; thence a
straightlineto the county lineat the mouth of Trace
branch .
§2 . This act shall take effect and be in force from .
and after itspassage .
Approved May 12,1890 .

on theirbooks , of a meeting at some suitablepoint

as the county judge may deem best, bearing six per

540 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
deliveredto the president of the said company ,who
shallexecute a receiptin the name of the company for

penditure of saidbonds to the legitimateconstruction
of said road and the necessaryexpense attending the

foreany court having jurisdiction.

afteritspassage .
Approved May 12, 1890 .

CHAPTER 1462 .

city .
officeunder the Constitutionand laws of Kentucky

Policejudge .

552 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 1463 .
AN ACT toprohibittheuseof wirefencingalongthepublicroadsand
turnpikesof Carrollcounty.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That itshallbe,and it ishereby, declaredun-
lawful to have or use barbed, serratedor twistedwire,
or any other than round ,smooth wire, in any of the
fences alone the county roads, turnpikes, or other
publicroadsof Carrollcounty, on or afterthe first
day of January , one thousand eight hundred and
ninety -one .
§ 2. That any person, firm, or corporationviolating
the provisionsof the firstsectionof thisact shall,
on indictment by the grand jury, or warrent before
any courtof competentjurisdiction,be finedone dol-
lar for each and every day he or itshallcontinue the
use of such prohibitedfencingas aforesaid:Provided ,
however, That an electionshallbe held in Carroll
county on the firstMonday of August next for the
purpose of ascertainingthe willof the people in re-
gard to the use of such wire fencingas aforesaid;and
if itappear from the resultof said electionthata ma-
jorityof the qualifiedvotersvotingat said electionis
in favor of the use of such fencing,then the penalty
aforesaid, or no other penalty,shallbe enforced.
§3 . That the sheriffof Carrollcounty shall, in at
least three consecutive issues of the " Carrollton Dem-
ocrat," cause to be published a noticeof saidelection,
and the objectof it, to be published; and shallcause
a column " for" and " against wire fencing" to be
opened in the poll-books used for the county election
at said time,in which the clerks of electionshallre-
cord the votesof electorsfor and againstwire fencing,
which polls are to be certified, returned, compared
and ascertainedby the same officers,and in the same
way as the other pollsof saidelection.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 553
§ 4. That the resultof said election,and the whole
number of votes cast for and against wire fencing,
shall be entered of record in the record books of the
Carroll County Court at its regular September term ,
one thousand eighthundred and ninety,and the same
shallbe published at leasttwice in the "Carrollton
Democrat ," and the penalty prescribed in this act
shallnot be enforcedshould itappear that a majority
of the votes cast at said election were in favor of wire
fencing .
§5 . This actshalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved May 12, 1890.

ing, not exceeding the following rates: For foot-pas-

back ,fivecentseach : Provided , That the General As-
sembly may , at any time, change the rates of toll

ratorsor stockholdersof said bridge.

stock, and to that extent may mortgage any property
itmay own .

in repairand safefor travel,and be responsibleto any

established,and , if necessaryto use forthat purpose ,
may buy lands; and if they cannot agree with the
owner or owners thereof,may condemn the same , as

Provided , Said company shall pay all cost of such
proceedingsand damages assessedtherein.
Organization .

orsmay open books and receivesubscriptionsto the
capitalstock of said company , and issue certificates
therefor, transferableas may be provided in the by-
laws : Provided, The company shall not commence
businessuntilfiftyper cent.of the capitalstock shall
be subscribed .

Exemptionof be exempt from the debts of said company ,except to
privateproperty.theextentof unpaid subscriptionstothecapitalstock.

CHAPTER 1469 .
AN ACT appointingcommissionersto select, locateand designatea
place whereon to build the court-house, clerks' officesand jailof
Magoffincounty,inthe corporatelimitsof the town of Salyersville.
WHEREAS , It has become necessaryfor the county
authoritiesof Magoffin county to build a new court-
house , clerk'sofficesand jail:
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That J. G. Trimble, of Montgomery county ;
John L. Higgins, of Breathittcounty, and W. М.
McGuire , of Morgan county, be,and they are hereby,
appointed commissionerstoselectand locatea suitable
place,anywhere in the corporatelimitsof the town of
Salyersville,whereon to erectand build a court-house,
clerks' offices, jail, and any other county buildings
for said county ; and any court-house, clerk'soffice
or officeshereafterbuiltby Magoffin county shallbe
builton the place selectedby said commissioners, or
their successors, or person appointed in theirstead,
or in stead of either one or more of said commis-
sioners .
§ 2. The commissioners appointed by this act, or
those appointed in theirstead, or in stead of one or
more of said commissioners, as hereinafterprovided,
shallmeet at the court-house, in the town of Salyers.
ville, as soon as practicableafter the passage of this
bill, and after firstbeing sworn to select the most
suitablelocationfor the public buildingsof Magoffin
county , the conveniences and inconveniences to the
people of the county to be considered; and when
they shallso selectthe locationfor the said public
buildingsof Magoffin county,they shallhave at least
two streetscrossingeach other laid off by a practical
surveyor. They will designate the width of the
streetsand alleys, selecta public square of as much
land as they may think proper, and plainlydesignate

572 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
it by stones or other permanent means , designating
the place in such publicsquare where the court-house
shall be built, also clerks' offices, if built separate
from the court-house and the jail; and such place
selectedby said commissioners as the public square
shall be the public square of Magoffin county , and
any court-house,clerk'sofficeor officesor jailhere-
afterbuiltby Magoffin county shall be built on the
place selectedby said commissioners.
§3 . That should said commissioners select another
place than that where the court-house,clerks' offices
and jailnow stand for the public buildings of said
county, the board of commissioners of said county
shallhave the right to tear down ,selland disposeof
any of the material now in the court-house, clerks'
officesor jail,or may sellthe said buildings as they
stand,the proceeds to be applied to buildinganother
court house , and so forth.
§ 4. The commissioners appointed under thisactwill
investigatethe titleto such land as they may select
for the public square, as herein directed, and report
the same as hereinafterprovided.
§ 5. That in the event of any of the commissioners
appointed by this act failingor refusing to act, the
judge of the Magoffin CircuitCourt shall,by writing,
appoint some suitableperson or persons to fillthe
place of such if said commissioners fail or refuse to
act .
§ 6. The commissioners provided for by this act
shall, afterthey have selecteda locationforthe court-
house and clerks' offices,and so forth,ashereindirect-
ed,make out a fullreport,in writing,designatingthe
place for the saidpublic buildings, and such streetsas
they think proper to designate, and returnsaid report
to the chairman of the board of commissioners of Ma-
goffincounty, who will deliveritto the county court
clerk of Magoffin county, whose duty itshallbe to re-
cord the same atlength in the book in hisofficeknown
as the Will Book .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 573
§ 7. The board of commissioners of Magoffin county
shall, at theirfirstregular meeting afterthe actionof
the commissioners hereby appointed,make a reason-
able allowance (to be paid out of the county levy of
Magoffin county) to said commissioners fortheirserv-
ices, and to such surveyor as may perform servicesin
locatingsaidpublic square.
§ 8. Provided , That no court-house,clerk'soffice,
jailor other county buildings shallbe erected on the
siteselectedby said commissioners untilthe votersof
Magoffin county have voted upon the question as to
whether the place selectedby said commissioners is
the proper place upon which to erect said buildings.
§ 9. That after the locationhas been selectedby
said commissioners,ifany of the citizensof Magoffin
county desire a differentlocation in the corporate
limitsof the town of Salyersville, they may apply to
the county court of Magoffin county,and move the
court to submit to the votersof the county the ques-
tion as to which placein the corporate limitsof said
town shall be selected for the location of the said
county buildings; and the court shall, by order
entered of record, fix the different desired locations
for said buildings that are in said corporate limits,
and directthat the questionas to which shall be se-
lectedtothelegalvotersof saidcounty atthe firstregu-
lar electionthereafter; and a pollshallbe opened for
that purpose at saidelectionin allthe votingdistricts
in said county,and votesshallbe receivedfor each of
the designatedlocations,includingthe place selected
by said commissioners ; and the place receivingthe
largestnumber of votesshallbe the locationfor said
county buildings, and they shallbe erectedthereon.
§10. That thisact shalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved May 12, 1890 .

CHAPTER 1476 .
AN ACT to prohibitthe saleof spirituous, vinous or malt liquors
withinone mile ofthepublicschool-houseofHindman ,Kentucky .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That whoever sellsany spirituous, vinous or
malt liquorswithinone mile of the publicschool-house
of Hindman ,Knott county,shall, upon conviction,be
finedone hundred dollars,recoverableupon a warrant
issued by the judge of the Knott County Court,the
policejudge of the town of Hindman ,or any justice
of the peace livingwithin one mile of said school-
house , or upon an indictment in the Knott Circuit
Court .
§ 2. That it shallbe the duty of the officersherein
named ,upon personalknowledge ,or upon the affidavit
of another that the provisionsof the firstsectionof
thisacthave been violated,to issuea warrant of arrest
forsuch offender,and placesaid warrant in the hands
of some peace officerof Knott county,who shall,with-
out unnecessary delay,proceed to arrestsuch offender
and bring him beforesome peace officerfortrial.
§ 3. If any officerherein named shallfailor refuse
toperformthe dutieshereinimposed,he shall,upon
indictmentand conviction,be finedone hundred dol-
lars.
§ 4. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved May 13,1890 .

CHAPTER 1477 .
AN ACT toincorporate"Chemical Bank " ofLawrenceburg,Kentucky .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That there shall be, and there is hereby , es-
tablished in the town of Lawrenceburg , Anderson
Name. county,Kentucky ,a bank ,by the name of " Chemical
Bank ," with a capitalstock of fiftythousand dollars,
to be divided into shares of one hundred dollars each ,
and which may be increased to two hundred thousand
dollars .
§2 . The subscribersand holders of said shares of
Body-politic. stock, and theirsuccessorsand assigns, shallbe,and
are hereby ,createda body -politicand corporate,under
the name and styleof " Chemical Bank ;" and by that
name shall be competent to contract and be contracted
with, to sue and be sued, plead and be impleaded ,
answer and be answered, defend and be defended , in
allcourtsand places,and in all matters whatever, as
naturalpersons; with fullpower and authorityto ac-
quire, hold, possess,use,occupy and enjoy, and the
same to sell,convey and disposeof,allsuch realestate,
goods,effectsand chattelsas shallbe necessaryand
convenientforthe transactionof itsbusiness,or which
may be conveyed,assigned, delivered,or in any way
pledged to said bank ,or which may be purchased or
conveyed in satisfactionof any debt or any judgment
or decree of any court in favorof saidbank ; and may
have and use a common seal,and alter,change orrenew
the same at pleasure ; and the board of directors of
saidbank ,hereinafterprovided for,may make ,ordain
and establish,and put in execution,such by -laws,or-
dinances, rules and regulationsas said board may
deem necessaryand proper for the good management
of saidbank and the prudent and efficientmanagement
of itsaffairs: Provided , That the same shall not be in

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 615
anywise contrary to the Constitutionand laws of this
State or the United States .
§ 3. The business affairsof said bank shall be man -Business
aged by a board of not lessthan three nor more than
fivedirectors,a majority of whom shallconstitutea
quorum for the transactionof business. Any stock-
holder may be a director. Said directorsshall,in all
cases, be electedby those owning a majority of the
stock ; and afterthe firstelection(which shallbe held
at a time and place chosen by thosesubscribinga ma-
jorityof the stock) said directorsshall be electedat
the banking -house of said bank on the Saturday be
fore the firstday of January of each year, and shall
continue in officeforone year,and untiltheirsuccess-
ors are duly elected; but a failureto hold said elec-
tion on the day above designated shall, in no case,
operate as a forfeitureof this charter, or in anywise
impair the rightsand privilegesof said corporation;
but in such casesthe electionmay be holden on a day
appointed by the board of directorsafterthirtydays '
written notices,by the cashieror president, posted at
the banking -house of saidbank ,post-officeand court-
house,or two successivepublicationsinany Anderson
county newspaper ,and shallhave the same effectas if
holden on the regularday designatedforsaidelection.
Said electionshall,in allcases, be by viva voce, and
shall be held by a clerk and judge designated by the
board of directors at some meeting previous to the
same ; and the certificateof such clerkand judge shall
be conclusive proof of the electionof the persons
therein named as directors . At all said elections for
directors each stockholder shall have one vote for each
and every share of stock he or she may own and hold
in said bank . Should the board,from any cause, fail
to designatea clerkand judge, or either, to hold said
electionon said regularelectionday ,then the stock-
holders,or any of them , may appear at saidbank , on
said regularelectionday ,and electa board ; and the
certificateof such stockholders,holding and owning a

duties .

majority of the stock, shallbe conclusiveproof and
evidence of election, and the directors so elected shall
commence their term of officefrom and after such cer-
tificate .
§ 4. Any vacancy occurring in the'directory,from
death, resignation, failureto accept, or from any cause
whatsoever,shallbe filledby the remaining board .
§ 5. The directorschosen may elect a president,
cashier, and such other officersas they deem necessa-
ry to carry on saidbank , fixthe salariesthereof,and
shallrequirebonds forthe faithfuldischarge of their
duties; and theirterms of officeshallcontinue only at
the pleasure of the board .
§ 6. Said bank may take deposits or pledges of
stock in thisbank , or other stocks,bonds ,realestate,
chosesin action, judgments of courts, warehouse re
ceipts, or other things,to secure it for any money or
demands thatmay be due or owing saidbank by per-
sons so depositingor pledging; and said bank shall
have a superiorand firstlien on all such stocks and
securitiespledged as aforesaid; and said bank shall
have the right, in order to realizeupon the same ,to
sellthem in any way agreed upon between theparties.
§ 7. C. A. Leathers , T. J. Ballard and T. C. With-
erspoon shall constitutea committee (any two of
whom may act) to receivesubscriptionsforstock, and
the books shallbe kept open for subscriptionsuntil
fiftythousand dollarsshallhave been taken ; but the
failureto secure the whole of the fiftythousand dollars
in subscriptions of stock shall in no sense work or

as much as thirtythousand dollarsof stock shallhave
been subscribedand paid in,saidcorporationshallbe
complete in allsensesand for allpurposes whatsoever,
and saidbank may then commence businessand have
allthe rightsand privilegesof thischarter.
§ 8. Privateproperty of the individualstockholders
shallin no casebe responsiblefor the corporatedebts
or liabilities .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 617
§ 9. The regular meetings of the board of directors
shallbe on every Saturday,but the presidentmay call
a meeting at any time he may think proper.
§ 10. Dividends,ifany ,shallbe declaredon theSat-
urday before the firstday of January and July of
each year.
§ 11. Said bank shall be taxed as other incorporated
banks .
§ 12. Said bank may go intoliquidation, wind up

any way not inconsistent with this charter and the
rightsof the stockholders, any time,upon the written
desireof thoseowning a majorityof the stock;but
in allcaseswhere the stock isincreased,at leastfifty
per cent.of same must be paid in at the time,and the
balance of the increasemust be paid in fullwithin
twelve months after such increase .
§ 13. Said bank shallhave the rightto receivede-
posits, to investitsmoney and funds in bonds and
stocks, and to loan money on realestate, and to pur-
chase notes and billssecured by real estate,but said
bank shalldispose of allsuch real estatewithin five
years from the time itbecomes the absolute owner of
said realestate,by purchase or otherwise,except such
as is used for its own banking -house .
§ 14. This act shall take effectand be in force from
and after its passage ; and the incorporators, C. A.
Leathers,T. J. Ballardand A. C. Witherspoon,shall
have all the power and authorityof directorsuntil

Approved May 18,1890.

CHAPTER 1478 .
AN ACT toincorporatethe Bank of Fort Jefferson,atthe cityofFort
Jefferson, Ballard county.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That there ishereby established a bank of dis-
count and deposit in the city of Fort Jefferson, in
Ballard county; the subscribers, theirassociates, suc-
cessorsand assigns, shallbe a body -politicand cor-
porate,by the name and style " Bank of Fort Jeffer-
son ;" and shallbe capable of contractingand being
contractedwith, of suing and being sued, of implead-
ing and being impleaded, and of answering and de-
fending, in all courts and places whatever ; it may
have a common seal, and change and renew the same
at pleasure .
§ 2. That said bank may be under the control of
three or more directors, to be electedby the stock-
holders,and shallserve one year, or until theirsuc-
cessorsshallbe electedand qualified. The directors
may select, annually,a presidentfrom their number .
The stockholdersmay meet and electthe said threeor
more directorswhen twenty -fiveper cent. of the capi-
talstock shallbe subscribed, and when fiftyper cent.
thereof shall be paid. F. H. Jackson ,T. G. Stuart,
G. W. Richardson, StanleyT. Pruitt,John H. Stuart,
or any two of them , may , at such time,place,and in
such manner as suits their convenience, open books
for subscriptionfor the capitalstock, which shallbe
in sharesof one hundred dollarseach ; and said cor-
poration may commence business so soon as the said
fiftyper cent. of the twenty-fiveper cent. subscribed
shallbe paid in, and the presidentand cashiershall
have made oath to the fact of such payment be-
foresome judge,justiceor notary : Provided , The en-
tirestock subscribed for shall be paid in full within
twelve months after the date of the commencement of

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 619
business by the bank ; and itisfurtherprovided,that
should the board of directorsat any time increasethe
capitalstock,as providedinsectionone,atleastfifty
per cent.of said additionalstock shallbe paid in at
the time saidsubscriptionismade , and the remainder
within twelve months from that date ; and itisfurther
provided , that, in the event the stock is not paid in
fullat the expirationof twelvemonths from the date
of commencement of businessby the bank , or within
twelve months from the date of subscriptionof in-
creased stock,then the capitalof said bank shallbe
reduced to the amount of stock paid in full.
§ 3. That the directorsshallhave power to receiveStock.
subscriptionsforso many sharesof the stock of said
company remaining unsold as they may at any time
choose to sell; to declaredividends of the profitsaris-
ing out of the business,and to appoint such officers,
agents and servants as they may deem necessaryto
conduct the businessof said bank ,and pay them such
sum as wages and salaries,and take from them such
bonds to secure the faithful performance of their
duties, as they may think reasonable and proper.
In case of death or resignationof any director, the
vacancy may be filledby the board of directors.
The stock shall be deemed personal property, and
shall be assignableonly on the books of the bank ,
upon such rulesas the board of directorsshall, from
time to time, establish; but said corporation shall
have a lienon the stock to secure any unpaid balance
thereon. The privateproperty of stockholders shall
not be responsiblefor the debts of said corporation,
except to the amount of theirsubscription.
§ 4. The capitalstock of said bank shall be one
hundred thousand dollars, in shares of one hundred
dollarseach, as setforthin sectiontwo .
§5 . That saidbank may transactbusinessas a nat-Business.
ural person ; may loan money , discount promissory
notesor billsof exchange ; may buy and sellgold and
silvercoin, exchange , stock or other securities, and

620 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
allother evidencesof indebtedness,and may do a gen-
eral banking business, except that it shall not have
power to make or issuenotes of itsown . The prom-
issorynotes and billsof exchange made negotiable
and payable at its banking -house , or at any other
banking -house, when discounted by the bank , shall
be, and they are hereby, put upon the same footingof
foreign billsof exchange , and like remedy may be
had thereon,jointlyand severally,againstthe makers ,
drawers and indorsers . The said bank shall have
power to make advances on approved securities,upon
agriculturalproducts, includinggrowing crops, and
upon manufactures and fixtures,as personalproperty.
It may receivein pledge, or as security for money
loaned or debts owing it,bonds ,stocks,produce and
merchandise, United States vouchers, certificatesor
bonds of indebtedness,or bonds of this or any other
State,cityor county or company incorporatedby the
United States,or any other State,and sellthe same on
the non -payment of the debt or demand by ordinary
judicialproceedings.
§ 6. The bank may receivedepositsof gold, silver,
bank notes, or other notes which may be circulating
as money ,and repay the same in such manner , at such
times, and with such interest,not exceeding six per
cent.per annum ,as may be agreed upon with the de-
positorsby specialor general contract. Said bank
may receivedepositsfrom minors and married women ,
and the receiptsof such minors and married women
shall be valid .
May hold
property - how .
§ 7. That saidbank may acquire,hold, possess, use,
occupy and enjoy allsuch realestate, goods and chat-
tels, as may be necessaryfor the transactionof its
business,or to further the objectsof its creation, or
which may be conveyed to itas securityof any debtor
purchased to aid in the satisfactionof any debt, judg-
ment or decree : Provided , Said bank shall not hold
such real estatelongerthan fiveyears, and selland
convey the same ; and said bank may make such by

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 621
laws for theirgovernment as they may deem neces-
sary : Provided , The same be not contrary to the laws
and Constitution of this State or of the United States .
§ 8. That it shall not be lawful for this bank to
issue any note or billto be passed or used as currency .
§ 9. That thisact shalltake effectfrom itspassage,
and continue in force thirty years .
Approved May 13,1890.
CHAPTER 1479 .
AN ACT to amend the charterof the cityof Covington .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. The act to amend the charter of said city,ap-
proved May fifteenth, one thousand eight hundred
and eighty-six,so far as it provides for a board of
equalization,isherebyamended totheextentthatsaid
board shall hereafter consist of three members ,to be
appointed by the mayor of the cityof Covington.
Said board may sit for thirtydays, excluding Sun-
days , and their compensation shallbe fixed by the
common council at not exceeding to each member five
dollarsfor each day saidboard is actuallyin session.
Said board shallnot increasethe assessmentupon any
propertyin said cityuntilit has given threedays'
noticeto the property-holder in person, or by leaving
a written noticeat his usual place of residence, or by
mailing a notice to his customary address . Should
said property-holderbe a non -resident,saidnoticemay
be given to his agent ; should he have no known agent
residing said city, and should his address be un-
known , said noticemay be dispensed with. If,within
the time limitedby said notice,said property-holder
appearsbeforesaidboard, he may urge any reason
he has against increasingsaid assessment, and may

622 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
introduce witnesses on his behalf . The member of
saidboardsittingas chairmanisherebyauthorizedto
administer oaths to such witnesses .
§ 2. From and after the expiration of the current
term of officeof the present streetcommissioner of
the city of Covington, the officeof streetcommis-
sioner of said city shall become abolished, and the
duties of said officeshall be performed by such per-
son or officeras the mayor of said city may recom-
mend and the common council may elect.
§ 3. The commissioners of water-works for the city
of Covington may contractto supply water to any in-
dividual, corporationor municipalitylocated beyond
the corporatelimitsof said city,the water to be de-
liveredat the limitsof said city; but the connections
must be made there by such individual, corporation
or municipalitywithout expense to said commission-
ers or to the city of Covington. All contractsfor
furnishingwater,within or without the limitsof said
city,shallbe made by said commissioners upon such
terms as shallbe agreed upon , provided saidcommis-
sionersmay fix schedules of prices which shall be
general in their applicationto all consumers of the
same class; but in no case shall a contract be made at
lesssum than the cost of pumping , including therein
interestupon the amount expended upon the water-
works system and the wear and tearupon machinery .
Before any contractmade by said commissioners to
furnish water to consumers beyond the corporate
limitsof saidcityshalltake effect,the same shallbe
ratifiedby the citycouncil or the common councilof
saidcityby ordinance,resolutionor order.
§ 4. The treasurerof the city of Covington shall
hereafterbe ex officiothe treasurerof the commission-
ersof water-works forthe cityof Covington,and shall
receive,keep and pay out allthe funds and revenues
of saidboard under itsrules,regulationsand by -laws,
and shallexecuteto saidcommissioners separatebond ,
conditionedfor the faithfulperformance of his duties

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 629
as such treasurer, in such penalty as the said board
shall direct, and for his servicesas such treasurer the
said commissioners shallpay him a salarynot exceed-
ing two hundred and fiftydollarsper annum .
§ 5. Said commissioners are authorizedand empow-
ered to constructand maintain threehouses in Camp-
bell county ,Kentucky , for the use of the engineers
and employes at the new pump -works of the cityof
Covington ; the totalcost of constructingsaid three
houses not to exceed the sum of five thousand dollars .
§ 6. The judgeof thecitycourtof Covingtonshall
have thesame power tosolemnizemarriagesthatjudges
of the county court now have in this Commonwealth .
§ 7. This actshallbe in forcefrom itspassage ; and
all acts and parts of actsin conflictherewith are re-
pealed .
:
Approved May 13,1890.

CHAPTER 1476 .
AN ACT to prohibitthe saleof spirituous, vinous or malt liquors
withinone mile ofthepublicschool-houseofHindman ,Kentucky .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of theCom-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That whoever sellsany spirituous, vinous or
malt liquorswithinone mile of the publicschool-house
of Hindman , Knott county, shall, upon conviction,be
finedone hundred dollars,recoverableupon a warrant
issued by the judge of the Knott County Court,the
police judge of the town of Hindman , or any justice
of the peace livingwithin one mile of said school-
house, or upon an indictment in the Knott Circuit
Court .
§ 2. That it shallbe the duty of the officersherein
named ,upon personalknowledge ,or upon the affidavit
of another that the provisionsof the firstsectionof
thisact have been violated,to issuea warrant of arrest
forsuch offender,and placesaid warrant in the hands
of some peace officerof Knott county,who shall,with-
out unnecessary delay,proceed to arrestsuch offender
and bring him beforesome peace officerfor trial.
§ 3. If any officerherein named shallfailor refuse
to perform the duties herein imposed ,he shall, upon
indictmentand conviction,be finedone hundred dol-
lars.
§ 4. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved May 13,1890 .

CHAPTER 1479 .
AN ACT to amend the charterof the cityof Covington .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . The act to amend the charter of said city,ap-
proved May fifteenth, one thousand eight hundred
and eighty-six,so far as it provides for a board of
equalization,ishereby amended to the extent thatsaid
board shall hereafter consist of three members , to be
appointed by the mayor of the city of Covington.
Said board may sit for thirtydays , excluding Sun-
days , and their compensation shallbe fixed by the
common council at not exceeding to each member five
dollars for each day said board is actually in session.
Said board shallnot increasethe assessmentupon any
property in said cityuntil it has given three days '
noticeto the property-holder in person, or by leaving
a written noticeat his usual place of residence,or by
mailing a notice to his customary address . Should
said property-holderbe a non -resident,saidnoticemay
be given to his agent ; should he have no known agent
residing said city, and should his address be un-
known ,said noticemay be dispensed with. If,within
the time limitedby said notice, said property-holder
appears before said board, he may urge any reason
he has against increasingsaid assessment, and may

622 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
introduce witnesses on his behalf . The member of
saidboardsittingas chairmanisherebyauthorizedto
administer oaths to such witnesses .
§ 2. From and after the expiration of the current
term of officeof the present streetcommissioner of
the city of Covington, the officeof streetcommis-
sioner of said city shallbecome abolished, and the
duties of said officeshallbe performed by such per-
son or officeras the mayor of said city may recom-
mend and the common council may elect.
§ 3. The commissioners of water-works for the city
of Covington may contractto supply water to any in-
dividual,corporationor municipalitylocated beyond
the corporatelimitsof said city, the water to be de-
liveredat the limitsof said city; but the connections.
must be made there by such individual, corporation
or municipalitywithout expense to said commission-
ers or to the cityof Covington. All contractsfor
furnishingwater,within or without the limitsof said
city,shallbe made by said commissioners upon such
terms as shallbe agreed upon ,provided saidcommis-
sionersmay fix schedules of prices which shall be
general in their applicationto all consumers of the
same class; but in no case shall a contract be made at
lesssum than thecostof pumping , includingtherein
interestupon the amount expended upon the water-
works system and the wear and tearupon machinery .
Before any contractmade by said commissioners to
furnishwater to consumers beyond the corporate
limitsof saidcityshalltake effect,the same shallbe
ratifiedby the citycouncil or the common councilof
saidcityby ordinance,resolutionor order.
§ 4. The treasurerof the city of Covington shall
hereafterbe ex officiothe treasurerof the commission-
ersof water-works for the cityof Covington,and shall
receive,keep and pay out allthe fundsand revenues
ofsaidboard under itsrules,regulationsand by -laws,
and shallexecuteto saidcommissioners separatebond ,
conditionedfor the faithfulperformance of his duties

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 629
as such treasurer, in such penalty as the said board
shall direct, and for his servicesas such treasurer the
said commissioners shallpay him a salarynot exceed-
ing two hundred and fiftydollarsper annum .
§ 5. Said commissioners are authorizedand empow-
ered to construct and maintain three houses in Camp-
bellcounty,Kentucky, forthe use of the engineers
and employes at the new pump -works of the cityof
Covington ; the totalcost of constructingsaid three
houses not to exceed the sum of five thousand dollars .
§ 6. The judgeof thecitycourtof Covingtonshall
have thesame power tosolemnizemarriagesthatjudges
of the county court now have in this Commonwealth .
§ 7. This actshallbe in forcefrom itspassage; and
all actsand parts of actsin conflictherewith are re-
pealed .
:
Approved May 13 ,1890.

-
WHEREAS , E. L. Garnett, James Carman and John
W. Wrather , as trusteesof school districtnumber
forty-two ,Graves county,Kentucky , duly electedand

duties,and in obedience to an order from the county

of - , one thousand eight hundred and eighty-six,
open a pollat the publicschool-house inschooldis-
trictnumber forty-two, in the county of Graves,and
State of Kentucky ,afterdue noticethereof, signed by
the county superintendentand the trustees,had been
posted at threeor more public placesin said district,
as the law required,for the purpose of ascertainingthe
will of the legal white voters of said districtas to
whether or not they would tax themselves twenty -five
cents on the one hundred dollarsfor the purpose of
paying for a supply of desks and seatspurchased by
saidtrusteesfortheuseand benefitof publicschools-
of said districtnumber forty-two ; and whereas , a
largemajorityofthelegalvotersofsaiddistrictvoted
in favor of said tax, and the resultof said election
was properlycertifiedtothecountysuperintendentby
the judgeand clerkthereof,as requiredby law,after-
which the trusteesplaced a listof the names of the
property-owners of saiddistrictin the hands of the
sheriff, J. T. Wilson, with instructionto proceedat
once to the collectionof said tax ; and whereas,
the said sheriff had collected about three-fourths
of said tax when P. D. Carman et al . swore out a
writenjoiningthesheriffand trusteesfrom collecting
said tax ; and whereas, the suit of injunction re-
mained in court from term to term, and from year to
year, thedefendantsand theirwitnessesappearingat

640 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
each term ofcourt,untiltheJuly,one thousandeight
hundred and eighty-nine,term of the Graves County
Court of Common Pleas, when W. P. Lee, special
judge,heard the case and rendered judgment sustain-
ing and perpetuating the injunction upon a mere
trivialoversightof the trusteesin failingto date their
record of election, and of the county superintendent
in failingto date his order of electionand designating
the locationof the district,thus leaving the trustees
with their obligationfor the desks and seats pur-
chased by them for thedistrictunpaid,besidesa large

money collectedby him .

sage .

CHAPTER 1490 .
AN ACT toregu'atemunicipalelectionsin the cityof Owensboro.
Be it enactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That from and afterthe passage of thisact all
electionsin the cityof Owensboro of municipal offi-
cers,members of the citycounciland schooltrustees,
or to determine any municipal question submitted to
the people,shallbe held and carriedon by ballotin
the manner hereinafterprovided.
§ 2. In order to have their names printed on the
ballots hereinafterdescribed,candidates must be nom-
inated, as follows : A candidate for one ward must be
nominated by a written petition,accompanied by the
citytreasurer'sreceiptfor fivedollars,of ten or more
registered voters of such ward ; a candidate for the
cityat large must be nominated by the written peti-
tion, accompanied by a like receiptfor ten dollars,
of thirtyor more registeredvoters of the city; the
petitionand receipt, in eithercase,to be presented to
the mayor not less than ten days, Sunday included,

Any registeredcitizenof the cityof Owensboro shall
have the power to enforce,by mandamus , the mayor's
or council's compliance with this section.

contemplated in said act, to Kirkwood , a distanceof

same rules, regulationsand restrictionsin the con-
structionof the said extensionas are provided in the
said act for the constructionof the said turnpike.

amount not exceeding thirty-five hundred dollars.

CHAPTER 1551 .
AN ACT toincorporatetheFlatCreek and Bald Eagle Turnpike Road
Company , in Bath county .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That a company be,and ishereby,incorporated,
under the name and styleof the Flat Creek and Bald
Eagle Turnpike Road Company ; and by said name
and styleshallhave perpetualsuccession, and may
sue and be sued, plead and be impleaded; and as
such,ishereby investedwithallthe powers enjoyed
by similarcorporationsin thisCommonwealth ; and
the tollcharged shall be as therein provided. The
purpose of which company is to build a turnpike
road from Flat Creek Storehouse , on the Mount Sterl-
ing and OwingsvilleTurnpike Road (about midway
between said towns) down said creek, by the most
practicableroute,totheOwingsvilleand Sharpsburg
Turnpike Road , at the mouth of Bald Eagle branch,a
distance of about four miles .
§2 . The capitalstock of saidcompany shallbe di

794 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
vided intosharesof fiftydollarseach ; and the entire
capital stock shall not exceed twelve thousand dol-
lars .
§3 . The following persons are hereby appointed
commissioners to receive subscriptions of stock to
said company , to wit : Reuben Harper ,E. E. Peck ,
George Hamilton ,Senior,Richard Donaldson ,Newton
Ratliff, Moses Lewis and James Carroll Hamilton ;
and they may each open books and receive subscrip-
tions at any time and place after the passage this act,
and so continue until enough is subscribed to com-
plete said road ; and may take subscriptionson any
conditionexpressed in writing.
§ 4. When the commissioners think a sufficient
quantity of stock has been taken to justify a com-
mencement of work , they, or such of them as may
act,shallcalla meeting of the stockholders, and hold
an election for a president and four directors for one
year,or untiltheirsuccessorsare elected; and there-
upon work upon said road may commence under their
superintendence and control.
§ 5. The president and directors shall appoint one
of their number treasurer, who shall execute bond ,
with approved security, and be responsiblefor allthe
company's moneys receivedby him , and shall pay
out the same as directedby the president and direct-
ors, and keep an account of allhis transactions.
§ 6. The board of directors,consistingof the presi-
dent and four directors,shallkeep a fairrecord of
theirproceedings, which shallbe open to the inspec-
tion of stockholders .
§ 7. The board of directorsshallhave allthe pow-
ers,rightsand privileges,and may do allthe actsand
things necessary for letting, contracting,completing
and managing said turnpike road, and locate the
same , and determine its width and grade on such
route as they deem proper, subject to section one of
this act .
§ 8. The county court of Bath county is authorized

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 795
to subscribeto the capitalstock of said company in
an amount not exceeding seven hundred and fiftydol-
larsforeach mile which may be builtin said county ,
and in the same proportion for each fractionof a
mile .
§ 9. Said company shall not subject itself to a
greater amount of indebtedness than five thousand
dollars .
§ 10.That theprivatepropertyofstockholdersshall
be exempt from corporatedebts.
§ 11. That in the election of officersand voting on
allimportant matters,each share of stock shall con-
stitutea vote, and can be cast by the holder or by
written proxy .
§ 12. That so soon as two and a half miles of said
roadshallhave been completedand acceptedby the
county commissioners,the president and directorsof
said road may erect a toll-gate and collecttolls in ac-
cordance with the laws of Kentucky .
§ 13. That this act shall take effectfrom and after
its passage .
Approved May 15,1890.

ply,allpersonswho arewithintheroad-working age,

isa necessityfor dividing the roads in the county in

and the county court shall make the order according
to the provisions of section twenty -three, chapter
ninety-four, of the General Statutes.

commissioners to keep a book , in which they record
the name of each surveyor of the public roads in said
county, and the number of their road precincts, to-
gether with a listof all the tools, and so forth,
furnishedsaid surveyors. Said book shallbe kept in

journments of said board; and for their servicesas

point a surveyor on each sectionor road precinctin

ment as surveyor, provided he iswithin the working
age .

nually in writing to the judge of the county court.

quired by law . Said reportshallbe sworn to by the

returnabletosaidjudge,tobe triedfor said offense.

any and all surveyorswho failto comply with their

judgeshallissuehiswarrantforsaiddelinquentsur-
veyors .
§ 13. It shallbe the duty of the sheriffto collectthe
taxes hereinprovided for,and he shallhave the same

this Commonwealth ; said sheriffshall expend the

road commissioners,and shall settlewith said com-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 799 ,
his bond as sherifffor the performance of the duties

§ 14. The severalsurveyorsof the road precinct in
said county shall,when hauling or plowing isneces-

ing intctheirhands as surveyor, from any source, or
so much thereofas may be necessaryto pay forsaid
hauling or plowing, at the rate of two dollarsper
day ; and the receipt of the owner of said team or
teams shallbe filedby the surveyor in his report to
the judge of the county court, saidreport tobe made
annually on or before the firstMonday of November
of each year .
§ 15. All finesunder this act shallbe collectedby
the sheriffand paid over to the surveyor of the road
precinct in which the delinquency occurred forwhich
the finewas imposed , and shall be accounted for by
said surveyor as other moneys coming intohis hands .
§ 16. The sheriffshall be allowed by the court of
claims, forcollectingsaidroad taxes,to be paid for

tion .
§ 17.All actsin conflictherewitharerepealed;but
thisact shallnot be construed to repeal any of the

§ 18. This actshallbe in forcefrom itspassage,and

CHAPTER 1560 .

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That from and after the passage of this act all
Electiontobebyelectionsin the city of Covington of municipal offi-
cers,members of the citycounciland school trustees,
or to determine any municipal question submitted to
the people, shallbe held and carriedon by ballotin
the manner hereinafterprovided.
Candidates
must be nomi-
nated .
§2 . In order to have their names printed on the
ballots hereinafter described ,candidates must be nom-
inated as follows : A candidate for one ward must be
nominated by a written petition, accompanied by the
city treasurer'sreceipt for fivedollars, of ten or
more registered voters of such ward ; a candidate for
the cityat large must be nominated by the written
petition,accompanied by a likereceiptfortwenty dol-
lars, of fiftyor more registeredvotersof the city; the
petitionand receipt, in eithercase,to be presented to
the mayor not lessthan ten days, Sunday included,
beforethe election. Each voter signing said petition
must add to his signaturehisplace of residence, with
the streetand number thereof,and each voter may
subscribeto one nominating petitionfor each office
to be filled,and no more . Each petition,containing
the full name of the candidate, shall specify, first,
the officefor which the candidate is nominated ; sec-
ond ,the candidate'splaceof residence,with streetand
number thereon,ifany . The candidate nominated by

by the said candidate'smark and the signatureof the
party doing the writing,indorseupon said petitiona
briefstatement that he consents to act, if elected, to
the officefor which the petitionnominates him . The
petition,being apparently in conformity with the pro-
visionsof thissection, shallbe deemed valid, unless,
within two days afteritspresentationto the mayor ,
objectionthereto, in writing, is made . Any such ob-
jectionor apparent irregularityshallbe passed upon
by themayor ,citysolicitorand thecityclerk,and the
decisionof a majority of these officersshall be final.
In casesuch objectionismade or an apparent irregu-

objection or apparent irregularity, shall be at once
sentby the mayor to the candidate affectedthereby,
addressed to his residence as given in the petition.
Should the decisionbe againstthe validityof the pe-

furthertime of one day to perfectthe petition. All
petitionsof nomination, and the receiptsaccompany-
ing them ,shallbe open to public inspection,and the
mayor shallpreservesame in hisofficefor at leastsix
months afterthe electionto which they pertain.

CHAPTER 1562 .
AN ACT to permit the Warren County Court to change or modify the
terms and conditionsupon which Warren countyagreedtosubscribe
to the capitalstockof the Bowling Green and Northern Railroad
Company .
WHEREAS , The county of Warren has agreed to
subscribeone hundred and fiftythousand dollarsto
the capitalstock of the Bowling Green and Northern
Railroad Company , to be used in the construction
thereof; and whereas,said county and said railroad
company mutually desire, and are mutually inter-
ested in, the speedy completion of said road from
Bowling Green to the Chesapeake , Ohio and South-
western Railroad ; and whereas,it is representedby
said county and by said railroadcompany that the
constructionof said road can be accomplished at

858 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
greatlyreduced costby the use of convictlabor; and
whereas , if such labor can not be used in the con-
structionof said road, the building thereof isjeop-
ardized; and whereas,the building thereof isof vast
importance, not only to said Bowling Green and
Northern Railroad Company ,but alsoto saidcounty
of Warren ; now , therefore,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Warren County Court, a majority of
the justicesin commission concurringtherein,acting
for and on behalf of Warren county, is hereby au-
thorized and empowered to change or modify the
contractof Warren county with said railroadcom-
pany in regard to the working of convicts, so as to
permit said company to work convicts in the con-
structionof said road in Warren county or elsewhere
along the lineof said road,as may be designatedby
the order of said court ; and if convict labor shall be
so used,itsuse shallnot in any way ,or to any extent,
affectthe subscriptionthat said county has or may
make to the capitalstock of saidBowling Green and
Northern Railroad Company ; nor shallit affectin
anywise the validityof any bonds that said county
may issuein payment of her subscriptionto the cap-
italstock of said railroad company .
§ 2. This act shall take effectand be in force from
and afteritspassage.
Approved May 15,1890.

CHAPTER 1565 .
AN ACT toincorporatethecityof Flemingsburg.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That so much of Fleming county as is em-
braced within a boundary of one square mile, the
court-house of Fleming county being the geographi-
cal center, and all persons residing within said
boundary is hereby created a body -politicand corpo-
rate,under the name and styleof the " City of Flem-
ingsburg;" the sidesof said square mile to run par-
allel with the sides of the said court -house .
council of said cityshall cause a survey and plat of
the same to be made and recorded in the office of the
clerkof the Fleming County Court ; and the corporate
limitsof the cityof Flemingsburg shall be as herein
The

860 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
indicated, namely , the one square mile above de-
scribed .
§ 2. That the fiscal,municipal and prudentialaffairs
of said city, together with the government thereof,
shall be vested in a board of council, six in number ,
who shall be elected by the qualifiedvoters of said
cityon the firstMonday in January , one thousand
eight hundred and ninety-one, three of whom shall
serve for two , and three of whom shall serve for four
years; the threewho shall so serve for two , and the
threewho shallso servefor four years,shallbe deter-
mined by lotby saidcouncilwhen electedand quali-
fied. And after the first election the term of office
of said board of council shall be four years,three of
the members of same going out of officeevery two
years. An electionshallbe held on the firstMonday
in January every two years thereafterfor the election
of the three members of said council in place of the
three members so going out of office. No one shall
be eligible to the officeof councilman of said city
who has not been a residentof same for one year next
before his election, and who shall not be a qualified
voterof saidcity. A majority of saidboard of coun-
cilshall constitutea quorum for the transactionof
business. In case of a vacancy, from any cause, the
remaining members of the board shallhave power to
fillsaid vacancy by appointment for the unexpired
term .
§3 . That said board of council, and their successors
in office, shallbe a body -politicand corporate, under
the name and style of the " Board of Council " of
the cityof Flemingsburg ,and by that name shall be
capable in law of contractingand being contracted
with, of suing and being sued, of pleading and being
impleaded, of answering and being answered ,of de-
fending and being defended, in allcourts and in all
places, and shall have the power of perpetual suc-
cession; may use a common seal, and may do all
matters, acts and things which any other municipal

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 861
body -politicand corporate may lawfullydo and per-
form .
§ 4. That allof the male inhabitantsof said city
over twenty-one years of age, and who shall have
residedin the State one year, and in the city sixty
days next before any election, and who shall be
entitledto vote for State representative, shall be
qualifiedvoters of said city, and entitledto vote at
electionsfor members of said board of council, and
for all other officersof said city hereinafternamed ,
who are requiredto be voted forunder thisact.
§5 . That the members of said board of council,
before they proceed to act, shall each take an oath,
before some officerqualifiedto administerit,that he
will faithfully, and without favor or affectionto any
one,discharge the dutiesof his office, as well as take
thegeneraloath provided and requiredby the Consti-
tution of Kentucky . The officeradministeringsaid
oath shall furnish a certificateof the qualificationof
saidmember ,which shallbe filedwith the records of
said board . The board shall elect one of their own
body president,and in his absence they shallappoint
a presidentpro tem ., and in caseof his death,removal
from the city, or vacationof his seat,from any cause,
as a member of the board, they shall elect another
member of the board presidentin his stead.
§ 6. That the councilshall meet at leastonce every
two weeks ,and at such time and placeas it may des-
ignate, and oftener if emergency requires. Any
member failingto attend a meeting shallbe liableto a
fineof not exceeding two dollarsfor each offense, to
be recovered before the mayor on a warrant in the
name of the Commonwealth of Kentucky : Provided ,
That no member shall be fined if his failure to attend
iscaused by absence from the cityor sickness, or he
can give to the council satisfactoryexcuse for his
absence .
§ 7. That the members of the board of council shall
not be compelled to serve on juries in any of the

862 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
courts. The council shallkeep ,or cause to be kept,
ina well-bound book,a correctjournalof allitspro-
ceedings. It shall have power , by ordinance, to pre-
scribethe rulesof itsproceedings,to enforcethe same
by proper penalties; the compensation of each mem-
ber of the board of council shall not exceed twenty-
fivedollarsper year.
§ 8. That the council shall have power to enact
ordinances, by -laws, and make all orders, rules and
regulationsnecessaryto the preservationof the peace,
good order,government,management and thepreser-
vation of the health of the citizensof said city,and
to regulate the speed of riding or driving horses or
mules in saidcity,and may prescribeproper penalties
for the infractionof same ;but no ordinance shall have
the force of a law until the same shall have been pub-
lishedat leasttwice in some newspaper published in
the cityof Flemingsburg .
§ 9. That upon allquestionsvoted for by the board
of council,any member shall have the right to call
for the yeas and nays,and requirethe same to be re-
corded in the journal.
§ 10. That the council shall have power to pass
ordinances to prevent the introductionor spread of
contagious or infectiousdiseasesin the city; to pass
quarantinelaws for the purpose,and to enforcethe
same within one mile of the boundaries of the city ;
to establishhospitalsand pest-houses,and to make
rules for the management of same .
§ 11. That the council shall have power to build
bridges, culverts, sewers, and regulate the use of
same ; to provide for the lightingof the streets, pub-
licbuildings, rooms and officesof the city.
§ 12. That saidboard of council,for the use,bene-
fitand ornament of the city,shallhave the power to
acquirereal,personaland mixed estate,by purchase,
donation or otherwise, and shall have power to sell
and conveythe same,themajorityof theboard con

sentingthereto, the vote forand againstthe saleto be
speciallyenteredon the recordsof the board .

of said city,and to allthe real,personal and mixed
estate which it has ,or hereafter may acquire, shall

ing or the destruction of the same ; and the same

not exceeding fiftydollars, the mayor of the city, or

diction, subjectto appeal as in other cases.
§ 14. That the councilshall have power to clearthe

apparatus for the extinguishment of firesand for-
the keeping of the same in repair; and shall have

make allnecessaryrulesand regulationsfor the man-
agement of same .

:

Eighth . The jury, after being duly sworn by the
officerholding the inquest, shall view the land and
property of the owners ,and shall allow the faircash
value of the same ; also fair compensation for the
additionalfencing which may be required thereby
necessary, and also for all incidentalor collateral
damage which the said taking will produce to the
other land of the owners ; but for such incidental or
collateraldamage shall be deducted the amount in
value of the benefits and advantages such other land
will derive from the said condemnation and taking .
Ninth . If a person claiming damages has only an
estate for life, or years, and the remainder in fee
belongsto another, the jury shallapportion the dam-
ages between them .
Tenth. The verdict of the jury shall be, in sub-
stance,as follows: We ,the jury, findthat said—— ,
named in the writ herein, will sustaindollars
damages in taking the required land or property for
the purposes named ; and we have taken into consid-
eration, in making up our verdict,the faircash value
of the -- proposed to be taken, the benefitsand
damages to be sustainedby the constructionor erec-
tion of the ---
§ 22. That eitherparty may , within ten days after
the findingof the inquest, filea traverse with the
clerk of the circuitcourt,whereupon the case shall
be docketed for the next term , and shall stand for
trialat the term if it isin ten days afterthe inquest,
otherwise,at the next term . It shallbe triedby a
jury of freeholders empaneled for that purpose .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 869
The party failingon the traverseshall pay the costs
thereof,and the party traversingfailswhen the ver-
dict traversed is not increased or diminished as his
interestmay require. The costsof the inquestup to
the traverseshall be paid by the party asking the
condemnation : Provided,however,That ifthe amount
finallyrecovered be no greater than the amount ten-
dered before the applicationfor the writ, then the
same costs shall be paid by the owner or owners of
the land or property. Either party may have the de-
cisionrevisedin the Court of Appeals .
§ 23. No appeal or supersedeasor traverseon behalf
of the owner of the land or property shall stay the
judgment,or stop proceedingsthereunder,except as
provided herein.
§ 24. In allcases where a writ of ad quod damnum
shallbe issued,and a verdictfound under this act,
and the same be not traversed,the city(or itsrepre-
sentative) may tender to the owner of the land or
property,orpay intothecourtwhence thewritissued,
the amount of the verdictof the jury ; and thereupon
the said court shall make an order confirming the ver-
dict; and such confirmationshallvestthe rightto the
land or property in the city,and a writ of possession
may issueat any time therefor.
§ 25. When the findingof the jury is traversedby
the owner of the land or property, the city(or any
one representingit) may filea bond in the clerk's
officewhere the traverseis pending, conditioned for
the payment to the party entitledto the same of the
finalassessment of costs and damages in the case ;
said bond to be signed as vouchers of the city are now
signed,and to be binding on the city,but not to be
binding on the partiesindividuallywho may signit,
as mayor or clerk of said city,and pledge the city's
credit to its performance ; and the clerk of the said
courtshallnot requireany securityon saidbond ;and
upon the filingof same itshallbe lawful forsaid city
to proceed with work as though the damages were

870 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
finallyassessedand paid,and no actionfor trespass
shall lie therefor .
§ 26. When the damages for taking are settled on a
traversein the circuitcourt,the said court, upon a
tender being made in court of the said damages or
payment,or tenderthereoftotheowner,shallconfirm
the verdictof the jury,which confirmationshallvest
the land or property in the city,and a writ of posses-
sionmay issuethereforat any time.
§ 27. If the damages assessed are not paid before
the end of six months next ensuing verdict(unless
there is a traversepending),the applicant shall be
considered as having abandoned the prosecution of
the writ, and all former proceedings shall be of no
effect .
§ 28. Any person who shall hinder or attempt to
prevent the use of any rightor privilegederived from
the condemnation shall pay the city damages and
costs, and any additionalamount not exceeding one
thousand dollars; and any person who threatensso
to hinder by violence, may be brought before the
mayor of the citycourt, or county judge , or justice
of the peace,and be required to give bail, in the
penalty of one thousand dollars,with good security,
to keep the peace and be of good behavior for the
next six months .
§ 29. The writ provided for herein shallbe in sub-
stance as follows: The Commonwealth of Kentucky
to the sheriffof Fleming county , or marshal of the
city of Flemingsburg ,greeting: You are commanded
to summon and empanel ,on some day to be fixedby
you , a jury of twelve freeholders, not personallyin-
terestedin the matter,or relatedto the parties,and
citizensof the State, to inquire what damages , if
any , will sustainby reason of the city of
Flemingsburg taking the for the purpose of
thereof, and the
- , considering the convenience and advantages
the party will have by the
damages the party will sustain by reason of the

; and that you will,in all things,execute this
writ according to law, and make due return thereof
within three days of the execution of it. Witness
my hand this day of - , one thousand
eight hundred and ninety — .
§ 30. That the board of councilshallhave authority
to compel all turnpike companies ,whose roads run
intoor through said city,to keep that portionof their
roads which are within the corporate limits of the
city in repairand freefrom obstructions;and ifthey
failor refuse,afterten days' notice, to make said re-
pairs, or remove said obstructions, such turnpike
road company so failingand refusingshall be liable
to a fineof two dollarsand fiftycentsper day for the
time they permit theirsaid roads to remain out of re-
pair, or forthe time they failto remove said obstruc-
tions ; said fineto be recovered upon a warrant issued
by , and triedbefore,the mayor , if the sum is fifty
dollars and under, and if over that sum ,by indict-
ment before the grand jury in the Fleming Circuit
Court .
§ 31. That the board of council shallhave power to
make and establish rules and regulations for the en-
forcement of an honest and a faithfuldischarge of the
officialdutiesof allthe officersofsaid city,and pre-
scribe penalties for a failure of said officersto dis-
charge theirsaid duties honestly and faithfully, as
requiredby said rulesand regulationsand by this
act .
§ 32. That the council shall have power to appro-
priate money for the payment of the necessary ex-
penses of the government of saidcity,and allexpenses
which it may incur under this act; they may also
make appropriationsforpublic improvement , to-wit :
railroads, building and repairingsidewalks, and so
forth: Provided , The councilmay ,intheirdiscretion,
pay one-fourththe cost ifthe sidewalk be builtof
stone, and one-half the costof buildingor repairinga
sidewalk if the same be paved with brick, and no

hundred dollars, unless the same shall have been first

voted on at a time and place designated in the ordi-
nance proposing the appropriation;and ifthe proposi-
tionto make theappropriationshallreceivea majority

tion, then the council shall be authorized to make the
appropriation: Provided, That not more than one
appropriationbe made forany one purpose ,and that
no more than one thousand dollarsshall be appro-
priated in any one year .
§ 33. That in order to enable the council to raise the
necessary funds to pay the expenses of the city gov-
ernment ,to pay the expenses incurredunder thisact,
and to pay such otherappropriationsas itmay make ,
it shallhave the power to annually levy and collecta
per capitatax of not exceeding three dollarsof each
freemale inhabitantand citizenof said citytwenty-
one years old and over ; and any person who shall fail
to pay said tax by the firstday of October in the year
in which saidassessment ismade , shallpay a penalty
of ten per cent. in addition to the tax imposed ; and
ifsuch person shall stillfailto pay the same , after
being requested three times by the collectorof said
tax so to do ,such person shallthen be required, upon
fivedays' notice,by the marshal of the city, to work
upon the public streetsand alleys of the city, or
other publicworks , at the rateof one dollar per day
untilsuch tax and penalty is paid : Provided , That
the provisionsof this sectiondo not apply to persons
who are sick or physicallydisabled, or who are fifty
years old and over; and ifsuch persons failor refuse
to work when required and notifiedso to do by the
marshal of the city, such person shall be fined three
dollars, saidfineto be prosecuted upon a warrant is

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 878
sued by and triedbefore the mayor ; if such person
be convicted, and if said fineand costs are not paid
or replevied,the person so convictedshallbe confined
in jailand required to work upon the public streets
and alleys, or other public works of the city,at the
rateof one dollarper day until the fineand costs are
so paid ; the board of councilshall also have power
to annually levy and collectan ad valorem tax of not
exceeding fiftycents on each one hundred dollars'
worth of allthe taxable property in said citythat is
taxable under the laws of the State of Kentucky .
§ 34. That the board of councilshallhave power , in
their discretion, to license, tax and regulateall ven-
dors of nostrums, notions, patent or other medicines,
auctioneers, retailersof goods, coffee-houses, gift
stores, peddlers, insurance agents, brokers, public
halls,theatricaland otherexhibitions,shows ,circuses,
menageries , retailers of all manufactured articles of
whatsoever kind,and they may impose a licensetax
on any business, professionor occupation within the
limits of the city,but such licenseshall not be ex-
orbitant, and shall be in proportion to the articles
sold .
§ 35. That the council shallhave power to license,
tax and regulate, restrain, prohibit and control, in
said city,all taverns, retailersof spirituousliquors,
in case itmay hereafterbe lawful to sellthe same ,ten-
pin alleys, houses of public resort, billiard-tables,
pigeon-hole tables and bowling-alleys, in case the
same should be established: Provided ,That the tax
or licenseshall not exceed fivehundred dollarsper
annum ,and no licensegranted by said council shall
authorize the holders thereof to transact business un-
tilhe shall have obtained such other license as the
law may require, under county or State laws ; nor
shallany licenseauthorizeany person to transactbus-
inessin saidcityuntilthe citylicenseshallhave been
granted and paid for.
§36. That the citycouncilshallhave power to sup

874 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
press any licensegranted by them whenever , in its
judgment , the business so licensedby them shallbe-
come injuriousto the morals or interstsof the inhab-
itantsof saidcity; they shallhave power to suppress
gaming ,drunkenness ,gambling -houses,bawdy -houses,
or houses of prostitution, street-walkers, and disor-
derly houses of all kinds, and to provide for the

forthe punishment of any person who shallrent prop-
erty, or shallsuffertheirproperty to be used or occu-
pied by lewd or disorderlypersons,and may prescribe
by ordinance such penalty therefor as they think
proper .
$ 37. That the board of council shall have power
to prevent and suppress any riot, rout, breach of the
peace, noise, disturbance, disorderlyor unlawful as-
sembly in the streets, houses,or other places in the
city, and for the arrestand confinement of such per-
sons, who may engage in the same , in the jailof
Fleming county,and to provide for the trialof the

tingthe same ; saidcouncil shall have power to pre-
scribethe penalty for any violationof the city laws
or ordinances: Provided, That it shall not exceed

not exceeding one hundred dollars, or both ; and it
may provide that the persons so convictedshall be
required to work out their fineand costs, and the

and alleys,and other public works , at one dollarper
day , untiltheirfineand costsare paid and the term
of theirimprisonment has expired ; the councilshall
provide that all persons arrestedin said city shall
have a speedy trial; but if arrestedaftersix o'clock
post meridian, they may be confined by the officer
making the arrestuntil the next succeeding day in

ingcountyshallreceivethe partyunder arrestwith-
out an order of commitment , and safely keep him

until he is ordered to be brought before the mayor
for trial; ifthe jailerrefusesto receivesuch prisoner,
when in custody of the marshal of the cityor other
officerauthorized to make the arrest, he shall be fined
forsuch refusalnot lessthan fivenor more than fifty
dollars,said fineto be prosecuted by warrant before

§38 . That the council shall have power to define

shall constitutepeddling, and for the punishment of

§ 39. That theboard of councilshallhave power to

pike roads,and that at leasta space of twenty -five

§ 40. That the councilshallhave power ,and itshall
be itsduty , to annually appoint an assessor, who ,

form the duties of his office,shallcall upon the in-
habitants within the limitsof said city, and make
out a truelistof the taxable persons and property ;
and he shallassesssaidpropertyat itsfaircash value.
He shall have power to administer an oath to each

and saidlistshallembrace allrealand personal prop
erty as is assessablefor taxation under State laws ;
and in making a listof the taxableproperty of said
city, in the dischargeof his dutiesas such assessor,
he shallbe governed by State laws relativeto as-
sessorsunder the statutesof Kentucky . Said assessor
shall also have power to swear and examine witnesses

mon such witnessesbefore him , and may require,and

it shall be the duty of, the citymarshal to execute
the same ; and , in addition,such person so failingand

tend the time for said assessor to return his list; the
assessormay be paid for his servicessuch sum as the

§ 41. That the board of council shall have power to

and regulatehis duties and responsibilities; and he

§ 42. That the board of council shall have power ,

Provided , however, That should the council appoint

on his officialbond as such. It shallbe the duty of

authority,as isconferredupon sheriffsunder the laws

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 877
of the State of Kentucky in collectionof the State

sponsibilitiesand penalties. All taxesleviedby the

§ 43. That the board of council shallhave power ,

quired under thisact; and as such supervisors, they

as soon as the board of council, acting as a board

gether with an order directinghim to make out the

§ 44. That the board of council shall have power to
appoint a treasurerof the city, who shall not be a

in such penalty,as the council may deem proper and
as itmay approve ; he shallreceiveand receiptforall
moneys paid into the citytreasury; he shall keep a
correctrecord of the fiscalconcerns of the city,and
shallonly pay out money from the treasury on the
order of the council,countersignedby the clerkthere-
of ;he shallsettlehis accounts on or beforethe first

exhibit a statement of the condition of the city treas-
ury at such time, that is, the firstday of January ;
which statement shallshow the balance remaining in

878 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the treasury, the amount received during the past
year, from what source, the amount paid out, for
what purpose,and to whom ; and before he proceeds

his duties;he shallbe paid for such servicessuch sum
as the councilmay deem proper.
§ 45. That the councilshallhave power toappoint a

§ 46. That in order to enforcethe provisionsof this
charterand the ordinancesthereunder,and to provide
forcertaincriminaland civiljurisdictionshereinafter
named , a court ishereby established, under the name
and styleof " The City Court of Flemingsburg ;" said
courtshallconsistof a mayor ,and he shallbe styledthe
Mayor of the City of Flemingsburg ; his term of office
shallbe two years; the firstelectionfor mayor under

uary, one thousand eight hundred and ninety-one,
and every two years thereafter; but he shall hold

in case of a vacancy, from any cause, the board
of council shall have power to elect a person

the officeof mayor of saidcityunlesshe isa male citi-
zen of the same , at leasttwenty-one years of age, and
has been a bona fide resident of the city for at leastone
year next preceding his election; he shallhave allthe
civiland criminaljurisdictionwhich ,by existinglaws,
or laws that may be passed by the General Assembly
of Kentucky ,which have been , or may be,conferred

upon justicesof the peace; he shallhave jurisdiction
of all offenses under this charter and the ordinances
made in pursuance thereof; and , in addition thereto,
shallhave alljurisdictionin feloniesand misdemean-
ors, and the jurisdictionof allother cases which ,by
the Code of Practice and the General Statutes of Ken-
tucky ,is conferredespeciallyupon mayors of cities,
as such, and county judges. Before he enters upon
the duties of his officehe shall be required to execute
bond to the board of council, with such conditions
and securityas they may requireand approve ; upon
which bond any person aggrieved by the mayor ,by
failureto discharge his duty , shall have the same
rightof actionas isprovided by the General Statutes
against justicesof the peace and county judges, and
he shallbe subjectto likepenalties; and , in addition
to the oath requiredto be taken by the Constitution
of Kentucky ,he shallbe sworn to faithfullyand im-
partiallydischargethe dutiesof his office.
§ 47. That the mode of practiceand procedurein
the citycourt of Flemingsburg shallbe the same , and
regulated by the provisionsof the Code of Practice
and the General Statutes, as are applicable to the
mode of practiceand procedure of justice'sand
mayor's courts; and appeals from judgments of the
citycourtshallbe regulatedby the provisionsofthe
Code of Practicein regard to courtsof justicesof the
peace .
§ 48. That the feesof the mayor , for servicesin his
court,shallbe thesame asthefeesallowedjusticesof
the peace and clerks of the circuitcourts, for like
services,by the General Statutes: Provided , That in
allcaseswhere the amount in controversyisover fifty.
dollars, he shall be allowed the same fees as clerks of
the circuitcourt are allowed by the General Statutes.
§ 49. That themayor shallhave allthe power and
authorityas isconferredby the General Statutesof
Kentucky on justicesofthepeace,clerksofquarterly
and circuitcourt,for the collectionof theirfees,and

shallbe subjectto likeduties, responsibilitiesand
penalties.
§ 50. That , in the trial of all offenses committed
within the limitsof saidcity, whether the person so
tried is convicted or not, and where the fineand costs
are not collectedof the defendant, if the defendant
be convicted, the board of council may allow the
mayor and the marshal their fees for services ren-
dered in the trial of such offenders .
§ 51. That the mayor shallhave power to impose
fines in all cases of misdemeanors to the amount of
twenty dollars,without the interventionof a jury,
and shallhave the power to imprison the person so

and costsof prosecution are discharged by said im-
prisonment;but such imprisonment shall not operate

hisdiscretion,cause such fineand coststo be paid by

ersof a justiceof the peace to issue all the process

upon failureto attend,may award compulsory process
to compel theirattendance,and , in case his orders are

Provided , The finedoes not exceed ten dollarsand
imprisonment twelve hours.
§ 52. That allpersonsconvictedof any misdemeanor
of violatingany of the provisionsof this charter, or
of the by -laws or ordinances made in pursuance
thereofby the board of council,and who shallfailto
pay the finesand costsassessedagainstthem therefor,

public works of the city,or in the yard of the jailof

and be confinedin the jailat night,untilthe finesand
costsas aforesaidassessedthem are discharged at the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 881
rateof one dollarper day ; and the mayor shallhave
power to pass judgment or sentence againstall per-
sons so convicted, and the marshal of the cityshall

§ 53. That the mayor shallhave power to collect

ble on his officialbond ,and allfinescollectedby him
or by the city marshal, assessedagainstpersons for

§ 54. That at the election held for mayor under this
act, the person receivingthe highest number of votes
shallbe declared elected, and a certificateof his elec-
tionshallbe furnished him by the clerk holding the
same ; and upon his furnishingsaidcertificateto the
Governor of Kentucky , the Governor shall issue to
him a commission as mayor ,which commission , with

§ 55. That the mayor shall have power to fix the

able notice,shall be held on the fourth Saturday of

vided, That his court shall be open each day during

and order-book , in which he shall keep a record of
the witnesseswho may claim attendance in a cause ;

on : and in the order-book he shall keep a record of

all the orders made and judgments rendered in the
causes tried in his said court .

within the cityas the board of councilmay designate;
and all process from the citycourt,in criminaland
penal cases,under the Statelaws,shallbe in the name
of the Commonwealth , and allother proceedingsand
processshallbe in the name of the cityof Flemings-
burg ; they shallbe directed to the marshal of the
city, or any other peace officerof the Commonwealth
alsoprocessin civilcasesshallbe in the name of the
Commonwealth ,and be directedand made returnable

electionof a citymarshal ; no one shallbe eligibleto
the cfficewho isnot at leasttwenty-one years old,and

cate of election, which , upon his production to the

wise, as is now provided by the laws of Kentucky

from officeof the constables of this Commonwealth ;

this charter and the ordinances made thereunder are

the treasurerallfinescollectedby him , and take the

§ 58. That the marshal shallhave authorityto make
arrests without warrant for offenses committed in his
presence, and alsowhen he has reasonablegrounds to
believe that a public offense, or an offenseunder or
against thischarter, or any ordinance made thereun-

CHAPTER 1570 .
AN ACT forthe protectionof the Mammoth Cave and the Mammoth
Cave estate,in Edmonson county.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be unlawful for any person or
persons to destroy, injure or deface any shade, orna-
mental or other tree on the lands of the Mammoth
Cave estate, in Edmonson county, or to hunt on the
inclosedlands of said estate, or fishin any pond or
reservoir thereon , without the consent of the trustees
thereof or their agent ; or to enter said cave or any
othercave on said lands without such consent,or to
deface or injureany of said caves, or to remove any
thing therefrom without such consent.
§ 2. That any one violatingany of the provisionsof
sectionone shallbe deemed guiltyof a misdemeanor ,
and ,upon conviction,shall be fined for each offense
not lessthan ten dollarsnor more than fiftydollars.
§ 3. That this act shall take effect and be in force
from and after itspassage .
Approved May 15,1890.

Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Com-
