CHAPTER 1589 .
AN ACT to authorize the consolidation of the town of Chester with the
cityof Maysville,in Mason county .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of theCom-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the board of councilmen of the cityof
Maysville, and the town councilof the town of Ches-
ter, in Mason county, be, and they are hereby ,
authorized,each in itsorganizedcapacity,to pass or-
dinances preparatoryto the consolidationof the two
corporationsintoone,under the charterand laws and
ordinancesof the cityof Maysville,the town of Ches-
ter to be known as the sixthward of the cityof Mays-
ville; the said ordinances to set forth the plan of con-
solidation, and be submitted to a vote of the legal

970 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
voters of each corporation,givingat least ten days '
notice in one or more newspapers of Maysville, and
by written or printednoticesthereofstuck up at the
placesof votingineach corporation,and in such other
public places as may be deemed necessary, of the
time,placeand purposeof such elections; the result
thereofto be made and certifiedby the officershold-
ing the same to the councilsof each corporation at
the firstregular or called meeting thereafter.
§2 . That should a majority of the legalvotersin
each corporationvote in favor of the consolidation,
the factshallbe duly entered on the recordsof each
corporation,and then,by properordinances,thetown
of Chestershallbe merged intoand form part of the
city of Maysville,to be known as the sixth ward ;
and the councilmen therefrom shall thereafter be
elected at the same time and for the same terms as the
other councilmen for the city of Maysville; three
-councilmen of Chester , in officeat the time of consol-
idation, to be selectedby lot out of the six, to serve
as councilmen of said cityfrom the sixth ward ,until
the firstregular election after the expiration of their
respectiveterms,and untila successoris elected and
-qualified. Should the propositionfailto carry on the
firstsubmission, it may again be submitted to a vote
of the legal voters in the same manner as at first.
§3 . That should the consolidation of the two cor-
porations be consummated ,as herein provided, then,
thereafter,the charterand allthe laws and ordinances
of the cityof Maysville shall be in full force and
effectin said sixthward of the city, and the charter
and laws and ordinances of the town of Chester , from
that time forward, shallcease and be of no forceand
effect, except as the same may apply to actionsof the
past .
§ 4. That this act shall take effect and be in force
from and after itspassage .
Approved May 16,1890 .

CHAPTER 1590 .
AN ACT to authorizethe Lewis County Court to provide for the
erectionof a bridge over " ScaffoldLick Creek," in Quincy pre-
cinet .
Be it enacted by theGeneral Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the Lewis County Court,a majorityof the
justicesbeing presentand concurringthereto,ishere-
by authorized to provide for the constructionof a
bridge across " Scaffold Lick," in Quincy precinct.
To raisethe money to pay for said bridge, the Lewis
County Court, a majority of the justicesbeing pres-
ent,shallhave the power to levy a tax, not exceeding
twenty cents on each one hundred dollars' worth of
taxable property in the county , to be collectedby
the sheriff in the same manner as other taxes are col-
lected,and paid over as the county judge may direct,
and forwhich said sheriffshallbe responsibleon his
officialbond . The county court may issue bonds of
Lewis county sufficientto pay for said bridge, which
bonds shall bear six per cent.interestper annum from
date until paid,and the taxes so levied are pledged
for the redemption of said bonds and interest, and
the tax shallceasewhen saidbridge,costand interest,
isfullypaid. The bonds issuedshallbe sold at their
face value , and no discount shall be allowed in the
sale. The bonds shall be signed by the presiding
county judge,and attestedby the clerk, and in sums
of not less than one hundred dollars and not more
than one thousand dollars. The contractors may take
the bonds at theirfacevalue in payment forbuilding
the bridge. The county judge shallappoint a discreet
and suitable citizen as commissioner to let out the
contractand superintendthe building of saidbridge,
which contract shall not be binding until the same is
confirmed by the presiding county judge indorsed on
the contractand signedby him .
§ 2. This act shall be in effect from and after its
passage . Approved May 16, 1890 .

county,Kentucky ,toissuebonds of saidtown forthe improvement
ofthe town of Dover,and in aidofthe establishmentofmanufactures
in said town .

monwealth of Kentucky :

town , to be voted upon by them , the proposition:

ver to be given in aid of the general improvement of
the town of Dover , and to aid in the establishment of
manufactories in said town ? "

voted upon ,the said board of trusteesshall give ten

voting upon said propositionshall vote in favor of
issuingsaid bonds ,it shall be lawful forsaid board
of trustees to issue the bonds of said town in any

ing interestat not exceeding the rate of six per cent .
per annum ,and give the same in aid of the general
improvement of the town of Dover ,Kentucky , and to

CHAPTER 1593 .

well Turnpike Road Company ,and toauthorizea portionof Henry

the same .

described,are hereby declareda body -politicand cor-
porate,under the name and styleof the Kentucky
River, Port Royal and Hopewell Turnpike Company ;

sary or proper to carry out the objectof thisincorpo-
ration; and shallhave perpetualsuccession.

from the siteof old Palmyra Church to the Kentucky
river, and to run through the town of Port Royal,
and intersectwith the Kentucky river at Lane's Land-
ing.
§ 3. Said road bed shallnot be lessthan thirtyfeet
wide ,and the stoneor metal thereon shallnot be less
than twelve feetwide, to be thus placed around stone
not lessthan eightincheslong,and the same in width,
and from one and one -half to three inches thick ;
foundationnot lessthan eightinchesin depth,and
twelve feetin width. Said stone shallbe sound ,and
setup on edge,withpointswellbroken,fora founda-
tion. On top of this foundation shall be placed
napped stoneof a depth ofnotlessthanfour inches;
and such napped stoneshallbe broken smallenough to
passthrough a two-inch ring. On thisnapped stone
shall be placed gravel or dirtof a depth of not less
than one inch; and the bed of said road upon which
isplaced the stone foundation hereinmentioned shall

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 975
be in allplacesnot lessthansixincheshigherin its
center than on itssides,that is, it shallbe convex ,
the metal to be shouldered or supported by earth
thrown up againstthe sidesof said road. The grades

except the riverhill, and then not to exceed seven
degrees within the above limitation. The board of
directorsof said company may determine the manner
of constructingsaid road,and say whether foundation
shallbe paved , except riverhill,which shallbe paved,
and locate the same ; but itmust be located within the
boundary of the civildistricthereinaftermentioned as
the Kentucky River,Port Royal and Hopewell Turn-
pike district. Wherever there isa pike at presenton
thisroad, the directors,ifthey deem itpractical, may
use the old bed by making it twelvefeet wide, and
placing enough metal thereon to make it as good a
pike as elsewhere, and said road which istobe leftto
the board of directors to accept; the river hill at
Lane's Landing is to be sixteen feet wide together
with the above mentioned specifications.
§ 4. The capitalstock of saidcompany shallnot ex-
ceed fifteen thousand dollars .
§ 5. The followingnamed persons are declared tobe
the directors: W. L. Nuttall,Frank Peniston,Ashby
Bullock, L. M. Sanford, James Matthews , P. J.
Honaker , Junior , T. J. Bourne , M. B. Perry , and
J. W. Jones . The above -named directors are to act
in this capacity for one year from the time of the vot-
ing upon thisact by the tax-payers in the above men-
tioned districts; said directors, after the firstyear,
are to be electedby the tax-payers of the aforesaid
district,at such placewithin saiddistrict,and at such
time as the board of directorsmay select; but printed
or writtennoticesof such electionmust be posted in
severalplaces in said districtbefore said electionis
held. There must be an electioneach year fordirect-
ors; majority of the directorsshall transact busi-
ness, and shallbe convened at any time by order of

976 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the president; none but the tax-payers in thisdistrict

as president,and one as secretary,and one as treas-
urer of said road ; only the treasurershall receive
any compensation for any servicesrendered, and his
compensation shall not exceed two per cent. of the

ditioned for the faithful performance of his duties
and the safe-keeping and proper disbursement of the
funds which may come into his hands ; said bond

986 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 1595 .
AN ACT toincorporateOilCity, in Barrencounty.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
ARTICLE I.
Boundary and Trustees .
§1 . That Oil City, in Barren county, be, and the
same is hereby, incorporated. The corporate limits
of said city shall include all the territory, streets,
avenues, alleys, buildings and improvements within
the following boundary , to wit : Beginning in the
center of Beaver creek, and running thence north
fifty-seven degrees, thirtyeast to elm and sycamore ,
on the north bank of said creek,corner to the tract
of land formerly owned by Burr Sims ; thence with
the linesof the same ,on the north-west sidethereof,
as follows, viz: north fifty-seven degrees, thirtymin-
utes easttwo hundred and fifty-three and two tenths
poles to a sycamore ,at the water-gap ; thence north
sixty-six degrees, forty-fiveminutes eastone hundred
and sixty-six feetto a ditch; thence with said ditch
north twenty -four degrees , fifteen minutes east six
hundred and twenty-one feet to a stake in a road ;
thence with said road north eighty-nine degrees,
forty-fiveminutes east one hundred and thirty-five
poles to a culvert ; thence north forty-two degrees ,
thirtyminutes eastsix hundred and thirty-three feet
to a stake in the southern limitof the rightof way of
the Glasgow branch of the Louisvilleand Nashville
Railroad ; thence north nineteen degrees, thirtymin-
utes east six hundred and twenty -seven feet with the
lineof the tractof land formerly owned by Jackman ,
to a stake in a branch ; thence south eighty-seven de-
grees,forty-five minutes eastseven hundred and nine-
ty-two feet to a beech ; thence north three degrees

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 987
east two hundred feet; thence,leaving the Jackman
lines, south sixty-nine degrees, thirty minutes east,
remaining the same distance from said railroad, to
the most easterlyline of the tractof land formerly
owned by J. C. Shipley; thence with said easterly
linesouth eightdegrees,forty-fiveminutes eastto the
center of Beaver creek ; and thence down said creek
to the beginning .
§2 . That J. Ritter, W. L. Porter, W. B. Smith ,
Jas. C. Evans and Moses Sweetser be, and they are
hereby , appointed trusteesof saidcity,and shallhold
their officesuntil the firstSaturday in May , one thou-
sand eighthundred and ninety-one,and untiltheirsuc-
cessorsareduly electedand qualified. Said trustees
shallcause a platof saidcityto be made and recorded
in the Barren County Court clerk'soffice.
ARTICLE II .
Officersand Elections .
§ 1. That the prudential,fiscaland municipal pow-
ers of said cityshallbe vestedin a board of council-
men , to consistof a mayor and four councilmen, to
be known and styled the Council of Oil City, which
council and itssuccessorsshallbe a body -politicand
corporate;and by said name shallbe capable of con-
tractingand being contractedwith,suing and being
sued, and of doing allacts which a body -politicand
corporate, having perpetual succession, can, in the
exerciseof itspowers,lawfullyand rightfullydo .
§ 2. The other officersof said cityshallconsistof a
policejudge, marshal,clerk and attorney; and when
the board of councilmen may deem the same neces-
sary, it may appoint or employ a civilengineer to
prescribethe grade for streets, avenues, alleys, side-
walks , and for buildings or other improvements in
said city. The clerk shallbe ex officiotreasurerand
assessor. The marshal shall be ex officiocollectorof
taxes .

988 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 3. The firstelection under this act for mayor and
councilmen shallbe on the firstSaturday in May , one
thousand eighthundred and ninety-one ; the persons
then elected shall continue in officeuntil the firstSat-
urday in May , one thousand eight hundred and
ninety-two, and until their successors shall duly
qualify. The mayor and councilmen chosen on the
firstSaturday in May ,one thousand eight hundred
and ninety-two, shallhold theirofficesone year, and
untiltheirsuccessorsqualify; and an annual election
shallbe held on the firstSaturday in May for the
election of said officers.
§ 4. No person shall be eligibleto the officeof
mayor , policejudge, marshal or clerk, unless he is a
male citizenof this Commonwealth , over the age of
twenty-one years,and has been a residentof said city
sixty days next preceding his electionor appoint-
ment . No person shallbe eligibleto the officeof city
attorney, unlesshe is a male citizenof this Common-
wealth , over the age of twenty -one years , and a resi-
dent of said city sixty days next preceding his
appointment ,nor unless he has been a licensedprac-
ticingattorneyfortwo years under the laws of this
Commonwealth . No person shallbe eligibleto the
office of councilman unless he is a male citizen of this
Commonwealth , over the age of twenty -one years, a
freeholder, and has been a residentof said citysixty
days next precedinghiselectionor appointment .
§ 5. No person shall be eligibleto vote in said town
for any officerthereof unless he is a male citizen of
thisCommonwealth ,over the age of twenty -one years,
and has been a residentof said city sixty days next
preceding the election; nor shall any person be eligi-
ble to hold any office,or to vote at any election of
said city, on any question that may be submitted to
the voters thereof, untilhe has paid his city taxes
levied for the year preceding the one in which the
electionis held, and allfines, penaltiesor forfeitures
adjudged againsthim due said city.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 989
§ 6. The cityclerkand attorneyshallbe electedby
the council annually, at the firstmeeting after its
election,or appointmentwhere thereis a failureto
elect .
§7. A policejudge shallbe electedby the votersof
said cityon the firstMonday in August ,one thousand
eighthundred and ninety,and every four years there-
after, to hold his officefor a term of four years, and
untilhis successoriselectedand qualified.
§ 8. A citymarshal shallbe electedby the votersof
said cityon the firstMonday in August ,one thousand
eight hundred and ninety,and every two years there-
after, to hold his officefora term of two years,and
until his successoriselectedand qualified. He shali
qualify and give bond before the Barren County
Court on the firstMonday in September following
his election .
§ 9. The term of officeof the presenttrusteesshall
expirewhen the mayor and councilmen electedunder
the firstelectionprovided for in sectionthree of this
articleshall have qualified, and the clerk, treasurer
and attorneyof said city may be appointed by said
trustees,and shallhold theirofficestillthefirstmeeting
of the council elected to succeed said trustees; and the
clerk and attorneythen electedshallhold theiroffices
untilthe firstmeeting of the council electedin May ,
one thousand eight hundred and ninety-two . The
said trusteesmay also appoint a police judge and
marshal , who shall hold their officesuntil the first
electionin August ,one thousand eight hundred and
ninety ; and any vacancy occurring in the officeof
mayor ,councilman, policejudge,marshal, attorneyor
clerk, shallbe filledby appointment of the said trus.
teesor council,and the appointment shallbe tillthe
next regular election, held for the electionof said
officers,and until their successorsthen chosen shall
qualify.
§ 10. If no electionis held for mayor and council-
men in saidcityon the regularelectionday fixedby

930 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
this act, it shall be the duty of the Barren County
Court to fix a day for such election, to be held within
sixty days after the regular electionday .
§ 11. The electionforall officersof said cityshall
be held at one voting place, convenientlylocatedfor
the votersin said city. It shall be the duty of the
clerkto cause to be advertised,forten days beforeany
electionby the voters of saidcity, insome newspaper
published in said cityor Barren county, or if none ,
by printedposters in fiveor more publicplaces in the
city, the time and place of said election,and what
officers are to be elected thereat .
§12. The Barren County Court shall, at itsregular
term in April,one thousand eighthundred and ninety-
one,and annually thereafterat saidterm,appoint two
judges of electionsand a clerk thereof in said city,
who shall be voters therein, and shall continue in
officeone year. The marshal,by himself or deputies,
shallperform the same dutiesas to electionsheld in
the cityas is required by law of sheriffsat general
elections; and the provisionsof the General Statutes,
so far as the same is defined,the duties of judges,
clerks and sheriffsof elections, and so far as the same
is not inconsistentwith thisact, shall apply to and
regulate the duties of the election of officersof said
city; and each judge,clerkor marshal of the election
held in said cityshallbe entitledto one dollar per
day , to be paid out of the citytreasury.
§ 13. That when two or more candidates for any
electiveofficeshall have an equal number of votes for
the same office, the electionshall be determined by
lot,in such manner as the councilmay direct,and in
the presence of the counciland at leastthreevotersof
the city.
§ 14. The poll-books of the cityshall be returned
within twenty-four hours after the closeof the polls
to the cityclerk,who shall, on the second day after
the election, together with the mayor and marshal ,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 991 .
count the votesfor the severalcandidates, and deliver
certificates, signed by them ,to the candidateselected.
§ 15. The trustees aforesaid shall exercise the same
powers granted to the council untilthe firstboard of
councilmen iselectedand qualified; and the clerk, at-
torney,policejudge and marshal,appointedby said
trustees, shall, as far as possible, exercisethe same
powers granted to those officersrespectivelyunder
thisact,untiltheir successorsare chosen and qual-
ified .
§ 16. Any person who votesin saidcityat any city
electiontherein, who is not qualifiedto vote by this
act, or who attempts to exercisethe duties of any
officeto which he is ineligibleby this act,shall be
guiltyof a misdemeanor ,and fined in any sum not
less than twenty -five nor more than one hundred dol-
lars,to be recoveredbeforethe Oil City PoliceCourt.
§ 17. All electionsby the qualifiedvoters of said
cityshallbe held between the hours of seven o'clock
in the morning and fiveo'clockin the evening ; but
the officersof the electionmay adjourn from time to
time during theday ,not exceeding threehours at any
one time ; and the council shalljudge of the elec-
tions, qualificationsand returnsof itsmembers ; and
in cases of contested electionsof any city officerelect-
iveby the votersof the city,the same shallbe heard
and determined by the council, under such rules as
may be prescribedby the by -laws or ordinances of
said city.
§ 18. The clerk shall certifyto the Secretary of
State the name of the person elected or appointed
policejudge of saidcity,and the Governor shallissue
a commission to said officerupon such certificate.
ARTICLE ΙΙΙ .
Powers and Duties of Officers.
§ 1. Every person electedor appointed to an execu-
tive,judicialorotherofficeunder thisactshall,before-

992 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
he enters on the duties of his office, take and sub-
scribean oath,before some officerauthorized to ad-

and to be faithful and true to the laws of this Com-
monwealth , and especiallythose pertaining to the

saidoaths in his officeas a part of the recordsthereof.
§2 . The marshal shall, previous to entering on the
dutiesof his office, execute a bond , with two or more
good and solventsureties,before, and to be approved
by , the Barren County Court, under the same restric-
tionsand requirementsof the law as in case of sheriffs.
The clerk of the said court shall filesaid bonds in his
office, and preserve the same among the records
thereof .
§3 . The clerk cf saidcityshall alsoexecute bond ,
with two or more good and solvent sureties, before
the Barren County Court, to the Commonwealth of
Kentucky , conditioned that he will well and truly
perform his duties under this act, and pay over any
money which may come to his hands as ex-officio
treasurer to the person or persons entitled thereto,
and in accordance with the orders of the council of
Oil City. Said bond shallalso be filedby the clerk
of said court in his office, and be preserved among
the recordsthereof. All feesofthe county court clerk
under thisactshallbe paid out of the citytreasury.
The cityclerk shallpay out no money in his hands
as treasurer,except upon thewarrant of the mayor ,in
pursuance of the order of the council;and saidclerk
shallannually, in the month of April, prior to the
annual election, make out and publish in some news-
paper of said city or county, or if none , in at least
one hundred printed handbills, a fulland complete
statementof all taxes, fines, penalties, forfeitures,
licensefees,or other moneys , which have come to his
hands as treasurerof said city, up to and including
the thirtiethday of April in the precedingfiscalyear,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 993
and of alldisbursements by him ,to whom made ,and
for what purpose ; and any balance in his hands at
the expirationof his term of officeshallbe paid over
to his successor. He shallreceiveallmoneys belong-
ing to the city, and keep an accurate account of all
receiptsand expendituresin such manner as the coun-
cilmay direct,which account shallalways be open to
the mayor and councilmen .
§ 4. That the clerk, as assessor, shall perform all
the dutieswhich the councilmay prescribein relation
to the assessment of property, for the purpose of
levying the taxes imposed by the council.
§ 5. The clerkshallkeep the corporate sealof said
city, if therebe one ; make and keep a record of the
proceedings of the council,at whose meetings itshall
be his,and the marshal's and attorney'sduty to at-
tend ; and copiesof allpapers duly filedin his office,
and transcriptsfrom the records of the proceedings
of the council certifiedby him , shallbe evidence in
all the courts of this Commonwealth .
§ 6. The mayor shall preside over the meetings of
the council,and ,in case of a tie,shallhave the cast-
ing vote. He may callmeetings of the councilwhen ,
in his opinion,the interestof the citydemand it,or
any three councilmen ,or any fivevoters, in writing,
request it; and itshallbe his furtherduty to see that
all ordinances, rules, regulationsand by -laws of the
cityare faithfullyexecuted. He shallhave power to
perform the dutiesof policejudge in allcriminaland
penal actions under the general or city laws when
there is a vacancy in the officeof policejudge, or
when the said judge is absent, sick or unable to
attend to the duties of his office from other cause .
He shall have the same fees allowed the judge in
such cases,and shallaccount for allmoneys received
forfines,as isrequiredof the policejudge.
§ 7. The mayor and two councilmen shallconstitute
a quorum for the transactionof ordinary business;

§ 10.The councilof Oil City,or any tax-payer,or

other person interested, may institutea suit on the

tively, before the police court, or other court of a

§ 11. The mayor and councilmen shallreceive,after

dred and ninety-one, an annual compensation of ten

erallaws shallbe paid to the trusteeof the jury fund

erty,papersand effectsofeverydescriptioninhispos

ages caused by his neglector refusalso to deliver, to

ARTICLE IV .
Police Court .
§1 There shallbe establishedin said citya court
to be styled the Oil City Police Court, to be held by
the policejudge of Oil City.
§ 2. The said policecourtshallhave exclusiveorig
inal jurisdictionof all violationsof the ordinances.

996 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and by -laws of said city, and concurrent jurisdiction
with the Barren county judge or Barren Quarterly
Court in all criminal,penal and civilcases,and pro-
ceedingsarisingwithin the limitsof said city. In all
civilactions,where the amount in controversy, exclu-
siveof interestand cost,exceeds fiftydollars,the ac
tion shall be by petitionand summons ; but before
any summons is issued, the plaintiffshallpay to the
policejudge a tax of fiftycents,to be taxed as costs,
and accounted for by the said judge on his official
bond to the trusteeof the jury fund of Barren coun-
ty. He shallhold his courtsat some convenient place
in said city, to be provided by the said trusteesor
city council, and for the trialof civilcases shall hold
his courtsquarterlyon such days as he may fixupon
in his judgment book . Notice of the days on which
he shallbegin his terms for the trialof civilactions,

fiveor more public placesin the city,but no altera-

in any one year. The marshal shallbe allowedthe same

Court, at itsregular terms for the trialof civilcases,
to be paid by the citytreasurer,as are allowed by law
to the jailerforlikeservicesin the county court. He
shall be allowed his regular fees for all services in
feedingprisonersplaced in the cityor county jail,for
violationsof cityordinances,or for a failureto pay
finesand costsfor such violations,to be paid him by
the city .
§ 3. The saidpolicecourt shallbe a court of record,
and the judge thereofshallact as clerkof the same ;
and certifiedcopiesof the recordsof said court,over
the signature of the judge, shallbe evidence in any
court of this Commonwealth . Said court shall have
no appellatejurisdiction; and alljudgments of said
courtin criminaland penalcasesunder the general

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 997
laws, where the fineimposed does not exceed fifteen
dollarsor the imprisonment ten days ,or for any vio
lation of a city ordinance , or in civilcases where the

clusive; but in allothercasesan appeal may be taken,
under the provisionsof the Civiland Criminal Codes
of Practice, within sixtydays after the renditionof
the judgment ; which appeal shall be taken directly
to the Barren Circuit Court .
§ 4. That allprocessissuedby the policejudge shall
be in the name of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ;
and where such process relatesto the violation of a
city ordinance it shall be in the name of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky for the use of the council of
Oil City. The process in civilcases, or in criminal
and penal cases,under the general laws, may be re-
turnable to saidpolicecourt, or to any magistrate of
said county : but in casespertainingto a violationof
a cityordinance the processmust be made returnable
to said police court. The marshal shall execute all
process from said court, unlessotherwise directedby
the party or his attorney, in whose behalfthe same is
procured ; which directionmay be verbal.
§ 5. That allfines,penaltiesor forfeituresimposed
by or arisingfrom casesinstitutedinsaidpolicecourt,
for a violationof a city ordinance, shall, when col-
lected, be paid into the citytreasury, and allothers
shallbe paid to the trusteeof the jury fund of Bar-
ren county .
§ 6. The defendant shallhave the rightto replevy,
for three months , any judgment for any fineor for-
feiturein said policecourt,or any capias pro fineor
fierifacias issued thereon, by giving good security;
and a return of nulla bona on a capias pro fine or
fierifacias, issuedon any judgment in the police
court, shallauthorizean attachment against the per-
sonal property of the defendant, or processto subject

998 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
his choses in action or other available effects to the
satisfactionof such judgment .
§ 7. That all persons adjudged to pay finesin the
Oil City PoliceCourt,who do not pay or replevy the
same ,shallbe forthwithconfinedin theBarren county
or cityjailuntilthe finesand costsof prosecutionas-
sessedagainstthem are dischargedat the rate of two
dollars per day for each day's confinement; but the
council shallhave power to compel any ablebodied

or replevy finesimposed by said policecourt, or on a
judgment of imprisonment by said court,to work out
said finesand costsunder the supervisionof the mar-
shal, or otherperson appointed by the council, on any
of the streets,alleysor publicgronds of saidcity,and
allow such persons one dollar per day for each day's
work as aforesaid; or in case of a judgment of im-
prisonment,allow the prisonerforeach day's work a
credit of one dollar on the judgment . In all cases
where the defendant works out the costs adjudged
against him , the officersto whom said costs are due
shallbe allowed the same out of the citytreasury.
§ 8. The policejudge shallbe entitledto the same
feesas the judge of the Barren QuarterlyCourt,or as
the clerk of the circuit court for similar services .
§ 9. In case of the absence from the city, sickness,
death or resignationof the policejudge and mayor ,or
a vacancy in said offices,the presidingjudge of the
Barren County Court, or the nearest justiceof the
peace of said county,may hold saidpolicecourt with
allthe powers of the policejudge.
§ 10. The marshal shall arrestany person who is
drunk and disorderlyin said city, in the day or night
time , with or without a warrant ; and it shall be his
duty to place such person in the cityor county jail
without a mittimus,untilhe isduly sober,when he
shallbring him beforethe policecourt fortrial. Any
disorderly person in said city at night shall be ar-
restedby the marshal or his authorizeddeputy ,with

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 999
or without a warrant or mittimus,and placed in the
saidjailuntilthe next morning ,when itshallbe his
duty to bring such person beforethe policecourt.
§ 11. Any failureof the marshal to discharge the

ject him to a fine of not less than twenty -five nor
more than one hundred dollars,to be recovered before
the policecourt, in which case the processmay be di-
rected to and executed by the sheriff, jailer,or any
constable of said county .
ARTICLE V.
General Powers and Duties of the Council .
§ 1. The said trustees, and when electedas herein
provided, the council shall have stated meetings,
and shall have the management and control of the
finances, and all the property, real, personal, or
mixed , owned by the corporation.
§ 2. The council shallhave power , within the cor-
porate limits,by by -laws or ordinances:
First. To restrainand prohibitall descriptionof
gambling and fraudulentdevices,and allplayingwith
cards or dice, or at other games of chance whereat
any thing of value isbet,won ,or lost, and to prohibit
and punish allparticipantsin any raffleor lottery.
Second . To prohibitthe sellingof any spirituous,
vinous, or malt liquors by any shop-keeper, hotel,
tavern or innkeeper, druggist, trader, grocer, mer-
chant, or other person in any shop , hotel, tavern,
store,grocery,out-house,yard, garden,or other place
within said town , or within three miles of the coгро-
rate limits .
Third . To prohibitthe sellingor giving away of
ardent spiritsor other intoxicating liquors to any
minor or apprentice, without the consent of his or
her parent, guardian, master or mistress,and to pro-
hibit the sale or giving away of such liquors to in-
ebriates or habitual drunkards .

1000 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Fourth . To licenseand regulateshop-keepers,hotel,
tavern and innkeepers, druggists, traders, grocers
and keepers of ordinariesor victualing or other
houses .
Fifth . To license, tax, regulate, suppress and pro-
hibit billiardtables, pigeon-hole, Jenny Lind , and all
similartables, pin-alleys, nine or ten-pin alleys, ball-
alleysand shooting-galleries.
Sixth . To tax , licenseand regulate auctioneers and
pawnbrokers , and tax or lay duties on the sale of
goods at auction .
Seventh. To license, tax and regulate hawkers and
peddlers,and all itinerantvendors of medicines or
othergoods,wares or merchandise, whether at auction
or otherwise .
Eighth . To regulate,license,suppress and prohibit
all exhibitions of common showmen , shows of every
kind by itinerantand other persons or companies ,
exhibitions of natural or artificialcuriosities, cara-
vans,circuses, theatricalperformances,and all other
exhibitions and amusements .
Ninth . To prevent and suppress any riot, rout,
breach of the peace, noise, or disturbance,or disor-
derly assemblage.
Tenth. To suppress and restraindisorderlyhouses.
Eleventh . To establish and regulate markets and
other public buildings,and provide for the erection
and determine their location, and make crossings
across streets .
Twelfth. To regulate,licenseor prohibit butchers,
and to revoke their license for mal -conduct in the
course of trade .
Thirteenth. To regulatethe keeping and conveying
gunpowder and other combustible and dangerous
materials .
Fourteenth. To prevent horse-racing, immoderate
ridingor drivingin the streets,and to authorizeper-
sons so ridingor drivingto be stopped by any person,
and to compel persons to fasten theirhorses, mules ,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1001
oxen or other animals attached to vehicles, or other-
wise,while standing or remaining in the streets.
Fifteenth. To prevent the encumbering the streets,
sidewalks,lanes,alleys and publicgrounds with car-
riages,carts,sleighs,sleds,wheelbarrows, boxes,lum-
ber,timber,firewood,brick,stone,post-awnings, signs
or any substance or material whatever .
Sixteenth. To prevent any obscene, indecentor pro-
fane exhibitions or conduct .
Seventeenth. To restrainand punish vagrants and
prostitutes.
Eighteenth. To restrain, regulate or prohibit the
running at largeof horsesand swine or other domestic
animals, and authorize the distraining, impounding
and sale of the same for the penalty incurred and
the costof the proceedings; and alsoto impose penal-
ties on the owners of such animals for a violation of
any by -laws or ordinancein relationthereto.
Nineteenth . To prevent and regulate the running
at large of dogs , and to authorize the destruction of
the same when at large contrary to the by laws or
ordinances .
Twentieth. To prevent and regulatethe playing of
ball, flyingof kites, or any other amusement or prac-
ticehaving a tendency to annoy persons passing on
the streetsor sidewalks, or to frighten teams and
horses .
Twenty -first. To make regulationsto prevent the
introductionof contagiousdiseasesintothe city.
Twenty -second . To have exclusive power over the
streets,avenues,alleysand sidewalks,and remove and
abate any obstructionsand encroachments thereinor
thereon;and shallhave fullpower to receiveby deed,
dedications or donations of lands for streets, avenues ,
alleysor other public purposes, or to purchase said
lands forsuch purposes,on such terms and conditions
as may be agreed upon with the owners thereof; and
shallhave power and authorityto condemn forstreets,
avenues,alleys,sitesforpublicbuildingsand grounds

1002 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the feesimple title, or any lessestate, in any lands
deemed necessaryfor such purposes by said trustees,
or the citycouncil, in the manner as required by an
act, entitled"An act to prescribethe mode of con-
demning land for the use of railroadand turnpike
companies," approved April eleventh, one thousand
eighthundred and eighty-two .
Twenty -third. To abate and remove nuisances,and
punish the authors thereofby penaltiesof fineand
imprisonment, and to defineand declarewhat shallbe
deemed nuisances, and authorizethe summary abate-
ment thereof .
Twenty -fourth. To appoint watchmen and police-
men , and prescribetheirduties,power and pay .
Twenty -fifth. To provide the town with water in
any manner they may deem proper, and to establish,
make and regulatepublic pumps , wells and cisterns.
Twenty -sixth. To inspectchimneys , fluesand stove-
pipes in allbuildings, and have them removed or re-
paired at the owner's expense,when the publicsafety
requires it.
Twenty -seventh. To do allacts,and make allregu-
lationswhich may be necessaryor expedient for the
preservationof health and the suppressionof disease.
Twenty -eighth. To make , publish, ordain, amend
and repeal all such ordinances, by -laws and police
regulations,not contrary to the Constitutionof this
State or of the United States,for the good govern-
ment and order of said city, and the trade and com-
merce thereof, as may be necessary or expedient to
carry into effectthe powers vested in the council, or
any officerof said city by this act, and enforce ob-
servance of allrules, regulations,by -laws and ordi-
nances made in pursuance of thisact by penalties
not exceeding, in any case, one hundred dollars' fine
and thirtydays'imprisonment forany offenseagainst
the same . In all cases where the fine may exceed
sixteen dollarsor the imprisonment five days , the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1003
defendant may demand , and shall be entitledto a
jury, beforethe policecourt.
Twenty ninth. To provide for the use of said town
a fire-engine, organizea firecompany ,and make nec-
essary regulationsfor the prevention of damage by
fire .
Thirtieth. To prohibitand punish insurance agents
doing businessin saidtown from enteringintocombi-
nations to fix minimum rates of insurance on the
property in said city for the purpose of preventing
competition .
ARTICLE VI .
Taxation .
§ 1. That all dwelling-houses, business houses,
store-houses,warehouses,hotels,shops,offices,stables,
barns,cribsor other buildingsadjacent to either, to-
gether with any inclosedlotor lotsupon which any
such improvements may be situated, and all vacant
lots of land now or which shall hereafter be laid off for
the purpose of erectingany such buildingsthereon,in-
cluding all yards, gardens,stable-lots,orchards and
vineyards,shall be subject to taxation for citypur-
poses in saidcity, except such property as is exempt
by the third section of this article.
§2 . That allpersonalor mixed property owned by
the citizensof said city,and all personal or mixed
property located in said cityowned by persons not
citizensof said city, subjectto Statetaxation, includ-
ing that under the equalizationlaws,shallalsobe sub-
jectto taxationfor citypurposes.
§3 . That there shall be exempt from taxation
under thisact for citypurposes all property belong-
ing to and owned by said city, Barren county, the
State of Kentucky , the United States, any church,
seminary,college, or public school,and all grounds
attached theretodedicated solelyto the uses thereof,
and alsoallpastureor agriculturallands; but in case
any road or street,frontingor running through pas

1004 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
ture or agriculturallands,is graded or macadamized
by the council,then such lands, to the depth of one
hundred and fifty-eight feet from and frontingupon
said road or street so graded or macadamized ,shall
be listedand taxed as other taxable property in said
city,to aid in the payment of the expense incurred
by such grading or macadamizing .
§ 4. The taxable property of said cityshall be as-
sessed at itsmarket value, in currency, on the first
day of May in each year. The assessorshall make
his assessmentbetween the tenth day of May and the
tenthday of June in each year,and ,when completed,
shall return the same in such manner as shall do
justiceand secure equalityto all, as far as possible,
giving ten days' public noticeof the time of theirre-
vision .
§ 5. That in lieuof a citypoll-tax, the council shall
have power to require, and itishereby made the duty
of, every able-bodied male inhabitantof saidcityover
sixteenand under fiftyyears of age, except ministers
of the gospel, to labor two days in each year upon the
streetsor alleysof said town,under the supervision
and directionof the marshal or a streetcommissioner,
to be appointed by the council,and furnishsuch tools
as may be required, on two day's verbalnoticeby the
marshal or commissioner ; but every such person may ,
at his option, insteadof laboring,pay into the treas-
ury of saidcity,at the rateof one dollarper day for
each day he may be required to work : Provided ,
Such payment be made beforethe firstday of labor is
required. But all persons over fiftyyears of age in
said city, not exempted by the council, shall pay a
poll-tax of two dollarsper annum as long as such im-
provement is required by the council. In default of
payment or labor as aforesaid,the sum of four dollars
and cost of suit before the police court shall be recov-
ered by the councilagainsteach person so in default,
unless excused by order of the council for good cause
shown . Any and all persons who are so required to

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1005
labor shall be exempt from working on any road in
the county outside of the corporate limits as desig-
nated by this act. The marshal, if he acts, or the
streetcommissioner appointed by the council, shall
be exempt from said tax and labor, and may be
allowed a reasonable compensation in addition for his
services .
§ 6. It shallbe the duty of the council to levy and
collect a tax of not less than twenty -five nor more
than fiftycents on each hundred dollars' worth of
taxable property of saidcity,afterthe year one thou-
sand eighthundred and ninety,annually,forthe term
of fiveyears,which tax shallbe applied to the grad-
ing and macadamizing of such of the streetsof said
cityas the council may direct,and to no other pur-
pose whatever ; and may levy and collecta tax of not
more than twenty -fivecents on each one hundred dol-
lars' worth of taxableproperty for publicschoolpur-
poses .
§ 7. After the expiration of the time mentioned in
the next preceding section,the same shall stillbe in
forceforany time, not exceeding fiveyears,if a ma-
jorityof the legalvotersof saidcityso directatan
electionto be held for that purpose ; and the said
sectionmay continue in force from time to time after
that as oftenand as long,not exceeding fiveyears at
any one time, as a majorityof the legalvotersof said
citymay directat electionsheld forthat purpose.
§ 8. The council shalldirect,by proper orders en-
tered upon the recordsof the city,when , how and by
whom the electionsprovided forin the next preceding
sectionshallbe held ;but shallgive ten days' printed
notice, posted in two or more public places in said
city, of the time and place of holding any such elec-
tion .
§ 9. The preceding three sectionsare not to apply
to the sidewalks of said city, nor are they to prevent
the collectionof other lawful taxes forpurposes other

1006 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
than grading and macadamizing the streetsof said
city.
§ 10. That the councilmay ,insteadof adopting the
laborand streetpoll-tax plan, as provided for in sec-
tion fiveof this article, levy and collecta poll-tax,
not exceeding two dollars,on each male person resid-
ing in said city over the age of sixteen years; and
the parent or guardian of any such male person over
sixteenand under twenty -one years of age shall be
liablefor said poll-tax, or for the failureto work
upon the streets, as may be requiredunder section
five aforesaid .
§ 11. The boarding place shall constitutethe place
of residenceof any person in said city who has no
dwelling-house or fixedabode in said city.
§12. That every ordinanceor by -law or regulation,
imposing any penalty, fine, imprisonment , or forfeit-
ure for itsviolation, shall, after itspassage, before
taking effect,be published one week in some news-
paper publishedin saidcity, or by postingprinted
noticesthereofin three or more public places in said
city.
§13. There may be,in additionto the streettaxes
aforesaid, an ad valorem tax leviedand collectedeach
year of not exceeding twenty -fivecents on each
hundred dollars' worth of taxable property. For any
tax leviedunder this act,there shall exist a lien in
favorof the councilupon all the property of the tax-
payer untilthe same ispaid ; and in case the owner
of any taxable property shall failto pay his or her
taxes,on or beforethe firstday of October in each
year, itshallbe the duty of the citymarshal to sell
forcash in hand ,aftertendays'advertisementin some
newspapaperpublishedin saidcity,or by printedad-
vertisementsposted in threeor more public places in
said city,so much of the real, personal or mixed
propertyof the person so in defaultas willpay off
and discharge the taxes of such defaulter due the
council,and ten per centum damages thereon for the
4

marshal, besides expenses of sale, and which shall
vestthe purchaser with the titleand right to hold the

§ 1. No person shall, within three miles of the cor-

trateof saidcounty . All finesor forfeiturescollected
under this section for offenses committed outside of
the corporatelimitsof saidcity, and within two miles
thereof, shallbe paid to the trusteeof the jury fund
of said county ;and all finesor forfeiturescollected
under this section for offensesinside the corporate
limitsshallbe paid to the treasurerof saidcity. All
laws, general or special, pertaining to said county ,
inconsistentwith the provisionsof this section,are
hereby repealed.
§ 2. It shallbe unlawful for any person to get or be
intoxicatedin saidcity. Any person found in a state
of intoxicationshall,upon convictionthereof,be fined
in the sum of fivedollars. Any person convicted of
intoxicationshallbe required,upon the trial,to desig-
nate the person or persons from whom the liquor, in
whole or part,was obtained. In default of so desig-
nating such person, he or she shall, in addition to
the fineabove mentioned , as a part of his or her pun-
ishmentfortheoffense,be imprisonedin the county
or cityjailnot lessthan one day nor more than ten
days, at the discretionof the court.
§3 . That every husband ,wife,child, parent,guard-
ian,employer orotherperson,who shallbe injured
in person or property,or means of support,by any
intoxicatedperson, or in consequence of intoxication,
habitual or otherwise , of any person , shall have a

rightof actionin his own or her name ,severallyor
jointly,against any person or persons who shall, by
selling or giving intoxicatingliquors, within three
miles of the corporatelimitsof said city,have caused
the intoxication,in whole or in part,of such person,
foralldamages and exemplary damages . A married
woman shall have the same rightto bring suit,and
controlthe same ,and the amount recovered,as a feme

quired by the board of directors.
§ 7. The presidentand directorsshall have printed

by treasurer,and sealedwith the sealof the corpora-
tion, which shall be delivered to the stockholder
whenever the stock subscribed for by him ispaid in.
Said certificatesshall be transferableupon the books
of the company ,and the person to whom transferred

CHAPTER 1606 .
AN ACT to providefora registrationof votersin the cityof Coving-
1
ton .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county judge of Kenton county, the
sheriffof said county , and the clerk of the Kenton
County Court, shallconstitutea board,which shall,
between the firstand fifteenthdays of June in each
year, appoint four sober and discreetcitizensin each
electionprecinctin the cityof Covington- two to act
as judges of registration,one as clerkof registration,
and one as marshal of registration, in the precincts
for which they are appointed. No person shall be
eligibleas judge,clerkor marshal who is not an in-
telligenthousekeeper of good repute, and a voter
in the precinct for which he is appointed ; and
when there are two distinctpoliticalparties, said
officersof registrationshall be so selectedas that
one of the judges at each precinct shall be of
one politicalparty and the other of the opposing po-
liticalparty, and the clerk and marshal shallbe like-
wise divided politicallyat each precinct. They shall
servefor one year from the time of theirappointment ;
and each officerof registration,before entering upon
the discharge of his duties, shalltake the oath pre-
scribedby sectionone,articleeight,of the Constitu-
tion, and shall,during the period of his term of ser-
vice, be excused from jury duty . The judges and
marshal shalleach receivethree dollarsper day for
their servicesas such , and the clerks shall receive five

1048 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
dollarseach per day for theirservices,and when duly
appointed,shall be subject to a fine of twenty-five
dollars, upon conviction in the Kenton Criminal
Court, for failureto serve, unless excused by the
power appointing them , for sickness,death or other
calamity in theirfamilies, certifiedto by a practicing
physician ingood standing,residentof saidcity.
§ 2. The registrationprecincts and places under
this act shall be the same as the election precincts
provided for by the citycouncil of Covington, and
shallbe held at the same places in the cityof Cov-
ington .
§ 3. No placeof registrationshallbe locatedorheld
at, in or upon any premises or property used for
saloon purposes,or where liquor is sold, or within
sixty feetthereof.
§ 4. The pay of all registrationofficersappointed
under thisact,and the necessaryexpense of printing
and servingnoticeon them , and the costof providing
placesfor registrationand registrationbooks , shallbe
certifiedby the board named in section one of this
act to the citycouncil of Covington,and the entire
expense shall be paid by the cityof Covington,and
the owner or occupant of the premises used for the
purpose of registrationshall be entitledto receive, as
compensation for the use thereof,not lessthan two
dollarsper day ,together with compensation for any
damage occasionedby putting saidpremises in proper
conditionto convenientlyhold said registration.
§ 5. The sheriffof Kenton county shall, at leastfive
days before the general registrationin each year,as
provided forin thisact,giveeach officerofregistration
writtennoticeof his appointment . Should thecounty
judge, sheriffand clerk failto appoint, or should all
the officersappointed fail, for any precinct, to attend
at the place of registrationfor one hour afterthe time
forcommencing the registration, or refuseto act,then
the sheriff,judge or clerk, or eitherof them that can
be found, shallappoint officerstoact intheirstead for

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1049
that registration; but should one or two officersbe in
attendance,he or they may fillvacanciesfor the pur-
pose of conducting thatregistration,having the same
qualificationsas providedin sectionone of thisact,
and may administer the necessary oaths of office.
§ 6. The officersof registrationshallhave the same
power to preserve order at the places of registration
as isexercisedby officersof electionsat polls. If the
officersof registrationentertain any doubt as to
whether or not any person offeringfor registrationis
entitledto such registration,or if any one's rightto
registeris challenged,citizensmay be called in, not
exceeding three in number , who shallbe examined
touching the qualificationsof such person who offerto
register.
Forms .
§ 7. Itshallbe theduty of the county clerk of Ken-
ton county to prepare the proper forms, and cause to
be printedtwo registrationbooks for each precinctin
the cityof Covington, one of which shall be kept in
his office, to be used as provided in sectiontwelve of
thisact,and the other furnishedtothe clerksof regis-
tration and election before each registrationor elec-
tion day , as hereinafterprovided. Said books shall
be so arranged as that the names of voters may be
registered alphabetically, and shall be ruled and
headed as follows :
NAME .
Residence
Specialelection..
Octoberelection
AugustelectionColor
REMARKS .

1050 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
General Registration .
§ 8. The officersof registrationshallattend at the
voting places of their respective precincts on the first
Monday in July in each year,and on the two next
succeedingdays,from the hours of six o'clockin the
morning untilnine o'clock in the evening,and shall
record in the registrationbook , which shall be fur-
nished by the clerkof the county court to each regis-
trationclerk, a listof such qualifiedvoters of the
precinct as may apply for registration. If either of
the days shallfallupon the fourth day of the month ,
then saidfourthday shallbe omitted as a day of reg-
istration, and the registrationshall proceed on the
next succeeding day , as if said fourth day had not
intervened. Said listof voters shallbe in alphabet-
icalorder, and shall show the name of the person reg-
istered,the name of the streetor alley,and number
of the house,lodging or tenement in which he lives,
whether he be white or colored, and if said house ,
lodging or tenement be not numbered , the location
thereof shall be described in the registrationbook as
accuratelyas is practicable, giving the streetand be-
tween what streets. Said registrationin July shallbe
known as the general registration, and any person
then registeredmay vote at allelectionsuntilthe next
general registration, unless he becomes disqualified
after registering.
Qualifications.
§ 9. Every person shall be entitledto be registered
who would be entitled to vote at the next succeeding
August election, that is to say, every male citizen
who , on that day , shall have attained the age of
twenty-one years, and shall have residedin the State
two years,or in the county one year,and in the pre-
cinct in which he offersto registersixty days . No
person shallbe registeredwho does not personally
appear beforethe officersof registration, and if he
be not personallyknown to one of the officers,or if

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1051
any by -standershalldemand it,he shallbe sworn by
one of the officersand interrogatedby him , or by
such by -stander, touching his qualificationsas pro-
vided in thissection. Opposite to the name of each
person so sworn the clerk shall write the word
" Sworn ," which entry shallbe prima facie evidence
of such swearing in any prosecution under thisact.
Aliens .
§ 10. Any alien possessingall the qualificationsof
a voter named in sectionnine of thisact,except citi-
zenship, shall be entitledto register, but the clerk
shallwriteoppositeto hisname ,inthe column headed
" Remarks ," the words " Not naturalized," and such
alienshallnot be entitledto vote at any electionheld
under that registrationunlesshe shallproduce to the
officersof electionhis naturalizationpapers in due
form of law .
Mode of Registration.
§ 11. In making the registrationthe clerk shall
act as the recording officer, and the judges shall
decide all questions relatingto the qualificationsof
persons offeringto be registered,except that, in case
of a differenceof opinion between the judges, the
clerk shall have the casting vote . It shallbe the duty
of the clerk to number , consecutively, the names
recorded under each letterof the alphabet as they
are taken down , and at the closeof the registration
he shall sign his name as clerk in the presence of
the judges,after the lastname recorded under each
letterof the alphabet as aforesaid, in such manner as
that no more names can be recorded above his, and
shallfoot-up and certifyin the back of the registra-
tion book , after the entire close of registration, the
whole number of names recorded at that registration;
and thiscertificateshallbe signed by allthe officers
of registrationbefore leaving the place of registra-
tion,and in the presence of by -standers, any two
of whom may , if they desire, sign their names as

1052 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
witnesses thereto. In addition to the requirements
above, the clerk shallsign his name as such clerkat
the footof each page .
Return and Custody of Books .
§ 12. On the day followingthe closeof the registra-
tion,the clerkshalldeliverthe registrationbook into
the hands of the clerkof the county court,or one of
his deputies,and shalltake his receipttherefor. It
shallbe theduty of said county clerkto keep said
books safelyin hisoffice,and not to sufferthe same to
be taken therefrom,except as provided in this act.
He shallalsocause to be made one copy of each regis-
trationbook in the blank book retained by him , as
provided in sectionseven of thisact, which shallbe
kept in his office, and not be taken therefrom for any
purpose. In case of lossof any registrationbook , a
copy shallbe made by the county clerk from the copy
retainedin his office,which copy shallbe used in reg-
istration and election with the same effect as the
original. The clerk shall permit any citizen, at any
time, to copy any registrationbook without fee or
charge, and he shall alsofurnish copies at the same
rateas provided in sectionthirtyof thisact forcopy-
ing the book kept in his office. Copies furnished to
citizensunder this sectionshallbe paid for in cash
by the person ordering them .
SpecialRegistration.
§ 13. On the firstMonday in September in each year
there shallbe a registrationof those persons whose
names are not already on the registrationbooks for
thatyear,and who ,but forthis,would be entitledto
vote at the ensuing municipal election. When , by
virtueof any furtheract of the Legislature, or of any
proclamation or writ of electionissued under existing
laws, an electionis ordered for any day other than
one of the days mentioned in sectionseight and thir-
teen of this act , at which a vote is to be taken in the
cityof Covington, itshallbe the duty of the sheriff

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1053
of Kenton county to fixa day for the registrationof
those entitled to vote at such election, whose names
are not already on the registrationbooks for that
year, and to publish notice of such day in a
newspaper published and circulated in Covington
as a part of his noticeof the election, and where ,
by virtue of the city charter of Covington, or any
speciallaw, an electionor vote is ordered to be
held or taken in the city of Covington, at any
other time than one of the days aforesaid,then the
city council, or other body or person, so ordering
said electionor vote,shall, at the same time, fix a
day for the registrationof those persons entitledto
vote thereat,whose names have not been recorded on

Registrationspriorto specialelectionsshall be held

How Absentees May Register.

necessarilyabsent from the cityof Covington during

act,and showing the absence or sicknesshereinbefore
referred to .

1054 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Registration of Absentees .

ding the August electionin each year, on Wednesday

book , to be kept by him for that purpose, the affida-

§ 16. The clerk shall receive a fee of ten cents for

this act, to be paid as other expenses of registration
are paid .
Penalty for False Swearing .
§ 17. Any person making any falsestatement,inany
affidavittaken under thisact,shallbe deemed guilty
of falseswearing,and punished by confinement in the
penitentiarynot less than one nor more than five
years .
Punishment for False Registering.
§ 18. Any county clerk, or deputy thereof, who
falselyor fraudulentlyregistersthe name of any per-
son,knowing that such person is not entitledto be
registered,or who registersa name at a time other
than thatprovided in thisact,shall,on indictment,be
deemed guilty of felony, and punished by confine-
ment in the penitentiarynot lessthan one nor more
than fiveyears .
Challenging Voters.
§ 19. Any person presentin the county clerk'soffice
may challengethe rightof any voterto registerunder
thisact,and thereupon the county clerk shallexam

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1055
ine such voter, and any witness es whomay be offered
under oath, and shallhear and determine the ques-
tion of his right to register.
Deputy Clerk May Officiate.
§ 20. The duties herein imposed upon the county
clerkmay be performed by hisdeputy ,and he or his
deputy may administeroathsunder thisact.
§ 21. On the day priorto each registrationprovided
for in sectionthirteenof this act, the county clerk
shalldeliverto the registrationclerksthe registration
books for theirseveralprecincts, and on the day of
such registrationthe registrationofficersshallattend
at theirseveralplacesof registration,and shallregis-
ter the names of all persons appearing beforethem ,
who may be entitledto vote at the electionforwhich

shallbe recorded immediately following the names
recorded at the previous registration, and the regis-
tration shall be conducted and the books returned as-.
provided in sectionseight,nine,ten,eleven and twelve
of this act ; immediately after the books are so re-
turned,the county clerkshallcause the names record-
ed at that registrationto be copied into the book
retainedin his office, as provided in sectiontwelve of
this act .
Removals .
§ 22. Any person removing from one precinctto
another, afterhaving been registeredfor that year,
may apply to the clerkof registrationof the precinct
from which he removes , on a registrationday , and
have his name canceled off by writing opposite to
it the word " Removed ," and thereupon said clerk
shall give him a certificatein the following form :
" This isto certifythat has removed from the
precinct, ward , to the - precinct,.
ward , and that his registrationhas been can--
celed. (Signed) -- -,clerk." Upon presenting

1056 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
said certificateto the officerof registrationof the
precinctto which he has removed , he shallbe entitled
to be thereregistered, if he possessesthe other quali-
ficationsnamed in section nine of this act ; and no
person so removing , afterbeing registered, shallbe
registeredin another precinctwithout the production
of such certificate.
Striking Off Names .
§ 23. Any votermay ,by givingfivedays ' notice, in
writing, to any person whose name has been regis-
tered, move the county court to strikehis name from
the register,and both partiesmay introducewitnesses,
not exceeding two in number on each side. Said no-
ticemust be served personally. If, at the hearing,
the court shall be of the opinion that the person reg-
isteredisnot lawfullyentitledto register, it shalldi-
rect the clerk to strikehis name from the registerby
insertingopposite to itthe words , " Stricken offby
order of the county court.--- , clerk." If the per-
son upon whom noticeisattempted to be served can
not be found , the clerkshallwriteoppositesuch name
on the registrationbook the word " Doubtful," and
when , at an election, such person whose name is
marked doubtful shall offer to vote, he shall be
sworn , and his rightto vote investigatedfully.
§ 24. On Saturday previous to the firstMonday
in August , one thousand eight hundred and ninety,
and on the juridicalday previousto every election
to be held, or vote of the people to be taken there-
afterin the cityof Covington, the clerkof the county
court shalldeliverto the clerks of electionthe reg-
istrationbooks for their several precincts, which
said registrationbooks shall be produced by said
clerks at their severalprecinctswhen the polls are

no vote shallbe receivedunlessthe name of the per-
son offeringto vote ison the registryprovided in this
act .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1057
False Registration .
§ 25. Any person who shall cause himself to be
registeredin more than one electionprecinct,other-
wise than is provided in section twenty -two of this
act, or more than once in the same precinct,or who

son who shallaid or abet in the commission of any of

and shall be imprisoned in the county jailnot less

False Registration Books .
§ 26. Any officerof registrationor other person
who shall unlawfully alterany registrationbook , or

trationbook , shallbe deemed guilty of felony, and

holding office.
Perjury .
§ 27. Any person who shallwillfullymake any false
statement, under oath lawfully administered under
thisact,shall be deemed guiltyof perjury,and con-
finedin the penitentiarynot lessthan one nor more
than fiveyears .
ObstructingRegistration.
§ 28. Any person who ,by himself or in aid of oth-
ers,shall forciblybreak up , or attempt to break up ,
a registrationheld under this act, or shall forcibly
prevent,or attempt to prevent, any person from ap-
próaching or entering a place of registrationforthe
purpose of registering,shallbe finednot lessthan one
hundred dollars nor more than five hundred dollars,
67 - LOCAL LAWS , VOL . 3 .

1058 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
or imprisoned not lessthan six nor more than twelve
months in the county jail, or both .
§ 29. No witness shall be excused from testifying
beforeany grand jury,or in any prosecutionor indict-
ment under thisact,on the ground that his testimony
may criminatehimself;but no such testimony shallbe
used againsthim in any prosecution, except forper-
jury in giving the same .
Fees .
§ 30. For his services under this act the county
clerk shall receive the following fees: For copying
the registrylistsrequired to be kept in his office, or
to be used in supplying lostbooks ,the sum of two
cents for each voter whose name is so copied ; for his
services under section twenty -three of this act, the
sum of twenty -five cents for the name of each voter
strickenoffthe lists; forreceiptingto clerksof regis-
tration and election for registrationbooks , the sum of
fivecentsfor each book receiptedfor; the sheriffof
Kenton county shallreceive, for serving the notices
provided for in sectionfiveof this act, the sum of
sixtycentsfor each actual service. Officersof regis-
trationshallreceivethreedollarsperday foreach day
employed in attendingat the place of registration,ex-
cept clerksof registration,who shallreceivefivedol-
Jars for each day ; but said clerks shall not be entitled
toany pay , except upon the production of the county
clerk'sreceiptfor theirrespectiveregistrationbooks ,
which receiptshallbe the voucher of the officerpaying
them . The city of Covington shall pay allfees and
expenses incurredunder thisact for registration.
§ 31. A copy of thisact shallbe printedineach reg-
istrationbook made under itsprovisions.
§ 32. This actshallbe submitted to the legalvoters
of the city of Covington at the regular municipal
election in the month of October one thousand
eight hundred and ninety,and if a majority of the
legalvotersvoting at saidelectionshallvote in favor

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1059
of this act, then the same shall take effect from
the date of said election, but otherwise this act
shall be of no force or effect.
§ 33. No injunctionor writ of injunctionshall be
granted by any court, judge or officerto prevent, or
in any way hinder or delay the holding of said elec-
tion, nor shalltherebe granted,with referenceto any
matter contained in, or any duty imposed by or any

ing sectionof thisact, any injunctionor writ of in-
junction, except by the judge of the Kenton Circuit

nor shall any such injunctionbe granted after the

dred and ninety, nor otherwise than upon three
