Approved May 17, 1890 .
CHAPTER 1607 .
AN ACT to amend and reduce into one the several acts in relation to
thecharterofthetown ofAdairville,in Logan county,Kentucky .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That an act,entitled"An act to incorporatethe
town of Adairville, in Logan county," approved Feb-
ruary seven,one thousand eighthundred and seventy-
one, and the severalacts amending the same , be,and
they are hereby ,amended and reduced into one act,
which shall constitutethe organic law of said town ,
to wit : That the town as laid out and described in the
act approved February seven, one thousand eight
hundred and seventy-one,and the additionsthereafter
made by subsequent acts of the Legislature, and in-
cluding the grounds of the public school, together

1060 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
with all the streets, alleysand lotsas laid out and
fullydescribedin the recorded plat of saidtown ,shall
be the boundary of said town , and the inhabitantsof
saidboundary are hereby declared to be a body -pol-
iticand corporate, with perpetual succession; with
power tosue and be sued,contractand be contracted
with, plead and be impleaded, defend and be de-
fended, by the name of the town of Adairville,and
have a corporate seal.
§ 2. That the municipal government of said town
shall consist of five trustees, one of whom shall be
elected by the board to preside over their meetings,
and shall be styled chairman of the board of trus-
tees of Adairville . Said trustees and their successors
in officeshallbe a body -politicand corporate, under
the name and style of the board of trusteesof Adair-
ville, and by that name they may ,for and on behalf
of said town , contractand be contracted with,sue
and be sued,plead and be impleaded ,defend and be
defended , and in all courts and places ; may do all
thingswhich a body -corporate, with perpetual succes-
sion, may lawfully and rightfully do within the
limitsof the powers herein granted.
§ 3. The otherofficersof saidtown shallbe a police
judge, town marshal and assessor, and such other
officersas the board of trusteesmay ,from time to
time , direct and appoint .
§ 4. The electionfor the said trustees, and for all
other electiveofficesof saidtown ,shallbe held on the
firstSaturday in March , one thousand eight hundred
and ninety-one,and every year thereafter, except the
policejudge, who shallhold his officetwo years,
or untilhissuccessorisduly electedand qualified;
said election and all other elections held under the
provisionsof this act,shall be advertisedby written
or printednoticesposted up in threeof the most pub-
licplacesinsaidtown at leastten days just previous
to said election. The board of trustees, before each
election, shallappoint four persons to hold said elec

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1061
tion ; and if,for any reason, any of the persons so ap-
pointedshallfailto act,thoseremainingare empow-
ered to filltheir places. Officersof town elections
shallperform similarservices, be entitledto likepay ,
and liableto the same penalties, and governed in all
matters not inconsistent with this charter as officers
of State elections,and be entitledto the same pay .
§ 5. Every male citizen of the age of twenty-one
years, who shall have resided in said corporation
sixtydays justprecedingtheelection,and are quali-
fied votersof the State, non -residentsowning prop-
erty in said town , the titleof which was acquired
and of record not lessthan sixty days previous to
the election, shall be entitledto vote in said elec-
tion fortrustees; and allof said persons,except non-
resident property-holders, shall be entitledto vote
for policejudge or any other electiveofficers: Pro-
vided, That no persons who have failedto pay their
poll-taxes due the town for the previous year ten
days before saidelection,shallbe entitledto vote in
any municipal election; and it shall be the duty of
the clerk and marshal of saidtown , ten days before
the regular election, to make out a listof such de-
linquent tax-payers,and deliverit,securelysealed,to
the chairman of the board of trustees, who shall de-
liveritunopened to the officersof electionafter they
have been sworn to hold said election; and no per-
son whose name is on said list shall be entitled to
vote in said election, nor in any subsequent election,
untilsaidarrearagesare paid.§ 6. The officersconducting the electionsherein
provided for shall, on the succeedingMonday , meet,
and depositthe poll-books with the board of trustees;
and the clerk of the board shall,in the presence of
the board , add up the polls, and give to the persons
receivingthe highestnumber of votesfortrusteesand
policejudge certificatesof election.
§ 7. Every person electedor appointed under the
provisionsof this act, before entering upon the dis

1062 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
charge of the dutiesthereof,shalltake and subscribe,
beforesome authorizedofficer,the oathof officepre-
scribed by the Constitution of this Commonwealth ,
and filethe same ,duly certifiedby the officerbefore
whom it was taken , with the clerk of board : Pro-
vided, That before the police judge shall take said
oath and assume the dutiesof his office, he shallpro-
cure from the Governor of this Commonwealth his
commission ,empowering him to assume the dutiesof
said office, which commission shall issue upon the
certificateof electionfurnished the Governor by the
clerk of the board .
§ 8. The officers,except policejudge,electedby the
people under the provisionsof this act, shall hold
theirofficesfor one year, and until their successors
are elected and qualified.
§ 9. No person shallbe eligibleto any officein said
town who isnot a citizenthereof,and a qualifiedvoter
under the laws of this State ; and no one shall be
eligibleto the officeof trusteeor police judge who
has not , at the time of his election, been a resident of
said town at leastsix months preceding his election.
§ 10. Whenever a vacancy shall happen in any
officefilledby election,it shall be filledby appoint-
ment ,by the trustees,untilthe next regularelection,
or untilhis successoriselectedand qualified.
§ 11. If,forany cause,no electionisheld at the time
prescribedby thischarter, the judge of the Logan
County Court shallappoint fivetrusteesand a police
judge,who shallhold theirofficesuntilthe next regu-
lar election. When two or more candidates for any
electiveofficeshallhave an equal number of votes,the
person elected shall be determined by lot,in such
manner as the trusteesmay direct, in the presenceof
at leastthree other persons .
§ 12. Cases of contested elections for any town
officeselectedby the people shallbe heard and deter-
mined by the justicesof the peace and the constable
of the Adairvilledistrict; notice to be given as now

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1063
provided by law and returned to said board . And
should eitherof said persons fail, or be disqualified
to act in said contestedelections,then the nearestlike
officers shall act in their stead .
§ 13. The clerk shallkeep the corporate seal,and
keep a complete record of the proceedings of the
board of trustees,whose meetings itshallbe his duty
to attend ; and transcriptsfrom the records of such
proceedings,certifiedby him ,shallbe evidence in all
the courts in this Commonwealth . He shall draw all
warrants on the treasurer,directedby the board of
trustees,and shall countersignthe same , and keep an
accurateaccount thereof in a book to be provided for
the purpose . He shallissuelicensewhen directedby
the trusteesto do so,and perform allother duties
which the trusteesmay lawfullydirect.
§ 14. The treasurershallreceiveall money belong-
ing to the town , and keep an accurate account of
receipts and expenditures; he shallpay out money
only on the order of the board of trustees; the war-
rant foreach payment to be signed by the chairman ,
or actingchairman of the trustees, and countersigned
by the clerk, and it shall specify the purpose for
which the amount named therein is paid. He shall
exhibit to the board of trustees, at leastten days' be-
fore the regularelection, and oftener if required, a
detailed account of allreceipts and expenditures after
the date of the lastreport, and shallstatethe condi-
tionof the treasury. Such account shallbe preserved
by the clerk, who shall spread the same upon the
record of the board of trustees, and cause a copy
thereof to be posted up in some public place in.said
town .
§ 15. The marshal shall attend all sessions of the
policecourt. He shallexecute and make due return
of all processes directed to him by the Adairville
Police Court , and shall have the power and authority
of a constable within the corporate limits of said
own , and execute all orders of arrest for violations

1064 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
of corporatelaw in any part of Logan county, and
shall be entitledas compensation to the same fees
allowed to sheriffsand constablesfor similarservices,
to be collected in the same manner . He shall be liable
in the same cases,way and manner , that sheriffsand
constablesare liable,and may be sued or proceeded
against in the same manner . He shallbe , ex officio,
collector of taxes for said town , and shall collectall
taxes and assessments which may be levied by the
board of trustees, and for that purpose he shallhave
allthe powers of a sheriffto make salesof personal
and real estate. He shallpay over to the treasurer
alltaxes and other money which may come into his
hands as the board of trusteesmay direct. When
the tax-listisplaced in his hands he shallproceed at
once to collectthe same ; and to enforce payment ,
may levy on and sellpersonal property, afteradver-
tisingthe same by writtenor printednotice,posted in
at leastthree public places in the town for not less
than ten days justpriorto the sale.
§ 16. Within four months from the time he receives
said list, he shall make out and return to the board of
trusteesa report showing the amount collected, the
names of those who have paid and those who are de-
linquent; but he shallreport no person delinquent,
nor shall he be credited in his settlement with the
trusteeswith the listany person who owns personal
property in said town out of which the taxes can be
made . Upon the return of the collector'sreport as
above provided,the board of trusteesshall,by order
duly entered of record,direct a warrant to be made
out,signedby the chairman and countersignedby the
clerk, commanding the collectorto levy upon and sell
so much of the realestateof the delinquents as may
be necessaryto pay the taxesdue and costof sale. On
receiptof said warrant the marshal shall proceed to
make leviesas thereby commanded , and shallsellthe
propertyleviedon at publicauctionto the highest
bidder, afteradvertisingthe time and place of saleat

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1065
leastfifteendays justpriorto the sale, by written or
posted noticeposted up in three of the most public
places in said town . On receivingthe pricebid, he
shallgive tothe purchaser of the property a certificate
of such purchase, describingthe property in such
manner that itmay be identified. Such certificate, on
presentationto the board of trustees,shallentitlethe
purchaser to a deed from said trusteesto any real
estateso purchased,unlessthe same be redeemed as
hereinafterprovided. The marshal shall, within such
time as they direct, make to the board of trusteesa
fulland complete return of the manner in which he
has executed said warrant, which return shall be
spread upon the recordsof the board .
§ 17. The assessorshallperform allthedutieswhich
the trusteesmay ,by ordinance or by -laws, prescribe
in relationto the assessmentof property forthe pur-
pose of imposing or levying taxes.
§ 18. The trusteesmay ,by ordinance or by -law, at
any time requirethe performance of other duties, and
fix the compensation of all officerswhose duties are
hereinafterprescribed; and they may prescribe the
dutiesand fixthe compensations of allofficerselected
or appointed under this act, whose duties and com-
pensationare not specificallymentioned .
§ 19. The policejudge, town marshal,treasurerand
clerk, and such other officersas the board of trustees
may require, shallseverally, before they enter upon
the duties of theirrespectiveoffices,execute a bond
to the trusteesof Adairville, conditioned that they
willexecute the dutiesof their respectiveoffices, and
pay over to such persons,and at such times as they
may be entitledto receivethe same , allmoney that
may come into his hands as such officers. The bond
so executed shallbe spread upon the records of the
board of trustees, and the originalscarefullypre-
served by the clerk.
§ 20. The trusteesof Adairville,or any person in-
jured by the breach of any such bonds ,may , in any

1066 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
court having jurisdiction, prosecute suit or motion
thereon against the officersby whose breach of duty
such injuryisoccasioned,and his sureties, or any of
them ,or the personal representatives,heirs or devi-
sees, or any or allof them , as provided by law con-
cerning sheriffs,constables and other like officers. No
such bond shall be void until the trustees of Adair-
ville, and every person aggrievedby the acts, remiss-
ness or neglectof such officer,have been recompensed .
§ 21. Any person having been an officerin said
town , who shallnot,within ten days afternotification
and request,deliverto his successorsin officeall the
property, papers and effectsof every descriptionin
hispossessionbelonging to saidtown or appertaining
to the officehe held,shallforfeitand pay for use of
the town one hundred dollars, besides all cost and
damages caused by his neglectand refusalso to do , to
be recoveredby suitor motion on his officialbond .
§ 22. There is hereby establishedin said town a
court, to be styled the AdairvillePoliceCourt. Said
court shallbe held by one judge , who shall be styled
the Judge of the Adairville Police Court,who shall
be elected and qualifiedas hereinbefore provided.
The AdairvillePoliceCourt shallhave jurisdictionof
all prosecutionsfor the violationof the by -laws or
ordinances of said town ,and concurrent jurisdiction
with other courtsof Logan county of misdemeanors
committed in said town ,when the penalty imposed
by law does not exceed one hundred dollars and im-
prisonment forfiftydays ; saidcourt shallhave power
to issuewarrants and other processin the name of the
Commonwealth to compel the attendance of wit-
nesses; to take bond and recognizancesfrom persons
charged with offensesrecognizablebefore it to appear
and answer the same , and the power to declare the
same forfeited; to pronounce judgment thereon,and
enforcefullcompliance therewith, that circuitcourts
have . It shall have power to enter judgments , and
award and issuewritsfor the purpose of enforcingits

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1067
orders and judgments ,to the same extent and in the
same manner as other courts of this Commonwealth
of like jurisdiction. The police judge shall be a
conservator of the peace ; he shall have power
to take recognizances to keep the peace, and do
all other acts and things necessary and proper to
carry into full and complete effectthe powers and
jurisdictionconferred on said judge and said court.
The police court shallbe held at such place as the
trusteesshalldesignate,or they failingto designate
and provide a suitableplace,atsuch place in saidtown
as the judge may select. Said court shallbe open at
all times for the trialof criminal and penal causes
within itsjurisdiction. Itmay punish contempts by
a fine of not more than three dollars and imprison-
ment not exceeding ten hours. No person shall be
tried in said court for any offensepunishable for a
fine of more than fifteendollars or imprisonment
without the interventionof a jury, unless he shall
waive the privilegeof such trialby jury. The juris-
diction and dutiesof the policejudge in holding ex-
amining trialsshallbe such as are now presented by
the general laws of this Commonwealth for other like
offenses; and shall have power to hold examining
trialsin casesof felony; and in allthings pertaining
thereto, hisjurisdictionshall be equivalentto a jus-
tice of the peace in such cases.
§ 23. All processesfrom said police court shallbe
in the name of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,and
directedto the town marshal or other person author-
ized to execute the same . All fines and forfeitures
recovered for violation of corporate offenses in said
court are hereby granted to the town of Adairville,
and shallbe paid by the officercollectingthe same to
the treasurerof the town ;other finesshallbe paid to
theAuditoroftheState. Beforeissuingofany exe-
cution or finalprocess on a judgment in said court
fora fineor a forfeiture, the defendant may pay the
same to thejudge of the court. The defendant may

1068 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
also,before or afterthe issuing of any execution or
capiaspro fine,replevoment forthreemonths any fine,
judgment or forfeitureof the policecourt,in the same
manner as is allowed by law in similarcases within
the jurisdictionof othercourtsof thisCommonwealth .
§ 24. Upon alljudgments for finesrendered by said
court,and which shallnot be paid or repleviedat the
time of renderingthe same , an execution againstthe
estateof the defendent may be issued, or a capias
pro finemay issue, requiringthe imprisonment in the
town lock-up untilthe same be paid or replevied;but
imprisonment shall be one day for each two dollars of
such fine: Provided , That said court may ,in itsdis-
cretion, assignsuch defendant to labor forthe benefit
of the town until he shall pay the fine adjudged
againsthim at the rate of one dollarper day . Such
defendant shall not be required to labor more than
eighthours per day . While at labor, such defendant
shall be under the control of the town marshal , who
shall be responsible on his officialbond for the escape
of the defendant. All personssentenced to imprison-
ment by judgment of said court may be assigned to
labor in likemanner as those finedwhen persons are
imprisoned as contemplated by thissection. The jail
feesshallbe paid by said town .
§ 25. A returnof no property found on fierifacias,
and not found on a pro fine issued,or any judgment
of the policecourt, shallauthorize an equitablepro-
ceeding in favor of the trustees of Adairville against
the estateof the defendant, and for the purpose of
enforcingsatisfactionof such judgment of a returnof
no property found as are authorizedin such proceed-
ing in civilcauses as provided in the CivilCode .
§ 26. The Adairville Police Court shall have the
same jurisdictionin civilcasesin the corporatelimits
of saidtown as justicesof the peace for Logan coun-
ty now have for the recovery of money or personal
property. He shall have power to grant orders of
attachment,injunctionand restrainingorders, orders

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1069
of delivery; writsof habeas corpus,distresswarrants,
and writs of forcible entry or detainer . Quarterly
terms of said court shall be held on the firstSaturday
in February , May , August and November , until
changed by order of court, entered of record. The
trusteesof Adairvilleshall provide the judge with

as required by sectionthree hundred and sixty-four

the provisionsof the Civiland CriminalCodes of Prac-
tice of this Commonwealth . The fees of a police

Taxation .
§28. The trusteesof Adairvilleshallhave power,

annually levy and collecttaxes not more than fifty

in said town , includingevery speciesof property that

State for revenue purposes. They may also levy a

saidtown ,and they may levy a tax not exceeding one
dollaron every dog kept within the corporate limits
of said town . There shallbe exempt from taxation
in said town all property exempted by the laws of

posed by said trusteesunder this act shall be a lien

§ 29. The rightof redemption in allcasesof saleof

§ 30. The trusteesshallmake such rules and regu-

ituous, vinous or malt liquorsby the drink, for one

CHAPTER 1608 .
AN ACT to incorporate the Bank of Barbourville.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
L
§ 1. That there is hereby incorporated a bank of
discount and deposit in the town of Barbourville,
with a capitalstock of fiftythousand dollars,in shares
of one hundred dollars each, and the subscribers,
their associates, successors and assigns shallbe a
body -politicand corporate,by the name of " Bank of
Barbourville," and shallhave perpetual succession,
with allthe rightsand privilegesof a naturalperson,
in its said corporate name ,in contractingand being
contractedwith,in suing and being sued,and of im-
pleading, answering and defending, in all courtsand
places whatever ; and shallhave allother rightsand
powers usual and incidentto such corporations; and
may have and use a common seal,and may change the
same at pleasure.
§ 2. The property,business and affairsof said cor-
poration shallbe under the management ,government
and control of a board of directors, to consist of seven

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1077
persons,one of whom shall be electedby the board of
directorsas presidentof the bank ; and C. D. Chenalt,
John H. Wilson , G. Garrard, John Bennett, A. R.
Brunham ,Dutton Jones and D. K. Rawlings are here-
by constitutedand appointed a board of directorsfor
saidbank , to serve as such until their successorsare
electedand qualified; and in case of a vacancy or va-
canciesoccurringinthe board,the remaining directors
shallhave power to appoint a qualifiedstockholderto
fillit until the next regular election. Said board of
directors shall provide for an election of direct-
ors by the stockholders within one year from
the organization of the bank , and every one
year thereafter; and all elections of directors by
the stockholdersshallbe held at the placeof business
of the bank ,and noticein writingshallbe given each
stockholderby the cashieror presidentof said bank
two weeks previous; and such electionsshallbe held
under the inspectionof two or more persons, to be
appointed by the board of directors,who shallcertify
the result under their respectivesignatures, and a
pluralityof votes cast shall elect; and each stock-
holder shall be entitled to one vote for each share of
stock which he may own , and may cast the same in
person or by proxy in writing; and the board of
directorsmay provide a form for the appointment of
proxies, and specify the evidence that shall be re-
quired in execution thereof; and in allcasesdirectors
shall continue in office until their successors are elect-
ed and qualified. No one shall be elected director
who is not a stockholder .
§ 3. The persons named in the second sectionof this
act, or any one of them ,may open books for and re-
ceivesubscriptionsforthecapitalof saidbank ,at such
times and places,and subjectto such terms and con-
ditionsas they may deem proper ; and whenever four
hundred sharesof the capitalstock of said bank shall
have been subscribed, and sixty-fiveper cent.thereof
paid in, in accordance with the terms and conditions

1078 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
The
upon which same was subscribed,and affidavittothat
effecthas been made by one of the incorporators
above named ,before the clerk of the Knox County
Court, said corporation may proceed to transact a
general banking and financialbusiness,and may lend
money , discount notes, buy and sell exchange ,
stocks,bonds, mortgages and other evidencesof debt,
and take personal and other securitiesfor same .
said bank shall have the power to make advances
upon approved securities,upon agriculturalproducts,
includinggrowing crops, and upon all manufactures
and fixtures,as personalproperty. Itmay receivein
pledge or as securityfor money loaned, or fordebts
owing it,bonds ,stocks, produce or merchandise, Uni-
ted Statesvouchers,certificates, or bonds of indebted-
ness, or the bonds of this or any other State, city,
county or company , and secure any debt or liability
to said bank by mortgage on any real or personal
property, in or out of thisState, and sellthe same on
the non -payment of the debt or demand by ordinary
cause of judicialproceedings.
§ 4. Said bank may receivedepositsof gold,silver,
bank notes or other currency,and repay the same in
kind ,or as may be agreed upon with the depositors,
by specialor generalcontract; and may allow inter-
est on deposits. It may issue certificatesof credit,
payable through the United Statesand elsewhere, for
the convenience of merchants and travelers, but shall
not issueany notesor billsto circulateas money .
§ 5. The saidbank may acquire,hold, possess, use,
occupy and enjoy allsuch realand personalproperty,
goods, chattelsand other thingsas may be convenient
for the transactionof itsbusiness, or which may be
pledged or conveyed to itas securityfor any debt or
purchased in satisfactionof any debt or judgment ,
and selland convey or otherwisedisposeof same as a
natural person : Provided, That said bank shall not
hold any real estateconveyed to it as securityfor or

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1079
in payment of any debt, judgment or decree,for a
longer periodthan fiveyears.
§ 6. Said corporationmay build a safetyvault, with
a number of small compartments or boxes designed
and adapted forrentingto persons who desireto have
a place of safedepositfor valuables,and said corpora-
tion is hereby empowered to rent out the boxes in
such safetyvault to such persons, and for such sums
of money ,and for such time, as may be agreed be-
tween such corporationsand such persons as may
rent said boxes or compartments .
§ 7. That allsuch subscribersforthe capitalstock
of said bank as shallfailto pay sixty-fiveper cent.on
each share subscribed for,at such time and place as
may be designated by the persons named in the
second sectionof this act, shall cease to be stock-
holders, and the stock subscribed for by them
may be subscribed for by others under the direc-
tions of the persons named in the second section ;
and in case any subscribershall failto pay any sub-
sequent callmade by the board of directors(who are
hereby authorizedto make said calls) for the unpaid
balance of his subscription,and remain in defaultfor
sixty days afternoticeof saidcall,the board may , in
itsdiscretion, forfeithis stock for the benefitof the
bank ,upon the bank refunding tohim his money ,and
sellsaid stock to other parties. The form of certificates
of shares of stock shallbe regulated by the board of
directors,and no transferof stockshallbe binding as to
the bank untilitshallhave been notifiedthereof, and
the same transfer has been entered on the books of
the corporation; and a lienis hereby createdin favor
of said corporation on the stock of each share -holder
forany claim that the corporationmay have for any
unpaid part of his sharesto saidbank .
§ 8. The board of directorsof said bank are hereby
authorizedand empowered to enact and put in force
such rules,by -laws and regulationsfor the manage-
ment , government and controlof itsproperty, busi-

1080 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
ness and affairsas they may deem expedient,not in-
consistent with the laws of this State or the United
States, and alter, amend and repealthe same at will;
and shallspecifythereinthe number of directorsnec-
essary to constitute a quorum for the transaction of
business. They may appoint such officers,agents and
servantsas they may deem necessaryto conduct the
business of the bank ,and pay them such sums for
their servicesas they may think reasonable. The
cashier and clerk, and such other officersas the board
of directorsmay require, shall,before entering upon
their respectiveduties,execute a bond to the bank ,
with securityapproved by the board of directors, and
such conditionsas they shall require, and to be re-
newed when demanded by saidboard;and upon any
bonds thus taken recoveriesmay be had for breaches
of conditions thereof .
§ 9. The president, directorsand other officersof
this bank , previousto entering upon the discharge of
theirduties,shalltake an oath before some justiceof
peace or notary public of this State to faithfully,
honestly, and to the best of their ability, skilland
judgment to discharge all the duties of their re-
spective officesand stations under this charter, or
which may be requiredof them by the by -laws,rules
and regulationsof the corporation; and that they
willnot,during theircontinuancein office,knowingly
sanction or permit any of the provisions of this charter
to be violated .
§ 10. The stock of thisbank shallbe deemed per-
sonal property , and every stockholder shall be indi-
viduallyliableto the creditorsof the bank to the full
amount of stock subscribedfor and owned by him .
§ 11. This act shall take effectand be in force from
and afteritspassage,and continue for the period of
twenty-fiveyears.
Approved May 17, 1890

panies .

the sheriffof Logan county, in his finalsettlementof
his accounts as collectorof the county levy in said
county , a listof insolvents, and the said listshall
have been allowed him as a credit on his accounts in
the manner and upon the terms now provided by law,
itshallbe the duty of the county court clerk of said
county to forthwith prepare, in duplicate, a listof
said insolventas returned from each magisterialdis-
trictin said county,and to furnish one of said lists,
so as aforesaidmade , to each of the magistrates re-

county ; and thereupon the said magistrateshalltake
said listso furnished him by said county court clerk,
and shall thereupon ascertainin what road precinct
in his magisterialdistrictsuch insolvents,or any of
them , properly belong ; and shall thereupon furnish
the surveyor of such road precinctwith a listof the
insolventsreturned as aforesaidbelonging therein;
and thereupon it shallbe the duty of such surveyor
of such road precinctto appoint a day and notify
said insolventsthereof, by one day's notice, either
writtenor verbal, whereupon said delinquents, or

1118 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
such of them as are able-bodied, shallattendupon
said road under the supervisionof such surveyor of
said road , and shall, under the conditions hereinafter
imposed , work out the amount of said county levy
tax .
§2 . That when any insolvents, so as aforesaid re-
turned, are residentsof any incorporatedtown , and
not assigned to labor upon any public road in said
county, then itshallbe the duty of the county judge
of said county to designateupon what sectionof road
leadingintosuch town such insolventsshalllabor as
aforesaid ; and he shall thereupon notify the surveyor
of such section or precinct, in writing , of his action in
that behalf, whereupon such surveyor shall notify
said insolventsas provided in sectionfirstof thisact ;
and itshallbe the duty of said delinquents to attend
when notifiedand labor upon said road, excepting
those excluded by sectionfirst.
§ 3. That such insolvents as come within the pro-
visionsof sectionsfirstand second of thisact,shall,
when notifiedas aforesaidby saidsurveyor, attend at
the time designatedand labor upon said road, and for
theirlaborshallreceivea creditof seventy-fivecents
per day upon theirsaid tax ; and they shallcontinue
to laborfrom day today ,as directedby said surveyor,
untilthey have thus worked out the fullamount of
said tax ; and for a failure to attend when notified, or
to laborwhen in attendance,they shall be liableto a
fineof two dollarsand fiftycents for each day they
shallfailto attend when notified, or when in attend-
ance shallfailto labor,said finerecoverableby war-
rant in the name of the Commonwealth before any
justiceof the peace in Logan county and enforceable
as other finesare by law .
§ 4. Any finesrecovered under the preceding sec-
tionsof thisact shallbe applied as itisnow provided
by law that finesfor a failureto work upon roads
shallbe applied.
§ 5. That if,afterhaving been returned as insolvent

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1119 .
as aforesaidby the sheriff, any person shalldesireto
pay his said tax, he shallhave the rightto do so,and
thereby be releasedfrom the provisionsof this act,
and the production of his tax receiptshall be evi-
dence of this fact .
§6 . That nothing in this act shallbe construed to
release any one from any road duty now imposed
upon him by law.
§7. That any officerfailingto perform his duty
under the provisionsof this act shallbe liableto a
fine of not more than ten dollars for such failure,
recoverableas provided in sectionthree.
§ 8. It shallbe the duty of the county attorney to
prosecute under the provisionsof thisact.
§ 9. This act shall take effectand be in force from
its passage, and shall only apply to Logan county ;
and all actsin conflictherewith are hereby repealed..
Approved May 20, 1890.
CHAPTER 1633 .

monwealth of Kentucky :

county,saidcompany to be known as the " Sinking
Creek Turnpike Road Company ;" and by that name .

1120 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
sue and be sued,to contractand be contracted with,
to implead and be impleaded .

eight thousand dollars,divided into shares of fifty
dollars each .
§3 . That D. A. James , J. J. Carter, I. B. San-
dusky , William Sandusky and D. M. Megee , are
hereby appointed commissioners to perform the
duties of this act; and they shalltake subscriptions
to the capitalstock of said company by taking from
the subscriberssuch obligationsas in theirdiscretion
they may think best.
§ 4. When ten sharesof said stock are subscribed,
said commissioners, or a majority of them , shall
notifythe saidsubscribersthat a meeting of the sub-
scribersto the stock of saidcompany willbe held at
a time and placeas they may designatein saidnotice,
forthe purpose of choosing officers; and in the elec-

§5 .The officersof saidcompany shallbe a president

at all times be accessible to each share -holder for in-
spectionupon applicationthereforto said board .
§ 6. The width of said road shallnot exceed sixty
feet,nor be lessthan thirtyfeet, and the part covered

saillesTurnpike Road Company .
§ 9. This act shall take effectfrom after its pas-
sage .
Approved May 20,1890 .

monwealth of Kentucky :

Bradley,AlexanderPearson,W. P. Hampton ,Godfrey
Hunter, Matt. O'Doherty, George Denny , W. W.
Huffman , David G. Colston,Albert Scott,John W.
Yerkes , John M. Wilson , E. H. Hobson , W. McD .
Shaw , John W. Breathitt, James S. Van Natta ,
Thomas Todd , A. R. Burnam , M. V. Monarch , N. S.
Roark , LeslieCombs , H. C. McDowell , T. S. Given ,
William M. Collins, T. M. Shaw ,Gideon P. Jolly, A.
H. Benton , W. H. Johnson , George D. Todd , E. F.
Tucker , S. D. Pinkerton, Cad .Burba , H. H. Barnes,
L. P. Tarlton, H. K. Millward, R. P. Stoll, John L.
Wheat , J. G. Adams , M. Cardwell, G. M. Davison ,

R. Barnes ,E. M. Nell,J. M. Burbridge,C. L. Barnes ,
W. S. McFarland ,J. H. Compton ,J. H. Black ,T. Η .
Hardin , W. D. Ray , E. Farley,John B. Weller ,John

Wellington Harlan , J. M. Douglass, Isaac N. Card-
well, R. C. McClure , Richard P. Ernst , C. Ander-
son,B. F. Copeland ,George W. Burry , StithT. Noe ,
W. H. Wadsworth , A. N. Jett, W. H. Fritts, John
S. Cooper , Ossian C. Scott,W. H. Ritchey, F. S. Hill,
E. B. Edwards , Stephen Nethercutt, H. C. Howard ,
J. W. Walker , Oscar Hooge , Robert B. Anderson ,
Benjamin R. Waller, T. J. Walters, George M.
Thomas and S. T. Hillis, and their associates and suc-
cessors,be, and they are hereby,constitutedand de-
clareda body -corporate,under the name and style of
the " Republican StateLeague of Kentucky ;" and by
that corporatename and stylethey are investedwith

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1127
all the powers incident to corporations, and shall
continue and have perpetual succession, and by that
name and style contractand be contractedwith, sue
and be sued, acquire by gift, donation, bequest or
purchase, and hold personal and real estate; plead
and be impleaded with ; may have and use a common
seal, and alterthe same at pleasure, and may estab-
lish and ordain such constitution,by -laws and regu-
lations as may be necessary for the well-being and
government of said League and the attainment of
the objects and exerciseof the powers hereinafter
set forth, not, however , contraryto the laws of this
Commonwealth or of the United States .
§ 2. The objectsof said corporationshallbe to ad-
vocate, promote and maintain the principlesof the
Republican party ; to interestin politicsthose who
have been indifferentto theirpoliticalduties; to en-
courage attendance at the primary meetings, in order
that honest and capable men may be nominated ; to
guard and defend the purity of the ballotbox ; to
promote the cause of good government ; to promote
acquaintance among the Republicans of Kentucky ; to
associatethe Kentucky Republicans intoa permanent
organization, auxiliaryto the Republican League of
the United States and the establishedparty authori-
ties .
§ 3. They shall have power to acquire, own and
publish, in the name of saidcorporation,newspapers
and periodicaljournals advocating the purposes of
the League .
§ 4. The League shall indorse no candidate for
public officewho is not the nominee of a regularcon-
vention of the Republican party, nor shallit indorse
any person forappointment to any public office.
§5 . The amount of capitalstock ofsaidcorporation
shall be two hundred thousand dollars, divided into
sharesof one dollareach,and no one person shallhold
more than one share of stock or be entitled to record
more than one vote in any convention at which he is

1128 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
entitledto vote as a member of said League ; and
such persons as are electedmembers of said League ,
and none other, shall be entitled to own or hold said
stock. Said stock shallnot be transferableeitherby
the acts of the person in whose name it is entered
upon the books of the corporation or who is the
holder thereof, or by operation of law, but shall
revertabsolutelyto the corporationwhen such per-
son ceasesto be a member of saidLeague ,whether by
death, resignation, expulsion or otherwise.
§ 6. The members of said League shallbe such per-
sons as are electedin the manner prescribedby the
by -laws,and who shalleach have subscribedand paid
for one share of said stock. The qualificationsof
members may be fixed by the by -laws : Provided ,
however , That no person shall become a member un-
lesshe be a citizen of the State of Kentucky , and an
activesupporterof,and avowed believerin,the princi-
ples of the Republican party.
§ 7. The officersof saidLeague shallbe a president,
a firstand a second vice-president, a secretary, an
assistantsecretary, a treasurer and an executive com-
mittee , composed of one member from each Congress-
ional District in the State of Kentucky ; and the
presidentand secretaryof the State League ,and the
vice-president and executive committeeman for Ken-
tucky of the Republican League of the United States,
shallbe ex officiomembers thereof. Said officersshall
be electedin the manner prescribedby the by -laws at
the annual convention of the League , and shall hold
theirofficesforthe term of one year,and until their
successorsshallbe electedor appointed.
§ 8. There shall be an annual delegate convention
of the League , to be held at such time and place,and
composed of such ratablerepresentationfrom subor-
dinate Leagues,as shall be fixed by the by -laws or
determined upon by the executivecommittee .
§ 9. The executive committee shall have the control
and management of the affairsand property of the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1129
League of every kind, subjectto the directionof the
League in convention ; but in no case shall said com-
mittee incur on behalf of the League any expense or
obligationexceeding the sum then in the treasury.
§ 10. Said committee shall have power to assess
dues not exceeding one dollara year on each member ,
and when any installment of dues has been in arrears
thirty days ,may declarethe member owing the same
discontinuedfrom membership in the League ,and his
stock forfeited; and shallhave power tomake by -laws
for itsown government , subjectto the approval of the
League in convention, and to provide for declaring
and fillingvacancies in its membership , and in the
officersof the League , excepting where a vice-presi-
dent succeedsthe presidentfor re-instatingpersons to
membership in the League ,and for otherpurposes not
inconsistentwith thisact or the constitutionand by-
laws of the League .
§ 11. The Secretary shall receive an annual salary
for his services to be fixed by the executive com-
mittee .
§ 12. The treasurershall receivea commission upon
allmoneys of the League collectedby him to be fixed
by the executive committee, in no event, however , to
exceed fiveper cent. thereof.
§ 13. The State League of Kentucky shall have
power by itsconstitutionand by -laws to provide for
the division of its stockholders and members into
subordinate Leagues , and to organize such subor-
dinate Leagues .
§ 14. The privateproperty of the stockholdersshall
not be liablefor the debts of saidcorporation.
§ 15. This act shall take effect and be in force from
and after itspassage .
Approved May 20, 1890-

CHAPTER 1640 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Colored Cemetery Company of North
Middletown ,in Bourbon county .

monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That Mitchell Allen,F. Bratton, Charles But-
ler, Harry West , Roy Green , James Gay , Thomas

and styleof the Colored Cemetery Company of North

such otherduties and exercise such other privileges
as are usuallyconferredon similarcorporations.
§ 2. Said company shallbe authorizedand ready to
transact business when and so soon as said corpora-
tors shall meet and elect out of their number five di-
rectors, who shall have the full management and
controlof the affairsof the company , subjectto the
control of the stockholders as hereinafter stated .
The first board of directors shall hold their offices
for one year, and until their successorsare elected
and qualified; and annually,afterthedateofthefirst
election, fivedirectorsshall be electedby the stock-
holders .

hold their officesfor one year , and until their success-

cute a bond to the company ,with approved security,

to the Bourbon county clerk for safe-keeping ; and

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1133
any person injured may sue on said bond , and re-
cover such damages as he may have sustained by
any failureof the treasurerto perform his duties.
The treasurer shall collect and distribute the funds
of the company ,and keep a faithfulaccount of same .
But such bond shall not be requiredof the treasurer
if the board of directors shall deem it unnecessary .
§ 4. The purposes of this incorporationis to estab-Purposeofcor-
lish,preserveand beautifya cemeteryforthecoloredPoration.
dead near North Middletown , Bourbon county . The
company may acquire, by purchase or gift,an amount
of land not exceeding fiveacres,and hold the same
as a naturalperson,but only for the purposes of this
incorporation. When the company shall have se-
lected the locationof the cemetery, and so soon as
the price to be paid the owner or owners, as agreed
by the parties,has been paid,the company may pro-
ceed to have the land surveyed and laidoffintocem-
etery lots,with such avenues and walks as may be
deemed proper by the board of directors.
§ 5. The directorsmay sellthelotsupon such termsMayselllots.
as they may think best ; and the purchasers of the
lots,and those who intrusttheirfunds in the enter-

company is authorized to receivedonations for the
purposes of thisact.

certificatesof purchase so signed may be given to
purchasers of lots,designating number and other de-
scription,which may be recorded in the county clerk's
officeas evidenceof title,and preserved by the secre-
tary. The fee-simple of lands acquired for the pur-
poses of this act,afterbeing paid for, shall vest in
the company .
§ 7. This act shall take effect and be in force from
and after passage . Approved May 20, 1890.

town of Glasgow , as they may see fit, to take the

Timeandplacethe order directingthe electionherein provided for

holding the same , and appoint two reputable house-
keepersof the corporationto act as judges of the
election;they shallalsoappoint a clerk and sheriffof
the election, and provide the necessary books for

published at leastthreeconsecutiveweeks next before

CHAPTER 1653 .
AN ACT toprovidefortheconstructionof any leveealong the water

monwealth of Kentucky :
bonds .§1 . That by a three-fourth vote of allthe members Mayissue
of the board of councilmen of the cityof Newport at

Sufficientto
build a levee.
lars, in denominations of eitheror both fivehundred
dollarsor one thousand dollars,dated July first, one
thousand eight hundred and ninety, and bearing in-
terestnot exceeding fiveper cent.per annum , repre-

and coupons shall be payable at the citytreasurer's
officein the cityof Newport ,the former twenty years
after date and the lattersemi-annually. The bonds

afterprovided; and the citytreasurershallhave the

the bonds hereinbeforeprovided for as may be neces-
sary to build a levee along such part of the water-
frontof the cityof Newport as, in their judgment ,
may seem proper and effectual in preventing the
almost annual overflowof the citycontiguous thereto,
and in arriving at such judgment may have such
preliminary surveys and expert opinions as they see
proper. When said board shall have adopted a plan
for said levee,they shall cause the cityengineer to
prepare specificationsfor same , and an estimate of
the cost of the work under same . When the board
shallhave approved specifications, they shall in their
discretion advertise for bids for the work as an en-
tiretyor in parts,or both ; bids shall be made upon
blanks to be prepared and furnished to the bidders
by the cityengineer,and accompanied by bond , with
securitiesto be approved by the board of councilmen,
conditioned that the bidder willdo the work as per
contract, if his bid be accepted . The contract shall

not be awarded to any but the lowest bidder or bid-
ders , but the board may rejectall bids and read-
vertise. When the bid or bids shall be accepted,
appropriate contractsshall be executed, and bonds
to secure the performance of same , with good
security, taken. All work done and material
furnished shall be under and subject to the
inspection, supervision, approval, or rejection of
the board of improvement of the cityof Newport ,
which said board is hereby constitutedthe soleand
final arbitrators to determine whether said work is
being constructed, or has been completed,according
to contract, and on theirreportthe board of council-
men shallaccept or rejectit; and if they shall reject
it, they shall,at the costof the contractorsand their
sureties,in such manner as they deem best,have the
work done to conform to the contract . From time to
time, as the work progresses,the engineershallmake
estimatesof the work done , which , when approved by
said board , shall authorize the board of councilmen to
pay to the contractoron account of the contract not
more than eighty per cent.of the estimate. The spec-
ificationsshallprovide that the contractorshalltake
bonds at theirface value, or cash, at the option of
the city, in payment for the work .
§ 3. The board of councilmen are authorized to
cause any land or materialthat may be necessary for
building said levee to be condemned , and shallpro-
ceed in the manner in which railroadsand turnpike
companies are now by law authorized to do for such
purpose .
§ 4. This act shall take effectfrom and after itspas-
sage .
Approved May 20, 1890.

ary of each year for the electionof directorsand

vious year ."

CHAPTER 1691 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Sodowski Lake Association.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That I. B. Sandusky , J. W. Sandusky , H. C.
Sandusky , H. C. Sandusky, Senior,R. T. Sandusky,
J. D. Woolley , L. C. Gooch , J. H. Blackford, and
theirsuccessorsand assigns,be,and they are hereby ,
created a body -corporate and politic, with perpetual
succession, under the name of the Sodowski Lake
Association; and by that name have power to con-
tract and be contracted with , to sue and be sued , in
allcourts of law and equity.
§ 2. It may acquire by purchase, leaseor donation
land,or the use of land, in Jessamine county , for the
purpose,of the associationhereinaftermentioned,not,
however ,to exceed two hundred acres.
§ 3. The business and aim of said association is to
create a pleasure resortby stopping the waters of
Sinking creek, in Jessamine county, and transform-
ing land, otherwise rendered uselessby continuous
overflow, intoa lake, to be used for stocking and the
propagation of fish.
§ 4. The capital stock of said association shall not
exceed the sum of thirty-fivethousand dollars,to be
divided into shares of fiftydollars each, payable in
such installmentsas the board of directorsmay di-
rect and provide. Payment of allsubscriptionsmay
be enforced by suit in the name of the association,
and no part of said subscriptionshall be due and
payable untilallof the stock issubscribedfor.
§5 . The board of directors shall cause to be issued
to the stockholders certificates of stock when the
same shall be paid in full. Said stock shall be held
as personal property, and shall be transferredonly
on the books of the corporation .
§ 6. The officersof said association shall consist of

1224 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
a board of five directors, who shall be chosen at an
annual meeting of the stockholdersof said associa-
tion to be held on the firstMonday in May in each
year, at the villageof Renu , in Jessamine county ,
and each share of stock held in said association shall
be entitled to one vote .
§ 7. The board of directorsshall, out of theirnum-
ber, electa president, secretaryand treasurer, who
shallhold theirofficesfor the period of one year, or
untiltheir successorsare duly elected and qualified.
§ 8. The management of the businessaffairsof said
association shall be under the control of the board of
directors, who shall have power to make such by-
laws, rules and regulationsfor the management of
said associationas they may deem proper, subject,
however ,to a ratificationby a majority of the stock-
holders in said association , and such as shall not be
inconsistent with the Constitution of the United States
and of this State .
§ 9. Said board of directorsshall regulate the fees
for the privilegeof boating, fishingand hunting on
said lake, or upon the premises owned by said asso-
ciation .
§ 10. Any person or persons who trespass upon the
grounds of said associationby hunting, fishing or
boating, without the permission of the person or per-
sons in charge thereof, shall be guilty of a misde-
meanor ,and shall be fined not less than twenty-five
nor more than fiftydollarsin any court of competent
jurisdiction.
§ 11. This act to take effectfrom and after itspas-
sage .
Approved May 21,1890.

CHAPTER 1692 .

Floyd and Pike to subscribeto the capitalstock of
the Ohio and Big Sandy Railroad Company on the
following terms and conditions,and in the following
manner , to-wit : Upon applicationof said railroad
company to the county court of any of saidcounties,
in writing,such county court may , in theirdiscretion,
the majority of the justicesbeing presentand concur-
ring therein, submit to a vote of the legalvoters of
such county (at a specialelectionto be held forthat
purpose) the questionof subscribingto the capital
stock of said railroadcompany for and on behalf of

tiablebonds of such county or counties having not

a rate not exceeding six per cent. per annum , and
payable at such place as may be designatedtherein,
a sum of money not exceeding fiveper cent. of the
aggregate assessment of the property of such county
or countiesforStaterevenue by the lastpreceding as-
sessment. The order directingsuch submission to a
vote shall fix the time and place of voting,and the
terms and conditions upon which such proposed
subscription shall be made , and the officersby
whom the election shall be held ; and such election
shall be held within one year after the passage of
thisact,and not lessthan twenty nor more than fifty
days after such order shall be made . The officers
holding such electionshall, within fivedays after the
election, return to the officeof the clerkof the county
court the poll-books , and the county judge, sheriff

in saidcounty court clerk'sofficein the order-book of
said court. If a majority of the legal votes cast at

shallbe the duty of the county clerk of such county
to enter the same of record in his office in said order-
book ,and make such subscriptionfor his county to
the capitalstock of such railroadcompany in accord-
ance with thisact,and upon the terms and conditions
setout in the order of submission, and the proposi-
tionvoted on as hereinprovided ;but the county court
shallhave no power or authorityto order the submis-
sionof any question of subscriptionto a vote until
said railroadcompany shall have entered intobond ,
with good and sufficientsurety or sureties,residentof
thisState, in the sum of fiftyper cent.of the amount
of the proposed subscription, to be paid in lawful
money to the sinking fund commissioners or other
designated officersor officerof such county for the
benefitof, and for pro rata distributionamong , the
common school districtsof each county for the pur-
pose of providing school-houses and grounds , furni-
ture and apparatus and efficientteachers for such
schools, ifsuch railroadcompany shall failto comply
with any of the conditionsof such subscriptionas set
'forth in the proposition voted on. The bond and
suretiesshall be taken and approved by the county
judge of such county .

scribe the terms and conditions of such subscriptions ,

the questionof subscriptionto a vote ;but these con-
ditions shall be inserted in the order of submission ,

scriptionto the capitalstock of the Ohio and Big

county or countiesvoting same, if said company

1
shall failto construct, or cause to be constructed,

within fivemiles of the south lineof Pike county, if

gotiable bonds of such county,of the face value of

ing or having the care of any stock to permit the

1232 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
same to run at large in the Chatham and German-
town precincts, in Bracken county ; and any person
so offending shallbe liableto such penaltiesas are
prescribedand provided for in thisact.
§2 . That it shallbe the duty of the sheriff, or any
of his deputies,or constable, to seizeany stock found
running at large in said precinct, and impound said
stock,and providefor theircare and keeping until
the owner or keeper of such stock shall have re-
deemed them by the payment of all fees and costs
incident to said procedure .
§ 3. That whenever any stock shall be found tres-
passing upon the inclosureof any person, the owner
of such stock shall be liablefor alldamages done and
for all fees and costsincidentto the prosecution of
such trespass, recoverablebefore any court having
jurisdiction; and the party injured shall have a lien
upon such stock for alldamages , costsand fees.
§ 4. That the officershall be entitledto the follow-
ing fees for taking up stock running at largeor tres-
passing upon the lands of any one : For each horse,
jack, mule or colt over six months old, one dollar;
for each bull, cow , steer, heifer or calf over six
months old, fiftycents, and for each sheep or hog
over three months old, twenty -five cents ; and all
expenses incurred by him in impounding and taking
care of the same : Provided , The owner of said stock
is not known to be in immediate pursuit of same .
§ 5. That the officermaking the seizure shall adver-
tiseall stock seizedby him (if not redeemed within
fivedays afterthe seizureof same ) for salein at least
threeprominent places in said precinctsten days be-
fore selling. The sale shall be made on a credit of
three months , the purchaser giving bond , with ap-
proved security, payable to the officermaking the
sale,who shall be entitled to receive the same commis-
sion and feesas are allowed by law for sales under
execution for debt ; and when the bond shall be col-
lected, he shall pay the residue, afterdeducting all

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1233
expenses and costs, to the owner of said stock, and
the officershallbe responsibleon his officialbond .
The officeror person injured by the trespassingof
stock shall firstapply to a justiceof the peace, or
proceed in a court of competent jurisdiction, for an
order of saleof such stock, and the costsof such pro-
ceeding,and for taking up and impounding the same ;
that ifsaidstockshallbe orderedto be sold,the offi-
cer making said sale shall advertisethe time,place
and terms of sales as officersare requred to do under
executive sales of personal property on a credit of
three months .
§ 6. This act shall be submitted to the qualified
votersof the Chatham and Germantown precinctsat
the regularAugust election,one thousand eight hun-
dred and ninety,and itshallbe the duty of the officers
of said election, to be held on the firstMonday in
August ,one thousand eight hundred and ninety, to
open a poll in said precincts, and propound to each
voter who may offerto vote at saidelectionthe ques-
tion, "Are you for or against the stock law ?" and
record his vote in accordance to his response to said
question, if he desiresto vote and does vote thereon ;
the vote shallbe certifiedand returned and compared
as they are requiredin electionsfor Stateand county
officers; and when it shall appear,by the certificate
of the examining board, that a majority of the voters
voting were in favor thereof, thisactshalltake effect
and be in forcein eitheror both of said precincts,ac-
cording to the majority in eitheror both of said pre-
cincts .
§ 7. This act to take effectfrom itspassage.
78- LOCAL LAWS , VOL . 3 .
Approved May 21,1890.

CHAPTER 1709 .
AN ACT to amend an act to revisethe charterof the city of Paducah ,
and reduce into one the severalacts relatingthereto.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. In addition to the power already conferred on
the council of the city of Paducah , and in order to
facilitatethe trade of the inhabitants of the city, the
councilof said cityshallhave the power to acquire,
by purchase , dedication or lease, at any point on the
Ohio and Tennessee riverswithin the present limits
of the cityof Paducah , the ground necessaryfor one
or more ferrylandings; and the power , in the name
of and for the city of Paducah , to acquire, own ,
operate and maintain a ferryor ferries, and ply the
same from any of said landings across the Ohio and
Tennessee rivers, to any point at low-water mark on
the Illinoisshore, from within one mile below to
within four miles above said town of Brooklyn ,Illi-
nois .
§2 . The council shall also have the power to ap-
ply for and obtain from the county court of Massac
county , in the State of Illinois, a license or priv-
ilege to operate, run and maintain a ferryor ferries
across the Ohio riverfrom any point on the Illinois

1260 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
shore , from within one mile below to within four
miles above the town of Brooklyn , in the State of
Illinois,to any point on the Kentucky shore wi.hin
the city of Paducah . The council shall have the
further power for and in the name of the city of
Paducah , to acquire, by purchase, dedication or
lease, within the points above -named in this sec-
tion, the ground necessary for one or more ferry
landings in the State of Illinois, and may convey
or assignthe same to others forthe purpose of ferry
landings, and to operatea ferryor ferrieswithin the
limitshereinprescribed.
§ 3. The councilof the cityof Paducah shall have
the power to appropriate money not to exceed three
thousand dollarsfor the purchase of saidferry land-
ingson the Illinoisshore, and the improvement of
same and approaches thereto.
§ 4. This act shall take effectfrom its passage .
Approved May 22,1890 .

CHAPTER 1730 .
AN ACT to amend an act,entitled"An act to prohibitthe saleof
spirituous,vinousand malt liquorsin Hardin county," approvedMay
fifth,one thousandeighthundred and eighty-four.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That an act, entitled"An act to prohibit the
saleof spirituous, vinous and malt liquorsin Hardin
county," approved May fifth, one thousand eight
hundred and eighty-four, be amended by adding
theretothe followingprovisions: If any person shall
keep a tippling-house,he shallbe fined two hundred
dollars,and may also,in the discretionof the jury, be
confined in the county jailnot lessthan one month
nor more than twelve months . Twice sellingin the
same house contrary to law shallbe evidence of the
keeping of a tippling-house.
§2 . That any person who shall rent property to
another,who shall use said property for the purpose
of sellingtherein spirituous, vinous or malt liquors
contraryto law,shallbe fined one hundred dollarsa
day for each day he permits said property to be so
used afterhe has noticethat the property is used for
this purpose, and neglectsto turn the tenant out.
Such conduct shall terminate any lease, and give the
owner the rightto immediate possession.
§3 . That it shall be unlawful for any person to
have in his possession, for the purpose of sellingthe

1302 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
same in said county,any spirituous, vinous or malt
liquors, and any person so offending shall be fined
not lessthan twenty nor more than fivehundred dol-
lars; and ifany person shallpay to the United States
government the tax required thereby for the retail-
ing of spirituous, vinous or malt liquors in Hardin
county , covering the time when the offense charged
in the indictment isallegedto have been committed ,
and shallhave in possessionsuch liquorsin any place
except his privatedwelling and itsdependencies, it
shallbe prima facie evidence of guilt under this
section .
§ 4. That itshallbe theduty of every peace officerto
see that thisact, and the said act of which this act is
amendatory ,are faithfullyenforced; and if any such
officerhas reason to believethat any one has violated
or is violatingthe same ,it shall be his duty imme-
diatelyto lay the factsbeforethe nearest magistrate,
who shall diligently investigate the matter , summon-
ing before him ,by subpœna , allpersons whose testi-
mony he may have reason to believewill aid in said
investigation,and examining them on oath to enable
him to ascertain the offender and issue a warrant for
his arrest. It shallbe the duty of all peace officers
to receiveand execute promptly all process issued in
such cases and delivered to them for execution . If,
on said investigation, it shall appear that spirituous,
vinous or malt liquorshave been sold in any house or
premises,the magistrateshallissuea search warrant,
directingsome peace officerat once to search said
house or premises,and to seizeand hold, subject to
the order of court, any spirituous, vinous or malt
liquors, or instruments for the sale thereof, found
therein, and to arrest the owner or controller thereof
and bring him beforesaid magistrateforan examin-
ing trial. If, on said trial, thereshall be reasonable
grounds for believingthe defendant guilty,he shall
be held over for further trial, and if, on final trial, he
isconvicted,the court shallorder the property seized

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1303
under the search warrant destroyed. If the defend-
ant isacquitted, the property seizedshallbe restored
to the owner . The search warrant in such cases shall
specify the premises to be searched, the party owning
or controllingthe same ,and the property to be seized
as particularlyas may be,and in executing the same ,
the peace officermay summon to his assistancethe
power of the county, and if admission be not given
on demand made , shall force an entrance .
§ 5. That any peace officer,having reason to believe
that the law has been violated, who shallfailto give
informationto some magistrate, and any magistrate
having such information,or having reason to believe
that the law has been violated, who shall neglect
promptly to investigatethe matter as hereindirected,
sha llbe fined not less than twenty nor more than five
hundred dollars, recoverable on his officialbond .
The following officersshallbe deemed magistrates,
viz: Judges of county courts, judges of city or
policecourts and justicesof the peace ; and the fol-
lowing shallbe deemed peace officers, viz: Sheriffs,
-constables,coroners, jailers, marshalsand policemen.
§ 6. That allprosecutionsunder this act,or the act
of which this is amendatory , shallstand for trialat
the same term of court at which the indictment is
returned: Provided , That process has been served
on the defendant ten days before the day of trial.
When an indictment has been returned, it shallbe
the duty of the court to set the case for trialfor a
day not lessthan twenty days from that time, and a
bench warrant for the defendant and subpœnas for
the witnesses for the Commonwealth shall be imme-
diatelyissuedreturnableon thatday . It shallbe the
duty of the clerk to put saidprocess in the hands of
the sheriffas soon as it is issued, and he shall
at once execute the same . If the clerk shall
neglect to issue said process and place it in the
hands of the sheriff,ashereindirected,orifthe sheriff
shallneglect to serve the same in a reasonabletime,

1304 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the party so neglectinghisduty shallbe finednot less.
than ten nor more than one hundred dollars, recover-
able by rule on motion of the Commonwealth attorney ..
If for any reason the case isnot ready for trialon the
day for which it isset, the court shallsetitfor an-
other day during the same term for trial, if in the
judgment of the court it can be done without preju-
dice to other cases on the docket previously set for
trial .
$ 7 . That this act shall take effect from and after
June the first,one thousand eighthundred and ninety,
but shallnot be in force in any magisterialdistrict
that shall vote against prohibition under the act
passed at this sessionof the General Assembly , en-
titled " An act resubmitting to the voters of Hardin
county the questionas to whether or not spirituous,
vinous or malt liquorsshallbe sold in said county :
Provided ,This act shall not be construed so as to
apply to distillers,or any liquorin the possession of
distillersor manufacturers of vinous or malt liquors.
Approved May 22,1890.

CHAPTER 1750 .
AN ACT to permit the booming of the tributariesof Licking river
in the countiesof Rowan , Morgan , Menefee and Magoffin.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That any person or persons engaged in timber
ing on the tributariesof Licking river, in the counties
of Rowan ,Menefee,Morgan and Magoffin,may erect
and maintain booms to catchand hold logs,timber,
et cetera, at or near the mouth of said tributaries, or
wherever a convenient place may be had for assort-
ing,driving and raftingsame .
§ 2. No charge shallbe made by the owners of said

1332 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
booms forcatching the timber of any person or per-
sons upon said streams ;but if, upon being notifiedto
take same from the boom and secure it,said parties
failto do so promptly ,the owners of saidboom may
take out or securesaid logs and timber for saidpar-
ties,and they shall have a claim againstsaid timber
and party owning itforcostof doing same .
§ 3. If the owners of lands upon what itisthought
desirableto securesaidboom object thereto, the par-
tiesdesiringto erect and maintain same shall first
give a good and sufficientbond to said owner to se-
cure him or them from present or prospectivedam-
ages. If they can not agree,the county judge shall
be judge of the sufficiencyof said bond .
§ 4. Any person or personswillfullyor maliciously
cut away , damage , weaken , or in any way interfere
with any of said booms ,whether same resultsin loss
or not,shall,upon indictmentand convictionthereof,
be fined in any sum not lessthan twenty-fivedollars
nor more than five hundred dollars, or be confined
in the county jailnot less than ten days nor more
than six months , or both , at the discretion of the
court or jury trying the case; and if lossor damage
result therefrom , he shall be liablefor three times the
amount thereof, to be recovered in any court of com-
petent jurisdiction.
§ 5. No person or persons building or maintaining
a boom under the provisionsof this act, shall hinder
or in any way prevent any person or persons from
using same for raftingor securing their logs, or for
any other purpose not inconsistenttherewith; and
any person so offendingshallbe fined in any sum not
lessthan twenty-fivenor more than one hundred dol-
lars, to be recovered in any court having competent
jurisdiction.
§ 6. This act shall take effect from and after its
passage .
[Became a law May 23, 1890,withoutthe approvalof the Governor.]

CHAPTER 1751 .
AN ACT toprohibitthe saleof spirituous, vinousor malt liquorsin
Knott county .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That whoever sellsany spirituous, vinous or
malt liquorsin Knott county shall, upon conviction,
be finedone hundred dollars, recoverableupon a war-
rant issuedby the judge of Knott County Court, the
policejudge of the town of Hindman , a justiceof the
peace of Knott county,or upon an indictmentby the
Knott Circuit Court .
§ 2. It shallbe the duty of the judge of the Knott
County Court, the policejudge of the town of Hind-
man ,and of each justiceof the peace of Knott county ,
upon personalknowledge ,or upon the affidavitof an-
other that the provisions of the firstsection of this act
has been violated, to issue a warrant for the arrest of
such offender, and place same in the hands of some
ministerialofficerof Knott county,who shall,without
delay, proceed to arrest such offender and bring
him before some peace officerof Knott county for
trial .
§ 3. Any peace officeror any ministerialofficerwho
failsor refusesto perform the dutiesherein imposed ,
shallbe liableto indictment, and , upon conviction,
shall be fined one hundred dollars .
§ 4. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
[Became a law May 23, 1890, withoutthe approvalof theGovernor.]

False Registration .
§ 13. Any person who shall cause himself to be
registeredin more than one ward ,otherwise than is
provided for in sectionten of this act,or more than
once in the same ward , or who shall cause himself
to be registered,knowing that he is not twenty -one
years of age,or knowing that he is not lawfully en-
titledto registration,and any person who shall aid
or abet in the commission of any of said acts, he
shallbe deemed guiltyof a misdemeanor ,and shall
be fined not exceeding two hundred dollars, or im-
prisoned in the county jail not less than one nor
more than twelve months , or both , in the discretion
of the jury.

CHAPTER 1761 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the town of Quincy , in Lewis
county .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That on the firstSaturday in April, one thou-
sand eight hundred and ninety, there shallbe elected
fivetrusteesand one policejudge, one town marshal,
who shall be elected by the bona fidevoterswithin
the limits of said corporation .
§2 . That no person shall be entitled to a vote in
said electionfor the said town officers,only persons
who are bona fide housekeepers within the limits of
said corporation .
§3 . That said trustees and their successors in office
shallbe a body -politicand corporate, and known by
the name and style of the Board of Trustees of the
Town of Quincy ; and by that name be capable of
contractingand being contractedwith, and of suing
and being sued,and of pleading and being impleaded,
in all the courts of this Commonwealth ; and the said
town officersshallhave allthe powers and privileges,
and be subjectto the same duties and liabilities, as is
provided by the generallaws and in relationto towns
incorporatedin thisCommonwealth .
§ 4. That said trustees shall elect out of their num-
ber a chairman ,clerkand treasurer,and shallkeep a
correctrecord of alltheirproceedings,which record
shall be opened for the inspectionof any person con-
cerned in the said town ; and the said board of trus-
tees shall meet at least once in each month for the
transactionof business, and shallhave power to enact
ordinances, by -laws and regulations for the good
government of said town , not inconsistentwith the
Constitutionand generallaws of thisState; and shall
have power to levy a tax on the real and personal

1372 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
tate, and said tax shallnot exceed twenty -fivecent
on the one hundred dollars'worth of taxable property
within the limitsof said corporation; said tax may
be paid by the performance of labor when so directed
by the said trustees.
§5 . That it shall require a concurrence of a ma-
jorityof said trusteesto exercisethe powers granted
in this act, and in the event of a failure to elect the
officersherein named ,annually, on the firstSaturday
in April,thatthe trusteesshallcontinue in officeun-
tiltheirsuccessorsshallbe duly electedand quali-
fied; and said trusteesshall have the power to fill
such vacancies as may occur in their board to fillthe
unexpired time only.
§ 6. That all fines for the violation of the ordi-
nances of the said incorporationshall be paid into
the town funds to be used for the public improve-
ment of said town .
§ 7. That the policejudge of said town shall have
the same power and jurisdictionof a justiceof the
peace, and shall give bond and take an oath before
the clerk of the Lewis County Court,beforehe enters
upon thedutiesof hisoffice,thathe willfaithfullyper-
form the dutiesof policejudge, to the best of his abil-
ity, of said town , in additionto the oath required by
the Constitutionof thisState;and he shallbe subject
toallthe penaltiesfora violationof law,the same as a
justiceof the peace, and shallhave the same power to
hold examining courts, and try allcriminal and civil
cases the same as a justiceof the peace, and his fees
shallbe the same as a justiceof the peace for similar
services;and the said judge shallhold a regularterm
of policecourt once in each month , and may hold
special terms when necessary .
§ 8. That the said trustees shall have full control
over the streetsand alleys, and shallkeep the same in
good repair,and said trusteesmay have the power to
assess a tax upon persons who may be doing heavy or

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1373
injurioushauling on and over the same , to an amount
not to exceed the actualdamage done thereby.
§ 9. That there shallbe no ardent spiritssoldwithin
thelimitsof the said corporation under any consider-
ation,and the county judge shall not issuelicenseto
any person or persons to sell intoxicatingor malt
liquorswithin the limits of the said town , and any
license so issued shall be void .
§10. That no druggist, residingwithin the limits
of said corporation, shall be permitted to sellany
spirituousor malt liquors only on prescription, and
no prescriptionshallbe legalunlessdated and signed
by a regularpracticingphysician authorized to prac-
tice medicine under the laws of this Commonwealth
showing the cause therefor.
§ 11. That if any druggistsellany of the aforesaid
liquorscontrary to the provisionsof thisact,he shall,
upon conviction by indictment by the grand jury
upon the warrant issuedby any justiceof the peace
or policejudge in saidtown or county , be fined no
lessthan sixty dollarsnor more than one hundred
dollars for each offense .
§12. That any section, or part thereof,as provided
inthe charterfor the town of Quincy ,in county of
Lewis, not consistentwith the provisionsof this
act, which was approved March twenty -eight, one
thousand eight hundred and seventy-two , shall be,
and ishereby , repealed.
§ 13. This act shall take effect from and after its
passage .
Approved May 23, 1890.

votingplacein the fourth magisterialdistrict, in Ken-

CHAPTER 1765 .

provement Company .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That H. D. Bellew, George S. Lancaster,G. T.
Incorporation. O'Bryan , Abraham Balee, Martin Froehlich, R. S.
Edelen , and their associates, assigns and successors,

under the name and style of " The Goose and Deer
Pond Drainage and Improvement Company ," with
perpetual succession, and the power to contract and
be contracted with, sue and be sued, plead, answer
and defend, in any and all courts as a natural
person ; have and use a corporate seal, and alter,
change and amend the same at pleasure.

ditch, improve and benefitcertainlands in Daveiss
county, Kentucky , known as " Goose and Deer

landof MistressSteele,B. T. Kelly, Thomas Hundley
and James Duke , followingthe best course and the
one that will most effectuallydrain and dry the

northernlineof H. D. Bellew to a ditchopened by
G. T. O'Bryan ; and to improve and benefitthe gen-
eralhealth and sanitaryconditionof saiddistrict
and the country adjacent thereto.

company is hereby authorized, empowered and in-
vestedwith the right,power and authoritynecessary

towiden and deepen ditches and drains,and to con-
demn lands necessaryfor theiruse ; and to assessthe
lands of all partiesin said districtin proportion to
benefitsreceived, and to collectsame in the manner
herein provided.

the passage of this act, said corporators, a ma-
jorityof them , shall meet at such place in Daveiss
county, Kentucky , as they may have agreed on, and
from theirnumber , and such personsas they may

and conditions of payment thereof; and said com-

CHAPTER 1771 .
AN ACT to charterthe Elkhorn City Boom Company .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1. That W. M. Pally, James Hatcher , R. M.
Ferrelland James Pallay, are hereby createda body-
politicand corporate, under the firmname and style

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1397
of the Elkhorn City Boom Company ; and as such are
made capable in law of suing and being sued, of
pleading and being impleaded , of defending and
being defended, in all the courts of this Common-
wealth .
§2 . That as such corporation, they shallhave the
rightand power to erect a boom or booms in and
acrossthe Russell'sfork of the Big Sandy river, at
any point they may desire from Bowen's rock to the
Stateline, between the State of Kentucky and Vir-
ginia. That the objectand purposes of such boom or
booms is hereby declared to be for the purpose of
booming ,catchingand holding saw -logs afloaton the
waters of said river, and delivering them to the own-
ers thereof, upon the payment of charges,which are
hereinafter fixed .
§ 3. That the corporation shall be allowed to
charge and receivefrom each owner , his agent or
representative, any sum agreed upon , not exceeding
twenty cents foreach log so taken,boomed ,held and
delivered .
§ 4. That the corporation shallhave a lienon the
said logsso caught and held by them for the charges ;
and when it becomes necessary to open said boom or
booms , if any of the owners of such logs should not
be present and able to take care of their logs, or
being present,should failand refuse to do so, then
the said corporationshall be authorizedto raft said
loose logs and take care of said raftsas they may
deem best, and charge a reasonable compensation
therefor, and shallhave a like lienon the logs thus
rafted for raftingand caring for as is given herein
forcatching; said liens shall be enforced by action
in any of the courtsof thisCommonwealth under the
general laws regulating liens.
§5 . Said boom or booms shall be so constructed as
to permit raftsand flat-boats to pass on the demand
of the owner of said rafts or boats, other persons
having controlof said raftsor boats : Provided , That

1398 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
nothing in this section shall authorize the owners of
such raftsor boats,those having controlof the same ,
forsaidowners to open said boom or booms whenever
itwould endanger the losses of said loose logs then
held by said boom or booms by floatingthem out of
saidcreek : Provided further, That saidcorporation,
owners and keepers of saidboom or booms ,shall not
be held responsibleforunavoidable accidents.
§ 6. This act shall take effectand be in force from
and after itspassage .
[Became a law May 22,1890,withoutthe approvalof the Governor.]

fivecents,and when drawn by more than two horses,

ney-General,and by such othercounselas the Gov-
ernor may designate,theproperjudicialproceedings
