quishment of any forfeiture, or of any ground or

CHAPTER 1782 .

monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . So much of the act to amend the charter of the
city of Covington, approved February twenty -sixth,
one thousand eight hundred and eighty-six, as au-
thorizesthe school board of the cityof Covington to
issue bonds for the erection of additional school
buildings in said city, is hereby repealed; but this
repeal shall not be held to invalidateany bonds
already issuedunder the provisionsof saidlaw .
§2 . The schoolboard of said cityishereby author-
ized to issuebonds to an amount not exceeding twenty-
five thousand dollars, in such denomination as said
board shall determine , and bearing interest at a rate
not exceeding four per cent.per annum , and payable
as said board may elect,eitherin fifteenyears from
date, or fivethousand dollarsin fiveyears,fivethou-
sand dollarsin ten years, and fivethousand dollars
in fifteenyears from date, or in such proportionsand
amounts as the board shall direct . Said board shall
not sellsaidbonds at lessthan par and accrued inter-
est. The proceeds thereof are to be used exclusively
in making additionsand repairsto the presentschool
buildings,and providing heating and ventilatingap-
paratus and facilitiesforsame .

of said board and countersignedby itsclerk, and may

nually at such place or places as said board shall
determine , and shallbe exempt from taxationby said
city. To secure the payment of said bonds , allthe
public school property held by said board is hereby
pledged , and so much as necessaryof the city taxes
allowed to the school board of said city is hereby
pledged to the payment of said coupons .

scribed by law for members of said board .

CHAPTER 1785 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe "C. T. Rice Cemetery Company ," near
Union , in Boone county .
Be it enactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That C. T. Rice,J. W. Conner ,James A. Huey ,
M. C. Norman and B. L. Rice , and their successors,
be, and they are hereby,createda body -corporateand
politic,under the name and style of " The C. T. Rice
Cemetery Company ;" and by that name said com -
pany shall have perpetual succession, and shall be
able and capable in law to have and use a common
seal, to sue and to be sued, plead and be impleaded ,
defend and be defended , in all courts of this Com-
monwealth and elsewhere .
§2 . They shallhave power to acquire and hold,
by purchase, gift or devise, real and personal es-
tate necessary to provide a suitable cemetery for
burialof the dead in the county of Boone and in the
vicinityof Union , not exceeding twenty-five (25)
acres of land , and of more value than two thousand
dollars($2,000), and to receivea conveyance of the
same in itscorporate name ; and when the same is so
conveyed, itshallnever be held or used forany other
purpose than that of a cemetery ; shallbe foreverfree
and exempt from all executions or other legal pro-
cess, and from any charge or assessment to or for any
public purpose inconsistentwith its use as a ceme-
tery, forwhich purpose itisforeverdedicated.
§3. The affairsof this corporation shall be man-
aged by the above-named corporators,or their repre-
sentatives, who willelectannually from theirnumber
a president, secretaryand treasurer,who shall hold
their officefor one year , and until their successors
are electedand qualified. The treasurershall exe-
cute a bond to the company , with approved security,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY.- 1441
forthe faithfulperformance of allhisduties; and it
shallbe the duty of the treasurerto collectand to
pay out the funds of the company , and to keep a
recordthereof; but he shallpay out money only on
the orderof the president, attestedby the secretary.
The firstelectionshallbe held on the firstMonday in
January, one thousand eight hundred and ninety-
one,and annually thereafter.
§4. They shallcause to be prepared and kept an
accuratemap and survey of the grounds and lots in
saidcemetery .
§5. They shallhave power to make by -laws for the
regulationand controlof lots, and for the control,
careand management of the grounds and graves,and
the mode of ornamenting the same .
§6. They shallhave power to enterupon any of the
lotsand remove any ornaments , fixtures or shrubbery
thatmay be planted on said lotsagainst the regula-
tionsor by -laws of saidcompany .
§7 . They shall have power to sell burial lots at
eitherpublicor privatesale, and when sold it shallbe
theduty of the presidentto execute to the purchaser
a certificateof sale,giving the number of the lot or
lotsas purchased by him ,which shall vest the pur-
chaser with titleto said lot or lots, said certificateto
be attestedby the secretary; and the fullpower of
rejectingany bid for lot or lotsis reserved by the
company .
§ 8. If, from any cause,the electionis not held for
officersas herein required, then the secretaryshall
give ten days' written notice,settingforththe time
and place of holding same .
§ 9. The titleof any lotmay be transferredaccord-
ingto such rulesas may be prescribedby the by -laws
of the corporation, but in no otherway . Lots shall
descend as other real estate of an adult, except that
the survivinghusband or wife shallfirstinheritthe
entirety.
91 - LOCAL LAWS , VOL . 3 .

1442 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 10. That upon the death of any one of the above
named corporatives, the remaining members shall
have the power to selectfrom the family or repre-
sentativesof said deceased a person to representhim
in said corporation,and such person so selectedshall
have the same powers as one of the original corpo-
ratives .
§ 11. That M. C. Norman be president, B. L. Rice
secretary,and James A. Huey treasurer,to hold their
officesuntiltheirsuccessorsare electedand qualified.
§ 12. Any purchaser failingto pay the contract
price fora lotor lotsbargained for within the space
of three months from the date of such contract shall
forfeitallclaim to same , and the lot or lots, with all
improvements thereon,shallrevertto the corporation,
and be held as if never sold. If any interments have
been made in such lotor lots, the company shallhave
power to remove , or cause the bodies to be removed ,
to any publicgrounds kept forburial purposes, or to
a place set apart for that purpose in said cemetery :
Provided , That the saidcompany shallgive a written
notice to the person or persons in whose name such
lotor lotsare purchased of at leastthirtydays before
such action; and if such person or persons can not be
found , then said noticeisto be posted in said lot or
lots for sixty days .
§ 13. That any lot owner who attempts to inter a col-
ored person in any lot in said cemetery shall forfeit
said lot to said company , with allitsimprovements ,
and the said company shallhave power to prohibit
the interment of such coloredperson.
§ 14. That if any person or persons shall forcible,
and without lawful authority, violate any of the
graves of the dead ,or any vaultwhich may be erected
on the grounds of the company , or willfullyinjure
any of the ornaments,shrubbery,fencing, fixturesor
buildings, or in any way damage the grounds or
other property of the company ,such person or per-
sons so offending, and those who may aid or abet

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1443
therein, shallbe punished by finenot lessthan ten
dollarsor imprisonment in the county jailnot ex-
ceedingsix months , or both, which may be by war-
rantbefore any court having jurisdiction, and shall
alsobe liableto the company in an actionfordamages
inany courthaving jurisdictionthereof, which dam-
ages,when recovered, and fines, shallbe applied by
the company to the repairingthe injury committed ,
and the residue,if any ,to be used in repairingand
improving the ground and fixtures.
§ 15. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved May 24 , 1890 .

CHAPTER 1790 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Runyon School-house and Four Mile
Road Turnpike Company .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That a company is hereby created a body-
politicand corporate, under the name and style of
the " Runyon School-house and Four Mile Road
Turnpike Company ," for the purpose of constructing
a macadamized turnpike, from a point at or near
Runyon's school-house , in Madison county, on the
Drowning Creek and Union Turnpike Road , the best
and most practicableroute,to a point on the Four
Mile dirt road , near the Milo Baxter farm , at the
crossingof the East Fork of Otter creek .
§2 . The capitalstock of saidcompany may be any
amount desired, not exceeding seventhousand dollars,
and this may be divided into shares of fiftydollars
each, and every holder of one share of this stock
shall be entitled to one vote .
§3 . That W. C. French , James Noland , William
Jett and John A. Turpin are hereby authorized to
takesubscriptionsto the stock of saidcompany , and
forthispurpose they may open books and may re-
ceive subscriptions for a sufficientamount to con-
struct said road .
§4 . That as soon as a sufficientamount of stock
is subscribed, the commissioners named in section
threemay calla meetingof the stockholders,having
given ten days ' written notice to each stockholder
of the time and place and purpose of the meeting,
and at this meeting a presidentand two directors
shallbe elected, and these may appoint such other
officersas they may deem necessary to conduct the
businessof the company .
§ 5. The board of directorsmay have power to sue

1460 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and be sued,plead and be impleaded, contractand be
contractedwith, and make such by -laws as they may
deem necessary,and do any and all things necessary
to carry out the purpose of this act; and they may
acquireby purchase,gift,deed orotherwise,theright
of way for the road and the ground forthe toll-houses,
and such timber, rock and quarries as may be re-
quired for the construction and completion of the
road, and forkeeping same in repair.
§ 6. That the president and directorsshall hold
their officefor the term of one year from date of
election, and they shall have the power to erect a
toll-gate or toll-gateson said road,and such tollshall
be charged and collectedas are allowed by law ; and
whenever two and one -half miles of said road are
completedand open fortravel,then the saiddirect-
ors may cause to be erected a toll-house, at which
one -half tollmay be charged and collected.
§ 7. The stock in said company shall be transfera-
ble, provided the same shallhave been fullypaid up .
§ 8. The said corporationshall have fullpower to
do all other act or acts that may be necessaryto lo-
cate, construct and complete said road ; and this act
shall take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved May 24,1890.

CHAPTER 1792 .

CHAPTER 1795 .
AN ACT in relationtolandlordsand tenantsin the cityof Covington.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1. Any tenant rentingor occupying the property
ofanotherby any agreement,not in writing,and fail-
ingto pay the rent stipulatedto be paid upon the day
agreedupon ,shall, aftersaid day ,be held to be and
be a tenantat willor sufferance;and upon the refusal
ofsuch tenant upon demand to give possessionof the
propertyso occupied or rented, such person may ,
upon twentydays'noticeto surrenderpossessionof
saidproperty,be evictedfrom such premises by pro-
ceedingsin forcibleentry and detainer.
§2. The noticeprovided for in the precedingsection
may be served by any officerauthorized by law to
servesummons ,by deliveringa copy thereof to the
tenantoccupying the premises; or if such tenant can
notbe found ,by leavinga copy thereofat the tenant's
usualplace of residencewith some person over sixteen
yearsof age, or to be tacked upon or posted upon the
premises .
§3. Thisactshallonlyapply tothecityof Coving-
ton .
§4. All actsor partsof actsin conflictwith thisact
arehereby repealed, so far as the same relatesto the
cityof Covington.
§5 . This act shall take effectand be in force from
and after itspassage .
Approved May 24 , 1890 .

WHEREAS , James S. Patton, Frank M. Savage and
L. T. Everett have been incorporatedin the county of
Boyd , under the general law of this Statefor the or-
ganizationof incorporatedcompanies other than bank-
ing, insurance and railroad companies , under the
corporatename of the Midland Land Company ; and
whereas , said company desires further charter priv-
ilegesthan those granted by the general law ; and
whereas , the personsincorporatedherein are the same
who are incorporatedin the Midland Land Company ;
therefore,

monwealth of Kentucky :

T. Everett, theirassociatesand assigns,be, and they
are hereby,constituteda body -politic, by the name
and styleof Keys Creek Mining and Transportation
Company , with perpetual succession; and by that
name and stylethey are empowered to contractand
be contractedwith,to have ,enjoy and retain,to them-
selves and their successors, lands ,tenements ,heredita-
ments ,goods,chattelsand effects, as may be necessary
and convenient for the working of coal, building blast
furnaces , machine shops , rolling mills and any works
for the manufacture of wood , clay or iron; for the
buildingof boats, and allother power and privileges
granted to any otherlikecorporationin thisCommon-
wealth for said purposes .
§2 . They may build and constructall railroadson
and from their lands in Boyd county which they

river, for the purpose of transportingtheir minerals
and other natural products, and for transporting all

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1469
articlesmanufactured by them thereon : Provided ,
they firstobtain rights of way over the lands .

thorizedagents, may agree with the owners of any

wanted for the constructionor repairof said roads,
orany of theirworks ,for the purchase in fee-simple,

wealth; they may have a common seal, and alterthe

hundred thousand dollars, which shall be divided
intoshares of one hundred dollarseach ; and by the

products thereof, and for purpose necessaryor con-
venientforthe carrying on of thisbusiness,and may
disposeof theirproperty of any kind by sale or

such part of the subscriptionto the capitalstock as
theymay deem advisable,and shallhave therightto

CHAPTER 1806 .
AN ACT topunishpetitlarcenyinUnion county.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That justicesof the peace, the policejudges
and the judge of the quarterly court for the county
of Union shallhave jurisdiction, concurrentwith the
circuitcourt of said county , in all cases where a
person is charged with petit larceny .
§ 2. And allpersons brought before any justiceof
the peace, policejudge or judge of the quarterly
courtfor said county, charged with petit larceny,
shall try said charge without the interventionof a
grand jury or indictment.
§ 3. That any male person over the age of fifteen
years, who shall be convicted of petit larceny in
either of said courts, or the circuit court of said
county, shall be punished by having not less than
ten nor more than thirty-nine lasheswell laid on his
bare back by the sheriff,any constableor marshal of
any town in saidcounty .
§ 4. This act to take effect and be in force from its
passage .
Approved May 24,1890.

magisterialdistrict, at the general August election,
once every two years from August ,one thousand eight
hundred and ninety-two . The saidpetition,order for
such election, noticeof election,return and certificate
of the resultand record thereof, shall in allthings
conform to the requirements provided herein in case
of the electionof one thousand eight hundred and
ninety-two. And if it shall appear from the certifi-
cateand returns of any such electionthat a majority
of the qualifiedvoters in any magisterial district
shallbe in favor of working the public roads therein
by taxation, then the provisions of the act hereby
amended shall be deemed to be in full force for the
two succeeding years ; and if it shall appear from
such returnand certificatethat a majorityof the qual-
ifiedvoters in any such magisterial districtwas cast
againstworking roads by taxation, then said act shall
be deemed suspended in such district, and the pro-
visionsof the General Statutes, chapter ninety-four,
shall be revived and in force in such district until the
same shallbe changed as herein provided.
§ 3. This act shall be in force from and after itspas-
sage .
Approved May 26, 1890.

CHAPTER 1844 .
AN ACT to amend an act,entitled" An act to establishthe Lexington
policeand firedistrict, inFayettecounty,and toprovideforthegov-
ernment thereof," approved Februarytwenty-seventh,one thousand
eighthundred and eighty-six.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That an act,entitled" An act to establishthe
Lexington policeand firedistrict, in Fayette county,
and to provide for the government thereof," ap-
proved February twenty-seventh, one thousand eight
hundred and eighty-six,be,and the same is hereby ,
amended as follows: The territoryherein bounded
and described , and none other, shall be embraced in
the Lexington police and fire districtof Fayette
county, to wit: All of the cityof Lexington and that
portionof the county of Fayette outside of the city
of Lexington, and contiguous thereto, known as the
Woodlands . The said Woodlands are bounded on
thenorth-west by the cityof Lexington, on the south-
eastby the Ashland estate, north-east by the Rich-
mond pike,and south-west by the Tate'screek pike.
§2 . There shallbe leviedannually by the court of
claimsof Fayette county, a tax upon the property in
that portion of said district outside of the limits of
saidcity, sufficientto pay such portionof the expense
for maintaining the police and firedepartments for
the preceding year, as the assessed value of said
propertyforsuch year shallbear to the value of the
propertyin the whole of the districthereby created,as
assessedfor Stateand county taxation,and shallcause
thesame to be paid to the cityof Lexington, which ,
in view of the fact that neither policeand firepro-
tection can be as efficient in the suburbs as in the
city,shalldeem fulland faircompensation for both
policeand fireprotectionhereby provided for said
97 - LOCAL LAWS , VOL . 3 .

1538 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Woodlands , equally with said city: Provided , how-
ever, That nothing in the laws regulatingsaid police
and firedepartmentshallbe construedto prohibitthe
authoritiesof said cityfrom responding to the callsof
humanity or misfortune for the said department out-
sideof the districthereby created.
§3. The saidact to which thisis an amendment ,
and the amendments thereto, are hereby re-enacted in
so far as they are not in conflictwith thisact ;allacts
and parts of actsin conflictwith this act are hereby
repealed .
§ 4. This actshallbe in forcefrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved May 27, 1890.
CHAPTER 1845 .
AN ACT for thebenefitof ChristianCollege,Hustonville,Kentucky .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the charterof ChristianCollege, Huston-
ville, Kentucky , be amended as follows: That said
collegeishereby investedwith the power and author-
ity to conferupon itsgraduates the followingdegrees,
to wit : Bachelor of Arts , Bachelor of Science , Mis-
tressof Science; and the commercial department of
said collegemay confer also upon itsgraduates the
degree of Master of Accounts .

sage .
Approved May 26,1890 .

or purchased by it in satisfactionof any debt, judg-

gage bonds issuedby the saidGreensburg and Glasgow

road Company may issueand sellthe bonds of said

semi-annually, with coupons attached,signed by the

date .

and principalof bonds issued by authority of this
act, saidcompany may execute a mortgage or deed of

chise to a trustee or trustees, and from time to time

deputationsas may be necessaryto effectthe purpose
and objectof itsexecution.

izedby the lastpreceding sectionof this act shall be
foreclosedby legalproceedings,such foreclosureshall
be for the benefit of all the holders of bonds secured

road, by reason of any saleor leasingto satisfythe

redemption of the mortgage bonds issued under au-
thorityof this act, a sinking fund shall be created
by said company ; and in order to create such sink-
ing fund said company shall,at the time of declaring
the firstcash dividend, ascertainwhat sum ought to

be set asideannuallyin order to createa fund suffi-
cientto redeem its bonds at maturity, and then set

pany ,which fund shallbe held sacred forthe redemp-

atingsuch fund : Provided . That said company may
use any or allof saidfund in retiringitsbonds before
maturity ifdeemed advisable; but no bond purchased
with the proceeds of the sinking fund shallbe resold,

§ 22. The holders of mortgage bonds issued under
authorityof this act may demand , and on such de-
mand be entitledto receive,evidencesof stock in said
Greensburg and Glasgow Railroad Company in lieuof
theirbonds at dollar for dollar, at any time within

Greensburg to Glasgow .
§ 23. The bonds of the company issued under au-
thorityof thisact,whether sold and deliveredin this
Stateor elsewhere, shallnot be avoided,in whole or in
part, by reason of the rate of interestagreed to be
paid or of the place at which they may be sold or
delivered,nor by reason of theirhaving been sold at
lessthan theirpar value.
§24. The GeneralAssembly reservesthe right, by
generallaws, to regulate the charges for way and
through freightand passengers on thisroad,and re-
servesthe rightto amend or repealthischarterwhen-
everitdeems itproper; and thischartershallbe null
and void if work is not begun in good faithwithin
two years and said railroadis not completed within
fiveyears.
§25. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved May 26, 1890.

and Anderson .

monwealth of Kentucky :

counties be so changed that hereafter said line shall
run as follows,to-wit : Beginning at the point on the
Hardinsvilleand Crab Orchard Turnpike Road , where

sage .

streamstributarytotheOhio and Licking rivers,inKenton county.

any person to catch, killor destroy fishin the various
running public streams tributary to the Ohio and
Lickingrivers,or either,in Kenton county,by means
ofdynamiting,seining,wing orhoop-netting,dip-net,

than twenty-five dollarsand not more than fiftydol-
larsfor each offense, to be recoverablebeforeany
magistratein Kenton county upon warrant,or upon
indictmentbeforethe grand jury,and upon convic-
tionin the criminalcourt in Kenton county .

thirtieth,one thousandeighthundred and ninety.

CHAPTER 1879 .
AN ACT for the benefit of the New Castle and Drennon Turnpike
Road , in Henry county .
WHEREAS , The turnpike road leading from New
Castle to Smith's Hill, in Henry county , is a very
difficultroad to keep up , and runs over an uneven ,
ly country, and is between four and fivemiles in
length ; therefore,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the managers of said road are hereby

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1641
given the right to charge full tollon said road, as
though itwas fivemiles long ; and allactsin conflict
with thisact are repealed .
§2 . This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved May 26, 1890 .

CHAPTER 1884 .
AN ACT to prohibitplayingbase ballon Sunday in Union county.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. It shallbe unlawful for any person to engage
in playing a match game of base ball in the county
of Union on Sunday .
§ 2. Any person who shall violatethe firstsection
of this act shall be punished by a fineof not less
than fivenor more than ten dollars,and each game
engaged shallbe deemed a seperateoffense.
§ 3. The justicesof the peace and policejudges in
said county,and the judge of the quarterlycourt of
said county ,shall have jurisdictionof all violations
of this act ; and it is hereby made the duty of all
sheriffs, marshals and constables of said county to
arrestany person violatingthe provisionsof the first
section of this act, and carry them before the court
having jurisdictionof the offense.
§ 4. This act to take effect and be in force from its
passage .
Approved May 26,1890.

CHAPTER 1916 .
AN ACT to charterthe Owingsville Normal and Business College.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1. That the school established at Owingsville,
Bath county, Kentucky , September third, one thou-
sand eight hundred and eighty-nine,by F. M. Hurst ,
and now under his management , shallbe known as
the OwingsvilleNormal and Business College.
§ 2. That said F. M. Hurst and his successor shall
be president of said college,and that the president
shall have power to appoint his successor : Provided ,
however, That the person appointed be a man of good
moral character, and a graduate of some collegethat
requiredthestudyof highermathematics,Latinand
Greek , forat leastfour years priorto graduation.
§ 3. The president of said college shall appoint

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1697
three trustees for same , who shall hold their office
for one year aftertheirappointment . He shall ap-
pointas many teachers as he may find necessaryfor
saidcollege.
§4.The president,togetherwith a majority of the
trustees, shallhave power to grantdiplomas,and con-
fersuch honorary degrees as isusualinother colleges.
§5 . The president, together with a majority of the
trustees of said college, shall make such rules and
regulationsas they may think necessaryfor the good
of the college,and it shallbe the duty of the presi-
dent to enforce said rules .
§ 6. Should the officeof president at any time be-
come vacant,eitherby neglect or accident, the trus-
teeswho lastheld officemay electa president to fill
said vacancy .
§ 7. The trustees of the common school of district
number one,inBath county,Kentucky, may contract
withthepresidentof saidcollegetohave thecommon
school of said districtnumber one taught in connec-
tionwith said college: Provided , Said contractshall
not extend beyond a period of threeyears,and that
no person shall be prohibited from attending said
schoolduring time said trusteesmay have contracted
tohave said common schooltaught in connectionwith
said college, for any other cause than for such
offensesas areprescribedby the common schoollaws
oftheStateofKentucky ; nor shallany childbe pro-
hibitedfora longertime than fivemonths forsame
offense .
§ 8. The trusteesof the college,in jointsessionwith
the trusteesof the common school in districtnumber
one , with the chairman of the board of common
school trusteesacting as chairman , shall decide all
casesof expulsion that may pertain to the students
belongingto said districtnumber one : Provided,
Said students at the time are attending such classes
as said trusteesand presidenthave agreed shall be
107 - LOCAL LAWS ,VOL . 3 .

1698 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
taught for benefitof the common school, otherwise
the presidentand trusteesof said collegeshall have
fullpower to decide.
§ 9. The trustees of said district number one , Bath
county, shall not contract with said president for
public school for lessthan fivemonths in each year ,
but may contractfor a term of ten months in each
year .
§ 10. Said trusteesmay contract with said presi-
dent to have such branches of learning taught in the
public school as they may think proper : Provided ,
No branch that the common school laws of this State
requiresto be taught be omitted.
§ 11. The presidentof said collegemay charge the
regular tuitionof the collegefor teaching any child
belonging to said districtnumber one : Provided ,
That the said child study other than the branches
which he may have contracted with the trustees to
have taught for benefitof children belonging to the
public school in districtnumber one.
§ 12. The faculty of said college, while teaching
aforesaid children of common school district number
one,shall be subject to the contract made with the
trustees of said district and this charter ; nor shall
they be considered as teachersof the common school
other than herein provided .
§ 13. Said presidentand trusteesof the Owingsville
Normal and Business College shall not grant a diplo-
ma in the normal course of saidcollegeto any person
until such person shall have completed in a satisfac-
torymanner,in additionto the branchesrequiredto
be taught by the common school laws of Kentucky ,
rhetoric, politicaleconomy , psychology, chemistry,
natural philosophy and algebra, and shall have fur-
nished said president a first-class county certificate
from some board of school examiners,together with
a certificateof good moral character from a county

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1699
i
court. Saidgraduatemay teachinany schoolinthis
State without further examination .
§ 14. The State Board of Education may , at any
time they think proper prior to graduation, require
candidatesfor said diplomas to appear before them ,
eitherat Owingsvilleor at the Capitalof the State, to
be examined upon above-named branchesnotrequired
tobe taughtby thecommon schoollawsof thisState.
If,intheiropinion,saidcandidatesforgraduationbe
not qualifiedin above -named branches, saidpresident
shallnot graduate them in said normal course.
§ 15. It shallbe the duty of the president of said
college, and the commissioner of common schools in
any county of the State,to revoke the diploma of any
suchgraduate,upon evidencethatsuch graduatehas
beenguiltyof drunkenness, profaneor abusivelan-
guage in or about any school-house,or any immoral
conductrenderinghim or her unfitfor the position
of a teacher of children. Said teachermay ,however ,
appeal from such decision to the State Board of Edu-
cation, whose decisionshallbe final.
§ 16.Neither the presidentof said college, nor
saidcommissionerof common schools,nor any mem-
ber of the State Board of Education, shall be liable
beforeany courtinthisStatefordamages for having
revoked said diploma .
§ 17.Any teacherholdingsucha diploma that has
been revoked,and attemptingthereafterto teacha
publicschool, using the same asa certificate,shall,
upon indictment before a grand jury and conviction
beforethe circuitcourt in which such offensemay
be committed, finednot lessthan two hundred dol-
lars nor more than three hundred dollars for each
offense .
§ 18. This act shall be in force and effect from and
afteritspassage.
!
Approved May 27,1890.

CHAPTER 1920 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Crow's Shop and LittleFlat LickTurn-
pike Road Company .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Peter Carter,John S. Murphy ,Thomas J. Hill,
Richard Cobb , John M. Hill, Ed . Carter and Sam-
uelMcKitrick, of the county of Lincoln, and their

1702 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
associatesand successors, be, and they are hereby ,
createda body -politicand corporate, with perpetual
succession, with power to sue and be sued, plead and
be impleaded with .
§ 2. The business of said corporation shall be to
constructand operatea turnpike road in the county

Stanford and Knob Lick Turnpike , to a point on the
Stanford and MillegevilleTurnpike ,at or near the res-
idence of Thomas J. Hill, and from said Hill's to a
point at or near the bridge over Lick Branch ,on the
Stanford and HustonvilleTurnpike , and from thence
to the Little Flat Lick .
§3 . The capitalstock of said company shall be in
shares of fiftydollars each , and shall not exceed ten
thousand dollars .
§ 4. When the sum of three thousand dollars, in-
cluding any subscriptionthe county court of Lincoln
may make thereto,shallhave been subscribedto the
capitalstock of said road company in good and bona
fide subscriptions,itshallbe the duty of the incorpora-
torsnamed hereinto give notice,in such manner and
at such times and places as they may think proper,
of a meeting of the stockholders, for the purpose of
electinga board of directors, consistingof fiveof the
stockholders , in which election one vote shall be al-
lowed for each share of stock subscribed . The five
persons receivingat said electionthe highest number
of votescastshallcontinuein officeforone year from
the date of theirelection,or untiltheirsuccessorsare
duly electedand qualified.
§5 . The board of directorselected by the stock-
holders shall, immediately after theirelection, elect
one of theirnumber president, and shallappoint such
otherofficersthey may deem necessaryfortransaction
of the business of the company ; and the president
and directorsshallfix the time and place of election
aftertheirfirstelection,to be held once in each year,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1703
at such time and place as they may direct; and a ma-
jorityof the board shallbe competent to do business.
§ 6. The Lincoln County Court, a majority of the
justicesin common being present, may , when ap-
pliedto by the officersof said road company , in be-
halfof saidcounty,subscribestock in said company ,
not exceeding one thousand dollarsper mile,to the
capitalstock of saidturnpike road company forevery
mile constructed, to be paid in such way as the court,
initsdiscretion, may direct; to be controlledby said
county court as other turnpike stock of said county .
§7. That the general laws of this State construct-
ing turnpike roads, and the acquisitionsfor land or
material for the same , and the rates of tollsand the
rightto collectthe same , and not inconsistentwith
theprovisionsof thisact, shallbe partsof thisact.

Road Company .
§ 9. That if, in the construction of said road, it
shallbe necessaryto locatethe same on any portion
ofany county road,thecompany shallhave theright
to do so .
§ 10.That the presidentand directors,in the con-

near the bridge over Lick Branch,on the Stanford
and HustonvilleTurnpike,and proceedto a pointat
or near the residenceof Thomas J. Hill, on the Stan-

pany may erecta toll-gate on said turnpike road, and

andallneighbors'funerals,and personsgoingtoand
from millon horseback,shallbe freefrom any charge
of toll.

topass allby -laws necessaryfor the management of

§ 1.3. The presidentand directorsshall have power

and styleof the "SaintStephensCemetery," with

1706 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .

poration,and , in general, to do allother acts neces-

of theirincorporation,which is to procure the prop-
er management , control and conduct of the Saint
Stephens Cemetery , near Owensboro , in accordance
with the rulesand regulationsof the Roman Catholic
Church respectingcemeteriesand burialof the dead .
§2 . The affairsof said corporation shall be man-
aged by a board of directors, consistingof the Roman

and four others, who shall all be members of the
Roman Catholic Church . The persons hereinbefore
named as incorporatorsshallconstitutethe firstboard
of directors,and they shallcontinue in officeuntila
vacancy occurs,in which event the remaining direct-
ors shall fillthe vacancy by the appointment of a

§ 3. Said board of directors shall have complete
control, management and direction of the Saint
Stephens Cemetery ground aforesaid belonging to
saidcorporation;may collectand expend ,in the inter-
est of said corporation, all its moneys ; shall have
power to inclose, improve , embellish and adorn the
grounds of saidcemetery ; to lay out said cemetery in
suitableavenues, walks and burial lots, and to make
and record on the books of the corporation , and on
the records of Daviess county, a plat of the plan of
said avenues and walks , designating the same by
names or numbers ; shallhave power to selland con-
vey in the corporate name ,and under the corporate
seal,said buriallots, in such manner and upon such
terms as they may deem best ; and they shall have
the power generally to do and transact allbusiness
necessaryto promote and carry out the aforesaid

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1707
purposes of this corporation, and incident to their
officeas directors thereof.
§ 4. Each purchaserofa buriallotinsaidcemetery
shall, upon the presentationby him to the board of
directorsof a receiptfrom theactingtreasurerofthe
corporation, showing thathe has paid in fullforsuch
lot,be entitledtoa conveyanceofsuchlot,which con-
veyance shall be made by a certificate, signed by the
presidentof thecorporation,sealedby the corporate
seal,and countersignedby the actingsecretary, stat-
ing that such purchaseristhe owner of such lot,
which certificateshall be recorded on the record of
titlesofDaviesscounty,and shallconvey tosaidpur-
chaserthefee-simplerightand titleinand tosaidlot
freeand exempt from alltaxation,assessment,execu-
tions,attachments,or otherlegalprocess,but subject
to the rulesand by -laws and regulationsof the cor-
porationand board of directors;which said certificate
shallhave thesame forceand effectforallpurposesas
deedsduly executed, deliveredand recorded:Pro-
vided, That saidlotssoconveyedshallbe used forno
otherpurpose than as buriallots; and ifused forany
otherpurposeby the owners thereof,they shallrevert
to the corporation .
§5. The said Saint Stephens Cemetery grounds,
and such otheradjacentlandsas may be hereafter
acquiredby saidcorporationforthe purposesof said
cemetery, are hereby foreverdedicatedto the said
corporationso long as it may use the same exclu-
sivelyasa cemetery,and thesame shallbe freefrom
allassessmentor taxation,or executions,attachments
or otherlegalprocess,and from any lien,charge or
appropriationfor publicuseor purposesinconsistent
with the use thereof as a cemetery.
§ 6.Any person who shallwillfullyand without
authorityof law violateany grave in saidcemetery,
ormutilate,defaceor injureany inclosure,monument
or other structureplaced thereinforprotection, orna-
ment or otherpurpose; or shallcut,injureor destroy

1708 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
any tree, shrub or plant within the limitsof said cem-
etery, or shalldrive over lotsin said cemetery laid
out for burial purposes,shallbe deemed guilty of a
misdemeanor ,and shall,upon a convictionin any court
of justicehaving jurisdictionthereof,be punished by
a fine of not less than five dollars nor more than five
hundred dollars; and such offendershallalsobe lia-
ble on a civilactionto saidcorporationfor alldamage
occasioned by his said act or acts. Power ishereby
conferredupon each of the board of directorsof this
corporation,and upon the superintendent and sexton
appointed by them to take charge of said cemetery ,
to arrestwithout warrant, and to take before the
proper officerand tribunal, to be dealt with accord-
ing to law , any and all persons found offending
against any of the provisionsof this section.
§ 7. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .

pauper idiot; and whereas, by inadvertenceon the
partof B. F. Landrum, ofMcLean county, commit-
teeforsaidpauperidiot,shewas notbroughtbefore
thesaidcourtin thefifthyear thereafterfortrialon
108- LOCAL LAWS , VOL .3.

1714 RESOLUTIONS .
the saidcharge as by law in such caseprovided ; but
was at the January term,one thousand eight hundred
and eighty-nine, of said court, brought before said
court,and again triedby saidcourt,and by said court
adjudged to be a pauper idiot; and whereas, for the
time in excess of the firstfiveyears of the time be-
tween said trials, to wit : Between the sixth day of
July, one thousand eight hundred and eighty-seven,
and the sixth day of January , one thousand eight
hundred and eighty-nine, the State'sallowance for a
pauper idiotwas refused said committee by the Au-
ditor because of said inadvertence ; and whereas , said
committee did procure the keeping of said pauper
idiotforsaid last-named period, and met the expense
therefor, and the Commonwealth has paid him noth-
ing forso doing ; therefore, be it
Resolved by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
1. That the Auditor be,and he is hereby ,directed
to draw his warrant upon the treasury for the sum
of one hundred and twelve dollarsand fiftycents in
favor of said B. F. Landrum .
2. This resolution to take effect from itsadoption .
Approved May 27,1890.
